28Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew. 29 To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_text:
Nyankopɔn atemmuo a ɔtene
1 Ne saa nti, adasamma nni anoyie biaa, sɛnea woteeɛ biaa, woobu bi atɛne he, na woobu woaa wo ho atɛne, efiri sɛ wo a woyɛ temmufoɔ he, woyɔ ade korɔ heaa. 2Hoka sɛ, “Yenim sɛ Nyankopɔn atemmuo a ɔba bɛ a bɛyɔ saa he so he tene.” 3Nti, wo nipa, wosusu sɛ, sɛnea woteeɛ biaa, sɛ wobu bɛ a bɛyɔ saa he atɛne na wo nso woyɔ saa bi a, wobɛdwane Nyankopɔn atemmuo anaa? 4 Anaa wobu N’ayamyɛ pii a ɔno ne boasetɔ ne n’abodwokyɛre he animtia? Wonnim sɛ Nyankopɔn ayamyɛ botae ne sɛ ɔde wo bɛkɔ adwensakram? 5Nso w’akoma pirim ɔne sesa a wompɛ sɛ wosesa nti, wooboaboa Nyankopɔn abufuo ano ama wo ho ama abufuo da he, berɛ a Nyankopɔn atemmuo a ɔtene he beyi ne ho adi; 6 efiri sɛ obetua biaa ka sɛnea biaa nnwuma teɛ: 7bɛ a bɛde ntoboaseɛ yɔ papa hwehwɛ animuonyam ne anidie ne nea ɔmporɔ he, ɔbɛma bɛ daa nkwa; 8 nso bɛ a bedi ntuturasɛm; na bɛpo nɔkorɛ na bɛfa kwane a ɔntene so he, bebenya abufuo ne asotwe. 9 Na ahohiahia ne ahokyerɛ bɛba biaa a ɔyɔ bɔne so, Yudani kane, na afei Helani nso, 10nso animuonyam ne anidie ne asomdwie bɛba biaa a ɔyɔ papa so, Yudani kane, na afei Helani nso, 11 efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn nyɛ nyiyimu.
12Dodoɔ a benni mmara na bɛyɔɔ bɔne he, bɛbɛkwati mmara he nso ayera, na bɛ a bɛya mmara ase na bɛyɔɔ bɔne nyinaa bɛde mmara he bebu bɛ atɛne. 13 Na ɔnyɛ bɛ a betie mmara he ne bɛyɛ ateneneefoɔ Nyankopɔn ani so, na mmom bɛ a bɛyɛ mmara he yɔfoɔ nkoaa ne benya bembuo. 14 Sɛ Amanamane mufoɔ a benni mmara he, de bɛ awosuo yɔ deɛ mmara he hyɛ sɛ bɛnyɔ a, hedeɛ, ewom sɛ benni mmara he deɛ, nso nea bɛyɔ he yɛ mmara ma bɛ ankasa. 15 Bɛda no adi sɛ deɛ mmara he hyɛ sɛ bɛnyɔ he bɛatwerɛ no bɛ akomam, sɛnea bɛ ahonim nso di ho adanseɛ; na bɛ nsusuiɛ a ayɛ ntanta bɛtoto bɛ ano anaa obetwitwa agye bɛ 16da a Nyankopɔn a ɔnam Yesu Kristo so, bebu nnipa nyinaa kokoam nsusuiɛ atɛne sɛnea asɛmpa a maka he teɛ.
Yudafoɔ ne mmara he
17Nso sɛ wofrɛ wo ho Yudani, na wode wo ho to mmara he so, na wohoahoa wo ho Nyankopɔn mu, 18na wonim n’apɛdeɛ na wokyerɛ nea ɔyɛ, efiri sɛ bɛatete wo mmara he mu, 19 na wogye di sɛ wokyerɛ anifirafoɔ kwane, woyɛ hann ma bɛ a bɛnam sum mu, 20wotene nkwaseafoɔ, woyɛ mmɔfra bɛ kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, wowɔ mmara he a nimdeɛ ne nɔkorɛ ahyɛ no ma; 21 wo a afei deɛ, wokyerɛkyerɛ afoforɔ, wonkyerɛkyerɛ wo ho? Wo a wokasa tia korɔnobɔ he, wommɔ korɔno? 22Wo a wokasa tia adwammammɔ he, wommɔ adwamane? 23Wo a wohoahoa wo ho mmara he mu, wommu mmara he so mfa mmu Nyankopɔn animtia? 24 Saa nti ne bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Wo nti bɛaka Nyankopɔn dini ho abususɛm Amanamane mu.”
25 Na mmom sɛ wodi mmara he so a twetiatwa ho hia; nso sɛ wobu mmara he so a wo twetiatwa he dane bɛyɛ te sɛnea wontwaa twetia. 26Nti sɛ bɛ a bentwaa twetia he di mmara he so a, twetia a bentwaeɛ he nti bemmu bɛ sɛ bɛ a bɛatwa bɛ twetia anaa? 27Nti bɛ a honam fam bentwaa bɛ twetia na nso bedi mmara he so he bebu ho a bɛatwerɛ mmara he amma ho, na bɛatwa ho twetia nso hoto mmara he fɔ. 28 Ɔnyɛ honam fam nnoɔma ne ma bi yɛ Yudani, saa ara nso ne twetiatwa kann nso nna adi anisoɔ ne honam fam nnoɔma mu. 29 na mmom, sɛ bi yɛ Yudani mapa a, ɔyɛ no ne mu, na twetiatwa ankasa yɛ akoma mu asɛm; ɔyɛ honhom mu adeɛ, na mmom ɔnyɛ honam mu adeɛ. Nea ɔyɛ Yudani mapa he, n’ayeyie firi Nyankopɔn hɔ, na mmom ommfiri nnipa hɔ.

english_text:
1What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised? 2It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews. 3It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith? 4 No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God,
“Your words
will be proven true,
and in court
you will win your case.”
5If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask. 6But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world? 7Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner. 8You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well.
No One Is Good
9What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said. 10 The Scriptures tell us,
“No one is acceptable to God!
11Not one of them understands
or even searches for God.
12They have all turned away
and are worthless.
There isn't one person
who does right.
13 Their words are like
an open pit,
and their tongues are good
only for telling lies.
Each word is as deadly
as the fangs of a snake,
14 and they say nothing
but bitter curses.
15 These people quickly
become violent.
16Wherever they go,
they leave ruin
and destruction.
17They don't know how
to live in peace.
18 They don't even fear God.”
19We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty. 20 God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin.
God's Way of Accepting People
21Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses. 22 God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ. 23All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory. 24But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins. 25-26 God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
27What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith. 28We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law. 29Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is! 30 There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith. 31Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_text:
1Afei, mmɛ ne Yudani wɔ boro so? Anaa mmɛ mfasoɔ ne ya twetiatwa so? 2 Ɔdɔɔso kwane hodoɔ bebree so, efiri sɛ nea odi kane he, Yudafoɔ he ne bɛde Nyankopɔn nsɛm hyɛɛ bɛ nsa. 3 Na sɛ bɛ mu binom annye anni ɛ? Nti bɛgye a bɛannye anni he bɛsesa Nyankopɔn nɔkorɛdie he anaa? 4 Daabi da! Ewom sɛ nnipa biaa nni nɔkorɛ, ma Nyankopɔn nyɛ nɔkwafoɔ, sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ:
“Sɛnea wobedi bem wo nsɛm mu.
Na woadi nkunim w’atemmuo mu.”
5Nti sɛ tene a yɛntene da Nyankopɔn tenenee adi a, mmɛ ne yɛbɛka? Nti Nyankopɔn ntene sɛ obehwie n’abufuo agu yɛ so? (Meka no nipa fam.) 6Daabi da! Saa deɛ a mmɛ kwane ne Nyankopɔn bɛfa so abu wiase atɛne? 7Nso sɛ tene a mentene ma Nyankopɔn nɔkorɛdie bu so ma n’animuonyam da adi a, mmɛ nti ne bɛda so bu me sɛ debɔneyɛni? 8 Na ɛɛne nti ne yɛnka sɛ (sɛnea binom ka to yɛ so sɛ, yɛ dɛ), “Homma yɛnyɔ nnebɔne na nnepa aba?” Bɛ afobuo fata bɛ!
Nipa biaa ntene Nyankopɔn anim
9 Hedeɛ asɛm he ne sɛ? Nti yɛ Yudafoɔ he kyɛne bɛ a bɛaka he anaa? Daabi, ɔnte saa koraa; efiri sɛ yɛadi kane aka sɛ, Yudafoɔ oo, Helafoɔ oo, biaa hyɛ bɔne tumi ase, 10 sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ:
“Biaa nni hɔ a ɔtene, baako mpo nni hɔ;
11Biaa nni nteaseɛ, biaa nni hɔ a ɔhwehwɛ Nyankopɔn.
12Biaa amane afiri kwanpa he so, bɛ nyinaa mfasoɔ nni bɛ so;
biaa nni hɔ a ɔda ayamyɛ adi,
biaa nni hɔ, baako mpo nni hɔ.”
13 “Bɛ mene mu yɛ awufoɔ adakamena a ano abie;
bɛde bɛ kɛtrɛma di torɔ.”
“Nankatia borɔ ya bɛ ano.”
14 “Duabɔ ne adenwono ahyɛ bɛ anum ma.”
15 “Bɛ nane ɔhare mogyahwieguo ho;
16sɛeɛ ne awerɛhoɔ ya bɛ kwane so,
17na asomdwie kwane deɛ, bennim.”
18 “Nyamesuro nni bɛ ani so.”
19 Afei yenim sɛ, nea mmara he ka biaa, ɔkasa kyerɛ bɛ a bɛya mmara he ase, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ano biaa bɛka atom, na wiase nyinaa abu nkonta akyerɛ Nyankopɔn, 20 efiri sɛ nipa biaa nni hɔ a obedi bem Nyankopɔn anim, sɛnea mmara he nnwuma teɛ, efiri sɛ yɛnam mmara he so ne yenya bɔne ho nimdeɛ.
Tenenee a ɔnam gyidie so ba
21 Na afei, bɛakwati mmara he ayi Nyankopɔn tenenee adi, na mmara he ne adiyifoɔ he di ho adanseɛ, 22Nyankopɔn tenenee a ɔnam Yesu Kristo mu gyidie so ma bɛ a bɛgye di nyinaa. Na nyiyimu nnim, 23 efiri sɛ nnipa nyinaa ayɔ bɔne na yɛtɔ sini Nyankopɔn animuonyam ho; 24 na afei ɔyɛ akyɛdeɛ kwa sɛ Nyankopɔn nam n’adom ne gyeɛ a ɔya Kristo Yesu mu he so abu nnipa bem. 25 Kristo ne Nyankopɔn de no asi hɔ sɛ ne mogyaa he nyɛ mpatadeɛ a nipa nam gyidie so ne nya mu kyɛfa; na Nyankopɔn de ayi ne tenenee adi sɛ nnebɔne a bɛyɔɔ no kane he, ɔnam ne boasetɔ so abu n’ani agu so; 26sɛnea ɔbɛyɔ a ɔbɛda n’ankasa ne tenenee adi saa berɛ yi mu, na berɛ a obu nipa a ɔwɔ Yesu mu gyidie bem he, ada adi sɛ ɔyɛ teneneeni mapa.
27 Afei mmɛ ne ahohoahoa yi? Wɔayi afiri hɔ. Mmɛ nhyehyɛeɛ so na ɔnam? Mmara nnwuma so? Daabi, na mmom ɔnam gyidie nhyehyɛeɛ so. 28 Na yesi no pi sɛ ɔnam gyidie so ne bebu bi bem, na mmom omfiri mmara nnwuma mu. 29 Anaa Nyankopɔn yɛ Yudafoɔ nkoaa bɛ Nyankopɔn? Ɔnnyɛ Amanamane mufoɔ nso Nyankopɔn anaa? Ampa, ɔyɛ Amanamane mufoɔ nso Nyankopɔn, 30 efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn yɛ baako; na ɔnam gyidie korɔ he a so ne bebu twetiatwafoɔ ne bɛ a bentwaa bɛ twetia bem. 31 Ɔsɛ sɛ yɛde saa gyidie he tu mmara he gu anaa? Daabi da! Na mmom, yɛma mmara he ase tim.

english_text:
The Example of Abraham
1Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham? 2If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God. 3 The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
4Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working. 5But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him. 6In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
7-8 “What a blessing
when God forgives our sins
and our evil deeds.
What a blessing
when the Lord erases our sins
from his book.”
9Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him. 10But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
11 Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised. 12This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised.
The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
13 God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable. 14 If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
15God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken. 16 Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all. 17 The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
18 God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations. 19 Abraham's faith never became weak, not even when he was nearly 100 years old. He knew he was almost dead and that his wife Sarah could not have children. 20But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
21Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised. 22So God accepted him, 23just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham. 24They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life. 25 God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_text:
Abraham nhwɛsoɔ
1Ɔte saa a afei mmɛ ne yɛbɛka sɛ ɔyɛ mfasoɔ a Abraham a ɔyɛ yɛ honam fam agya-panyini he nyaeɛ? 2Sɛ nnwuma mu ne bebuu Abraham bem a, nka ɔwɔ nea ɔde bɛhoahoa ne ho, nso ɔnyɛ Nyankopɔn anim. 3 Na mmɛ twerɛsɛm he ka? “Abraham gyee Nyankopɔn diiɛ, na Nyankopɔn buu ne gyidie he sɛ tenenee maa no.” 4 Na afei sɛ bi yɔ adwuma a, bemmu n’akatua sɛ akyɛdeɛ, na mmom bebu no sɛ adeɛ ɔfata no. 5Nso bi a wannyɔ adwuma he gye nea obu debɔnyɛni bem he di a, obu saa gyidie he sɛ tenenee ma no. 6Nti Dawid nso kasa faa nhyira a ɔba bɛ a Nyankopɔn kwati bɛ nnwuma bu bɛ bem he ho sɛ:
7 “Nhyira ne bɛ a bɛde bɛ mmarato akyɛ bɛ,
na bɛakata bɛ bɔne so;
8nhyira ne nipa a Awurade mmu ne bɔne ngu ne so.”
9Nti saa nhyira yi, kɔma bɛ a bɛatwa bɛ twetia nkoaa, anaa bɛ a bentwaa bɛ twetia nso ka ho? Yɛpae mu ka sɛ, Gyidie ne bɛde buu Abraham sɛ ɔtene. 10Mmɛ kwane so ne bɛde buu no saa? Ɔyɛ berɛ a na bentwaa no twetia he anaa betwaa no twetia wieeɛ he a? Ɔnnyɛ twetiatwa he akyi, na mmom yei siiɛ ansa na betwaa no twetia. 11 Onyaa twetiatwa he ho nsɛnkyerɛnne sɛ tenenee a ɔnam gyidie so nyaeɛ he ho nsɔano berɛ a na bentwaa no twetia. Nyankopɔn yɔɔ yei nam so ama Abraham abɛyɛ bɛ a bentwaa bɛ twetia, na nso bɛgye Nyankopɔn di he nyinaa agya, na ne saa nti bɛabu bɛ nso sɛ ateneneefoɔ, 12 saa ara nso ne ɔyɛ agya ma twetiafoɔ a, ɔnyɛ bɛ twetiatwa nkoaa, na mmom bɛnam yɛ agya Abraham a onyaa gyidie ansa na betwaa no twetia he anammɔno mu.
Nyankopɔn nhwɛsoɔ a ɔnam gyidie so
13 Na bɔ a bɛhyɛɛ no sɛ obedi adeɛ wiase he annyɛ bɔhyɛ a ɔnam mmara so maa Abraham anaa n’asefoɔ na mmom ɔnam gyidie tenee so. 14Sɛ mmara sodifoɔ he ne ɔsɛ sɛ bɛyɛ adedifoɔ a, hedeɛ na gyidie yɛ kwa na bɔhyɛ he nso nni mu; 15 efiri sɛ mmara he twetwe Nyankopɔn abufuo ba; na sɛ mmara nni hɔ a, mmarato biaa nso nni hɔ.
16Ne saa nti ogyina gyidie so, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɔhyɛ he begyina adom so na adi agyinamu ama n’asefoɔ nyinaa, ɔnnyɛ mmara sodifoɔ he nkoaa, na mmom bɛ a bekura Abraham gyidie he bi, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ yɛ nyinaa yɛ agya, 17 sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ “Maayɔ wo amane bebree bɛ agya,” Nyankopɔn a ɔgye no di he anim, nea ɔma awufoɔ nkwa, na ɔfrɛ adeɛ a onni hɔ ma no ba. 18 Berɛ a na anidasoɔ nni hɔ he, Abraham gyinaa anidasoɔ so gye dii sɛ ɔbɛyɛ amane bebree agya sɛnea bɛaka sɛ he, “Saa ara ne w’asefoɔ bɛyɛ bebree.” 19 Ne gyidie anhinhim, mpo berɛ a ohuu sɛ ne nipadua ayɛ sɛnea awu (efiri sɛ na waadi mfeɛ ha), anaa berɛ a ohuu sɛ Sara yɛ bonyini he. 20 Na Nyankopɔn bɔhyɛ he ho deɛ wamma annyeannie biaa amma n’akwane mu, amma no annye akyinnye, na mmom ogyinaa pintinn gyidie mu na ɔhyɛɛ Nyankopɔn animuonyam, 21onyaa awerɛhyɛmu paa sɛ Nyankopɔn betumi ayɔ nea w’ahyɛ ho bɔ he. 22 Gyidie a ɔnyaa yɛ nti “bebuu ne sɛ ɔtene.” 23 Afei nsɛm a ɔka sɛ, “Nyankopɔn buu ne gyidie sɛ tenenee maa no” he, bɛantwerɛ amma ɔno nkoaa, 24na mmom bɛtwerɛ maa yɛ nso; na bɛabu yɛ a yɛgye nea ɔnyanee Yesu a ɔyɛ yɛ Awurade firii awufoɔ mu he di nso sɛ yɛ tene. 25 Yesus ne yɛ bɔne nti beyii no maa wuo na yɛ bembuo nti bɛyanee no he.

english_text:
What It Means To Be Acceptable to God
1By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God. 2Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God. 3But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure. 4And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope 5that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
6Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful. 7No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person. 8But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
9But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger. 10Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son. 11And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us.
Adam and Christ
12 Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die. 13Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law. 14Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did.
In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later. 15But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
16There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times. 17Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
18 Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life. 19Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
20The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful. 21Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_text:
Bembuo so aba
1 Afei ɔnam sɛ bɛabu yɛ bem gyidie mu nti, yɛnam yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so anya asomdwie Nyankopɔn mu, 2 ɔno so ne yɛnam anya saa adom a yegyina so yi; na yɛhoahoa yɛ ho anidasoɔ a yɛ wɔ no Nyankopɔn animuonyam mu. 3 Na ɔnyɛ yei nkoaa, na mmom yɛhoahoa yɛ ho ya yɛ amanehunu mu, yenim sɛ amanehunu de yɔkoduru ba, 4na yɔkoduru de subane pa ba, na subane pa de anidasoɔ ba, 5 na anidasoɔ nni hwammɔ, efiri sɛ bɛnam Honhom Kronkron a bɛde ama yɛ he so ahwie Nyankopɔn dɔ agu yɛ akoma mu.
6Na berɛ a yɛda so yɛ mmerɛ he, berɛ a ɔsɛ mu he Kristo wu maa nnebɔnyɛfoɔ. 7Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, ɔbɛyɛ dene sɛ bi bewu ama deɛ ɔyɛ nipa tenenee mpo; na deɛ ɔyɛ nipa pa he deɛ, ebia nka bi besi ne bo sɛ obewu ama no. 8 Nso Nyankopɔn daa ne dɔ adi kyerɛɛ yɛ sɛ yɛda so yɛ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ he, Kristo wu maa yɛ. 9 Nea ɔka ho koraa ne sɛ, seesei bɛde ne mogyaa abu yɛ bem, bɛnam ne so bɛgye yɛ afiri Nyankopɔn abufuo mu. 10 Na sɛ berɛ a yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn atamfoɔ he, bɛnam ne Ba he wuo so apata yɛ ne Nyankopɔn ntam a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne bɛmfa ne nkwa so nnye yɛ. 11Nso ɔnyɛ yei nkoaa, na mmom yɛnam yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so hoahoa yɛ ho Nyankopɔn mu, seesei deɛ, yɛnam ne so anya mpata yi.
Adam ne Kristo
12 Saa nti ɔnam nipa baako so ne bɔne baa wiase, na wuo nam bɔne so baeɛ, maa wuo trɛ kaa nnipa nyinaa, efiri sɛ nnipa nyinaa ayɔ bɔne; 13 ansa na mmara he ɔɔba he na bɔne ya wiase, nso sɛ mmara nni hɔ a bemmu biaa fɔ bɔne ho. 14 Nso wuo faa nnipa nnommum firi Adam so kosii Mose so, mpo bɛ a bɛ bɔne nte sɛ Adam mmarato he, saa Adam yi yɛ nea na ɔɔba he ho nsɛso.
15Na ɔnyɛ sɛdeɛ mmarato he teɛ ne adom akyɛdeɛ he nso teɛ, efiri sɛ baakofoɔ mmarato nti nnipa bebree awu a, Nyankopɔn Adom ne nipa baako a ɔno ne Yesu Kristo he mu adom akyɛdeɛ aboro so ama nnipa bebree akyɛne saa. 16Na akyɛdeɛ a bɛde kyɛ kwa he nte sɛ nipa baako bɔne ho nsɛso he. Na atemmuo a ɔnam mmarato baako so he de afobuo baeɛ, nso akyɛdeɛ a bɛde kyɛ kwa he, a odi mmarato bebree akyi he de bembuo ba. 17Sɛ ɔnam baakofoɔ mmarato so maa wuo faa nnipa nnommum saa kwane yi so a, mprɛ meɛhe ne bɛ a benyaa adom ne tenenee akyɛdeɛ a ɔboro so he mmom ɔmmfa nipa baako, Yesu Kristo, so nya daa nkwa.
18 Nti sɛnea nipa baako mmarato de bebree kɔɔ afobuo mu he, saa ne ɔnam baakofoɔ tenenee so de bembuo ne nkwa bɛma nnipa nyinaa. 19 Sɛnea ɔnam baakofoɔ asoɔdene so maa nnipa bebree yɛɛ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ he, saa ara ne nipa baako setie bɛma nnipa bebree ayɛ ahoteefoɔ. 20 Nso mmara he baa sɛ mmarato bɛdɔɔso; nso sɛ bɔne dɔɔso a, adom broo so kyɛne saa. 21 Na sɛnea bɔne dii hene wuo mu he, adom so nam tenenee so adi hene afa yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so kɔ daa nkwa mu.

english_text:
Dead to Sin but Alive because of Christ
1What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better? 2No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning? 3Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death? 4 When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
5If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him. 6We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin. 7We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
8As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him. 9We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die. 10When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God. 11In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
12Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires 13or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God. 14Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law.
Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
15What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not! 16Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him. 17You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received. 18Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
19I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
20When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God. 21But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death. 22Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life. 23Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_text:
Yesu mu ayɔnkofa ku bɔne
1 Afei ɛɛne ne yɛnka? Yɛnkɔ so nyɔ bɔne sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a adom bɛdɔɔso anaa? 2Daabi da, ɔnsɛ sɛ yɛyɔ saa koraa! Mmɛ nti ne ɔsɛ sɛ yɛ a yɛawu ama bɔne kɔ so tena bɔne mu? 3 Anaa honnim sɛ yɛ mu dodoɔ a bɛbɔɔ yɛ asu Kristo Yesu mu he, bɛbɔɔ yɛ asu kɔɔ ne wuom? 4 Nti besiee yɛ ne no ya asubɔ mu kɔɔ wuom, sɛ, sɛnea bɛde Agya he animuonyam nyanee Kristo firii awufoɔ mu he, saa ne ɔsɛ sɛ yɛ nso yɛnante nkwa foforɔ mu.
5 Nti sɛ yɛ ne no ayɛ baako ne wuo a ɔte sei mu a, yɛ ne no nso bɛyɛ baako ya wusɔreɛ a ɔte sɛ ne deɛ he mu. 6 Yenim sɛ bɛabɔ yɛ nipadua dada ne no asennua mu sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɔne nipadua he bɛsɛe, na yɛankɔ so anyɔ bɔne nkoa. 7Na nea wawu he ade ne ho afiri bɔne ho. 8 Nso sɛ yɛ ne Kristo awu a, yɛgye di sɛ yɛ ne no bɛtena ase nso. 9Yenim sɛ Kristo a bɛanyane no afiri awufuom he, onwu bio; efiri sɛ wuo ntumi mfa no nommum bio. 10 Wuo a owuiɛ he, owuu prɛko maa bɔne, maa nnipa nyinaa; nso ase a ɔte he, ɔte ma Nyankopɔn. 11Nti ho nso ho mu ho ho sɛ hoawu ama bɔne ampa na hote ase ma Nyankopɔn, Kristo mu.
12 Nti, hommma bɔne mmfa ho nipadua a obewu yi nommum, sɛ hobɛyɛ setie ama honam akɔnnɔ. 13 Hommfa ho nipadua mma bɔne sɛ akodeɛ amumuyɔ kwane so, na mmom homfa ho ho ma Nyankopɔn sɛ bɛ a bɛagye bɛ afiri wuom aba nkwa mu, na homfa ho ho ma sɛ tenenee akodeɛ. 14 Na bɔne amfa ho nommum bio, efiri sɛ honni mmara ase, na mmom hoya adom ase.
Bɔne nkoa
15 Afei mmɛ bio? Yɛnyɔ bɔne efiri sɛ yenni mmara ase na mmom adom ase anaa? Daabi da! 16 Honnim sɛ nea hode ho ho ma no na hotie no sɛ nkoa he, hoyɛ ne nkoa, sɛ bɔne a ɔkɔ wuom, anaa setie a ɔkɔ tenenee mu? 17Nso yɛda Nyankopɔn ase sɛ, hoayɛ bɔne nkoa da, na hofiri akomam abɛyɛ setie ama nkyerɛkyerɛ a bɛde hyɛɛ ho nsa, 18 na afei, berɛ a bɛagye ho afiri bɔne mu he, hoabɛyɛ tenenee nkoa. 19Meeka no nipafam esiane ho honam mmerɛeɛ nti. Sɛnea hode ho honam akwaa maa fi nkoa na hoyɔɔ amumuyɔ toaa amumuyɔ so he, nti saa mmerɛ yi deɛ homfa ho ho ma sɛ tenenee nkoa nkɔ ahoteɛ mu.
20Berɛ a hoyɛ bɔne nkoa he, na hode ho ho tenenee ho. 21 Nti mmɛ mfasoɔ ne honyaa no nnoɔma a afei deɛ ho fɛre ho he mu? Saa nnoɔma he awieeɛ ne wuo. 22 Nso afei a hoade ho ho afiri bɔne ho yi, ho abɛyɛ Nyankopɔn nkoa, mfasoɔ a honya ne ahoteɛ. N’awieeɛ ne daa nkwa. 23 Na bɔne akatua a ne wuo, nso Nyankopɔn adom akyɛdeɛ ne daa nkwa a ɔya Awurade Kristo Yesu mu.

english_text:
An Example from Marriage
1My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive. 2For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free 3to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
4That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God. 5When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die. 6But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law.
The Battle with Sin
7 Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this. 8It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
9Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life, 10and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death. 11 Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died. 12Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
13Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is. 14We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin. 15 In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate. 16Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good. 17So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
18I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot. 19Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong. 20And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
21The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right. 22With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God. 23But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do. 24What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die? 25Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me.
So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
translation_text:
Awareɛ ho mfantoho
1Anuanom, honnim sɛ, meekasa akyerɛ bɛ a benim mmara he, sɛ mmara he kyekyere bi berɛ a onii he te ase? 2Nti baa warefoɔ hyɛ ne kunu mmara ase berɛ a ne kunu he te ase; nso sɛ okunu he wu a, ɔde ne ho firi ne kunu mmara he ase. 3Saa ne ɔteɛ, sɛ baa warefoɔ ne beema foforɔ te a, na ne kunu he nwuiɛ a, bɛbɛfrɛ no dwammanmɔfoɔ. Nso sɛ ne kunu he wu a, baa he de ne ho firi saa mmara he ho; sɛ ɔware beema foforɔ a ɔnyɛ dwammanmɔfoɔ.
4 Annuanom, saa kwane he so ne honam Kristo nipadua so awu mmara he fam, saa nti, hohyɛ nipa foforɔ ase, nea benyanee no firi awufoɔ mu na ɔnam so ama yɛ aso aba ama Nyankopɔn. 5 Berɛ a na yɛte honam mu he, mmara he yɛnee yɛ bɔne akɔnnɔ he, na ɔyɔɔ adwuma yɛ nnipadua mu maa yɛsoo aba maa wuo. 6 Na afei deɛ, yɛade yɛ ho afiri mmara he mu, yɛawu afiri nea ɔfaa yɛ nommum he ase, na yɛnam Honhom he so ayɛ nkoa kwane foforɔ so, na ɔnyɛ kwane dadaa a ogyina atwerɛeɛ dadaa he so he.
Mmara he ne bɔne
7 Ne saa nti mmɛ ne yɛnka? Mmara he yɛ bɔne anaa? Daabi da! Nso, sɛ ɔnyɛ mmara he a, nka na menim bɔne. Sɛ mmara he anka sɛ, “Mma w’ani mmere adeɛ” a, nka na mennim nea bɛfrɛ no anibereɛ. 8 Nso bɔne, nyaa kwane, de anibereɛ nyinaa hyɛɛ me mu; na sɛ mmara nni hɔ a, na bɔne nso awu. 9Na mekwatii mmara he tenaa ase berɛ bi, nso berɛ a mmaransɛm he baeɛ he, bɔne nyaneɛ 10na mewuiɛ, na mmaransɛm korɔ he a ɔhyɛɛ nkwa ho bɔ he mmaa mehuu sɛ ɔyɛ wuo ma me. 11Nso bɔne nam mmaransɛm he so nyaa akwannya, daadaa me na ɔnam so kuu me. 12Nti mmara he yɛ kronkron, na mmaransɛm he yɛ kronkron, ɔtene na ɔyɛ papa nso.
13 Nti nea ɔyɛ papa he, adane wuo ama me anaa? Daabi da! Na mmom ɔyɛ bɔne a ɔnam nea ɔyɛ papa so de wuo di dwuma me mu, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ɔbɛda adi sɛ bɔne yɛ bɔne, na ɔnam mmaransɛm he so ama bɔneyɔ aboroso.
Ahutaeɛ mu apereapereɛ
14 Na yenim sɛ mmara he yɛ honhom; nso meya honam mu a, bɛatɔno me ahyɛ bɔne nkoasom ase. 15Mennte me ankasa me nneyɔeɛ ase, efiri sɛ mennyɔ nea mepɛ, nso meyɔ adeɛ pɔtee a metane. 16 Nti sɛ meyɔ adeɛ a mempɛ a, megye tom sɛ mmara he yɛ. 17Nso afei ɔnnyɛ me ne meyɔ saa, na mmom bɔne a ɔte me mu he a. 18 Na menim sɛ bibi papa biaa nte me mu, mekyerɛ, me honam mu. Mewɔ pɛ pa sɛ mɛyɔ nea ɔyɛ, nso mentumi nyɔ, 19efiri sɛ menyɔ papa a mepɛ he, nso bɔne a mempɛ he ne meyɔ. 20Nti sɛ nea mempɛ ne meyɔ deɛ a, hedeɛ ɔnyɛ me ne me yɔ bio, na mmom bɔne a ɔte me mu he a.
21Nti mehu sɛ ɔyɛ mmara sɛ berɛ a mepɛ sɛ meyɔ nea ɔyɛ he, bɔne bɛne me. 22Na m’ani gye Nyankopɔn mmara a ɔte me mu he ho, 23 nso mehu mmara foforɔ me honam akwaa mu a ɔne m’adwene mmara di asie, ɔma meyɔ bɔne a ɔya me nipadua mu he akoa. 24Me, mmɔborɔwa! Hwane ne bɛgye me afiri wuo nipadua yi mu? 25 Aseda mfa yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so nka Nyankopɔn!
Nti afei, m’adwene mu he meyɛ akoa ma Nyankopɔn mmara, nso me honam mu he meyɛ bɔne akoa.

english_text:
Living by the Power of God's Spirit
1If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished. 2The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death. 3The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin. 4He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
5People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things. 6If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace. 7Our desires fight against God, because they do not and cannot obey God's laws. 8If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
9You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him. 10But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins. 11 Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
12My dear friends, we must not live to satisfy our desires. 13If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires. 14Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children. 15 God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father. 16God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children. 17His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him.
A Wonderful Future for God's People
18 I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us. 19In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are. 20 Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope 21that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children. 22We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
23 The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free. 24And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping. 25However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
26In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words. 27 All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people. 28We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose, 29and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children. 30God then accepted the people he had already decided to choose, and he has shared his glory with them.
God's Love
31What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us? 32God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else? 33If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them? 34Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us. 35Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death? 36 It is exactly as the Scriptures say,
“For you we face death
all day long.
We are like sheep
on their way
to be butchered.”
37In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us. 38I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future, 39and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
translation_text:
Honhom mu nkwa
1 Nti afei afobuo biaa nni hɔ ma bɛ a bɛya Kristo Yesu mu, 2 efiri sɛ Honhom mu nkwa mmara he ama ho faahodie Kristo Yesu mu afiri bɔne ne wuo mmara he mu. 3 Na Nyankopɔn ayɔ nea mmara he antumi anyɔ, ɔne sɛ ɔnam honam mmerɛyɛ nti ɔsomaa ɔnoaa ne Ba, honam subane mu maa no bɛyɛɛ nipa te sɛ yɛ nnipa adebɔneyɛfoɔ yi sɛ ɔne bɔne nni asie, na odii bɔne so nkunim honam mu, 4 sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a nnepa a mmara he hwehwɛ sɛ yɛyɔ he bɛba mu, ya yɛ a yɛnnante honam fam, na mmom yɛnante Honhom fam he abrabɔ mu; 5efiri sɛ bɛ a bɛbɔ bɛ bra sɛnea honam pɛ he de bɛ adwene si honam fam nnoɔma so, nso bɛ a bɛbɔ bɛ bra Honhom mu he de bɛ adwene si Honhom mu nnoɔma so. 6 Nea ɔde n’adwene si honam nnoɔma so he kɔ wuom, na nea ɔde n’adwene si honhom nnoɔma so he nya nkwa ne asomdwie. 7 Saa nti adwene a osi honam nnoɔma so he tane Nyankopɔn; ɔmmrɛ ne ho ase nhyɛ Nyankopɔn mmara ase, nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ ontumi nyɔ saa mpo, 8na bɛ a bɛya honam mu he ntumi nsɔ Nyankopɔn ani.
9 Nso honni honam mu; hoya Honhom mu, esiane Nyankopɔn Honhom a ɔte ho mu nti. Biaa a onni Kristo Honhom he nyɛ Kristo dea. 10Sɛ Kristo te ho mu a, ewom sɛ honam he awu deɛ, ɔnam bɔne nti, nso honhom he wɔ nkwa ɔnam tenenee nti. 11Na afei sɛ nea ɔnyanee Yesu firii awufoɔ mu he Honhom te ho mu a, hedeɛ deɛ ɔnyanee Kristo firii awufoɔ mu he bɛma ho nnipadua a owu he nkwa ɔnam ne Honhom a ɔte ho mu he nti.
12 Nti, me nuanom, ɔnyɛ honam ne yɛde no ka na yɛatena ase sɛnea honam pɛ; 13 na sɛ hotena ase sɛnea honam pɛ a, hobewu; nso sɛ hode Honhom he ku honam he a, hobɛtena ase. 14Na dodoɔ a Nyankopɔn Honhom kyerɛ bɛ kwane he, bɛ ne bɛyɛ Nyankopɔn mma; 15 Na sɛ hoannya akoayɛ honhom a ɔbɛma ho asane akɔ hu mu bio, na mmom hoanya abayɛ honhom. Nti yetumi team sɛ, “Abba! Agya!” 16 Na ɔyɛ saa Honhom he ankasa ne yɛ honhom ne di adanseɛ sɛ yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn mma, 17 na sɛ yɛyɛ mma a, hedeɛ yedi adeɛ nso, yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn adedifoɔ, na yɛ ne Kristo bom di adeɛ; nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, yɛ ne no hu amaneɛ a, saa ara nso ne bɛbɛhyɛ yɛne no animuonyam abom.
Daakye animuonyam
18 Menim sɛ yentumi mfa wiase yi mu amanehunu ntoto animuonyam a bɛde bɛhyɛ yɛ daakye wiase he mu he ho. 19 Na abɔdeɛ he de anigyina ɔɔtwɛne Nyankopɔn mma ahoyie dendenndene; 20 na bɛde abɔdeɛ he ahyɛ nnoɔma funu ase a ɔnyɛ ɔnoaa ne pɛ so, na mmom nea ɔde no hyɛɛ aseɛ he pɛ so, abɔdeɛ he anidasoɔ ne 21 sɛ ɔbɛde ne ho afiri nkyekyereɛ ne porɔeɛ a bɛde no ahyɛ aseɛ he mu na obenya ahofadie ya Nyankopɔn mma animuonyam he mu. 22Yenim sɛ abɔdeɛ he nyinaa asi apinie awoko yeyaa mu de besi nnɛ; 23 na ɔnyɛ abɔdeɛ he nkoaa, na mmom yɛ nso a yɛanya aba a odi kane a ɔno ne Honhom he, yesi apinie yɛ mu dendenndene berɛ a yɛɛtwɛne sɛ bɛbɛgye yɛ ayɛ mma he, gyeɛ a bɛnam agyedeɛ so bɛgye yɛ afiri yɛ nnipadua mu he. 24 Na saa anidasoɔ yi mu ne bɛgyee yɛ nkwa. Na hwane ne hwɛ nea ne nsa aka kwane? Na sɛ nea yɛwɔ ho anidasoɔ he, yɛ nsa aka a, nka ɔnyɛ adeɛ sɛ yɛbɛkɔ so atwɛne sɛ ɔbɛba mu. 25Nso sɛ yɛ ani da nea yɛ nsa nkaeɛ so a, yɛde ntoboaseɛ twɛne.
26Saa ara nso ne Honhom he boa yɛ yɛ mmerɛyɛ mu; na yennim sɛnea ɔsɛ sɛ yɛbɔ mpaeɛ a ɔsɛ, nso Honhom he ankasa si apini a mu dɔ kyɛne nsɛm de di ma yɛ. 27Na Nyankopɔn a, ɔhwehwɛ akoma mu pefee he, nim Honhom he adwene, efiri sɛ Honhom he di ma ahoteefoɔ he sɛnea Nyankopɔn pɛ.
28 Yenim sɛ bɛ a bɛdɔ Nyankopɔn he, bɛ a ɔnam ne pɛ so afrɛ bɛ he, ɔfa adeɛ nyinaa so di boa bɛ ma no wie bɛ yie. 29 Na bɛ a ohuu bɛ sieeɛ he, oyii bɛ too hɔ sɛ bɛnsɛ ne Ba he, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ne Ba he bɛyɛ abakane nnipa bebree mu. 30Na bɛ a oyii bɛ sii hɔ he, ɔfrɛɛ bɛ nso; na bɛ a ɔfrɛɛ bɛ he, buu bɛ bem nso; na bɛ a obuu bɛ bem he, hyɛɛ bɛ animuonyam nso.
Nyankopɔn dɔ a ɔya Kristo Yesu mu
31 Hedeɛ mmɛ ne yɛbɛka afa yeinom ho? Sɛ Nyankopɔn ya yɛ afa a, hwane ne betumi ne yɛ adi asie? 32 Nea wamfa ɔno ankasa ne Ba ankame yɛ na ɔde no maa yɛ nyinaa he, ɔmmfa nnoɔma nyinaa nka ne (Ba he) ho mma yɛ kwa anaa? 33Hwane ne bɛbɔ Nyankopɔn mma a waayi bɛ he kwaadu? Nyankopɔn ne nea obu bi bem. 34 Hwane ne ɔsɛ sɛ obu atɛne? Kristo Yesu ne nea obu atɛne, nea owuiɛ, na benyanee no, nea ɔte Nyankopɔn nsa nifa, nea odi ma yɛ nɔkorem he. 35Hwane ne bɛte yɛ afiri Kristo dɔ ho? Ahokyerɛ mu oo, ateetee mu oo, taa mu oo, kɔm mu oo, adagyada mu oo, amaneɛ mu oo, wuo mu oo; bibiaa nni hɔ a obetumi ate yɛ afiri Kristo dɔ ho. 36 Sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ,
“Wo nti beku yɛ daa;
bebu yɛ sɛ dwane a bɛɛkɔ ku no.”
37Nso, yeinom nyinaa mu he yɛnam nea ɔdɔ yɛ he so adi nkunim. 38Na megye di sɛ mpo wuo, anaa nkwa, anaa abɔfoɔ, anaa atumfoɔ, anaa nnoɔma a ɔya hɔ saa mmerɛ yi, anaa nnoɔma a ɔbɛba, anaa ahoɔdene, 39anaa nea ɔya soro, anaa nea ɔya bunum, asaase ase, anaa abɔdeɛ biaa, mpo betumi ntete yɛ mmfiri Nyankopɔn dɔ a ɔya yɛ Awurade Kristo Yesu mu he ho.

english_text:
God's Choice of Israel
1I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say 2my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow. 3I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people. 4 They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them. 5They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
6It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God. 7-8 In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised 9 Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
10Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah. 11-12 Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done. 13 That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
14Are we saying God is unfair? Certainly not! 15 The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to. 16Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do. 17 In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.” 18Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn.
God's Anger and Mercy
19Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?” 20 But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did? 21 Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
22 God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them. 23He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory. 24Whether Jews or Gentiles, we are those chosen ones, 25 just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea,
“Although they are not
my people,
I will make them my people.
I will treat with love
those nations
that have never been loved.
26 “Once they were told,
‘You are not my people.’
But in that very place
they will be called
children of the living God.”
27 And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel,
“The people of Israel
are as many
as the grains of sand
along the beach.
But only a few who are left
will be saved.
28The Lord will be quick
and sure to do on earth
what he has warned
he will do.”
29 Isaiah also said,
“If the Lord All-Powerful
had not spared some
of our descendants,
we would have been destroyed
like the cities of Sodom
and Gomorrah.”
Israel and the Good News
30What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith. 31-32It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble, 33 just as God says in the Scriptures,
“Look! I am placing in Zion
a stone to make people
stumble and fall.
But those who have faith
in that one will never
be disappointed.”
translation_text:
Yi a Nyankopɔn ayi Israel
1Meeka Kristo mu nɔkorɛ, meenni torɔ; Honhom Kronkron nam m’ahonim so di adanseɛ. 2Medi awerɛhoɔ paa ne yeyaa m’akomam daa. 3 Nka mepɛ sɛ bɛde me ankasa yɛ abusudeɛ na beyi me firi Kristo mu, me mane mufoɔ a bɛyɛ me honam fam abusuafoɔ nti. 4 Bɛyɛ Israelfoɔ, na bɛ ne abayɛ, animuonyam, apam, ne mmara he som he, ɔne bɔhyɛ he nyinaa yɛ bɛ dea; 5bɛyɛ tete agyanom asefoɔ, na ofiri bɛ abusua mu ne Kristo he baa honam fam. Nhyira nka Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ adeɛ nyinaa sodifoɔ daa. Amen.
6 Na ɔnyɛ sɛ ebia Nyankopɔn asɛm he adi hwammɔ. Na ɔnyɛ bɛ a befiri Israel nyinaa ne bɛyɛ Israelfoɔ mapa, 7 na nyɛ Abraham mma nyinaa ne bɛyɛ n’asefoɔ mapa; nso bɛatwerɛ sɛ “Wasefoɔ firi Isak mu ne bɛba.” 8 Yei kyerɛ sɛ ɔnnyɛ honam fam mma ne yɛ Nyankopɔn mma, na mmom bɔhyɛ mma ne bebu bɛ sɛ n’asefoɔ ankasa. 9 Na sei ne bɔhyɛ he kaeɛ, “Berɛ sei mu mɛba, na Sara anya ba beema.” 10 Na ɔnyɛ yei nkoaa; saa berɛ a Rebeka nso nam ne kunu, yɛ nana Isak so nyinsɛneeɛ he, bibi siiɛ. 11Berɛ a na bennya nwoo bɛ mmaa bɛmmɛyɔɔ papa anaa bɔne bibiaa mu mpo he (sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a Nyankopɔn botaeɛ a ɔde yi bi berɛ a, 12 ɔmmfa nwumam so, na mmom ɔnam ne frɛ so he begyina nti) bɛka kyerɛɛ no (Rebeka) sɛ, “Panyini he bɛsom kumaa he.” 13 Sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ,
“Medɔɔ Yakob,
nso metanee Esau.”
14 Hedeɛ mmɛ ne yɛbɛka? Atɛntenenee nni Nyankopɔn fam? Daabi da! 15 Efiri sɛ ɔka kyerɛɛ Mose sɛ,
“Mehu nea mehu no mmɔbɔ mmɔbɔ,
na me yam bɛhyehye me ama nea meyam bɛhyehye no ama no.”
16 Ne saa nti onyina nipa pɛ anaa nhyɛ so, na mmom Nyankopɔn a ohu mmɔbɔ he so. 17 Na twerɛsɛm he ka kyerɛ Farao sɛ,” Maama wo so sɛ me botae a ɔkyerɛ me tumi he bɛda adi wɔ wo mu, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛbɛbɔ me dini asaase nyinaa so.” 18 Ne saa nti ohu biaa a waayi no mmɔbɔ, na opirim biaa ɔpɛ akoma.
Nyankopɔn abufuo ne n’ahummɔborɔ
19Hedeɛ wobebisa me sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne ɔda so hu mfomsoɔ? Na hwane ne betumi asi Nyankopɔn pɛ kwane?” 20 Nso ampa wone hwane, o! Nipa dasani, sɛ wo ne Nyankopɔn yiyi nsɛm ano? Nti nea bɛanwono no he betumi aka akyerɛ nea ɔnwonoo no he sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne woyɔɔ me sei?” 21Nwonfoɔ nni tumi nɛteɛ so, sɛ ɔde nɛteɛ korɔ heaa bi bɛyɔ bibi a anidie yam na foforɔ so nea anidie nnim? 22 Na sɛ, Nyankopɔn pɛ sɛ ɔda n’abufuo adi na ɔma behu ne tumi, nso ɔde abodwokyɛre bebree kura abufuo adeɛ a ɔda hɔ ma ɔsɛe he mu a; 23 na sɛ ɔnam saa kwane he so da n’animuonyam ahonya adi ma mmɔborɔhunu adeɛ a wasiesie ato hɔ dadaada ma animuonyam a— 24sɛ yɛ a ɔfrɛɛ yɛ yi nso yɛka ho a, na omfiri Yudafoɔ nkoaa mu na mmom Amanamane mufoɔ nso a, yɛbɛka sɛ? 25 Sɛnea Hosea mu nso ɔka sɛ,
“Bɛ a na bɛnnyɛ me nkurofoɔ he mɛfrɛ bɛ ‘me nkurofoɔ,’
na nea ɔnnyɛ me dɔfo he mɛfrɛ no ‘medɔfo.’ ”
26 “Na baabi a bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, ‘Honnyɛ me nkurofoɔ,’ he
hɔaa nso ne bɛbɛfrɛ bɛ Nyankopɔn Teasefoɔ he mma.”
27 Na Yesaia teaa Israel ho sɛ, “Ewom sɛ na Israel mma te sɛ mpoano anwea, nso bɛ a bɛbɛka kakraa bi ne bɛbɛgye bɛ nkwa; 28efiri sɛ Awurade bedi n’asɛm asaase yi so awie atwa no tiaa.” 29 Na sɛnea Yesaia kaa too hɔ he,
“Sɛ Nyankopɔn a ɔya soro annya nkaeɛfoɔ amma yɛ a,
nka yɛ ase hyee sɛ Sodom
ama yɛayɛ sɛ Gomora.”
Paulo mpaeɛ a ɔbɔ ma Israel
30 Afei deɛ ɛɛne ne yɛbɛka? Amanamane mufoɔ, a bɛanhwehwɛ tenenee he, bɛ nsa aka, ɔne sɛ, tenenee a ofiri gyidie mu; 31 nso Israel a, bɛpere hwehwɛɛ tenenee a ogyina mmara so he, bɛ nsa anka. 32Ɛɛne nti a? Efiri sɛ ɔnyɛ gyidie so ne bɛde hwehwɛeɛ, na mmom bɛnam nnwuma so, 33 sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ,
“Hwɛ, meeto fapem bi Sion a ɔbɛto nnipa bebree sintidua, botane a ɔbɛma bɛahwe ase,
na biaa a ɔbɛgye no adi biaa nsa onsi fam.”

english_text:
1Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved. 2I know they love God, but they don't understand 3what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law. 4But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith.
Anyone Can Be Saved
5 Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
6 But people whose faith makes them acceptable to God will never ask, “Who will go up to heaven to bring Christ down?” 7Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
8All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith. 9So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death. 10God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
11 The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed, 12no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help. 13 All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
14How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them? 15 And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news. 16 Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
17No one can have faith without hearing the message about Christ. 18 But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say,
“The message was told
everywhere on earth.
It was announced
all over the world.”
19 Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said,
“I will make Israel jealous
of people
who are a nation
of nobodies.
I will make them angry
with people
who don't understand
a thing.”
20 Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said,
“I was found by people
who were not looking
for me.
I appeared to the ones
who were not asking
about me.”
21 And Isaiah said about the people of Israel,
“All day long the Lord
has reached out
to people who are stubborn
and refuse to obey.”
translation_text:
1Anuanom, m’akoma so adeɛ ne mpaeɛ a mebɔ kyerɛ Nyankopɔn ne sɛ ɔbɛgye me mane Israel nkwa. 2Metumi adi adanseɛ sɛ bɛwɔ Nyankopɔn mu mmɔdemmɔ, nso benni nimdeɛ, 3 efiri sɛ, benni tenenee a ofiri Nyankopɔn hɔ he ho nimdeɛ, esiane sɛ bɛpɛ sɛ bɛde bɛ ankasa bɛ tenenee si hɔ nti, bɛmfa bɛ ho nhyɛ Nyankopɔn tenenee mu. 4 Na Kristo mu ne mmara he nyinaa wie pɛyɛ, nti bɛnam ne so de tenenee ma nea ɔbɛgye adi biaa.
Nkwagyeɛ ya hɔ ma biaa
5 Mose twerɛ faa tenenee a ofiri mmara he ho sɛ, “nipa a ɔyɔ saa nnoɔma yi benya nkwa.” 6 Nso tenenee a ofiri gyidie mu he dɛ, “Nka no w’akomam sɛ, Hwane ne bɛforo akɔ soro?” (Ɔne sɛ, ɔde Kristo bɛba fam), 7anaa “Hwane besiane akɔ bunum” (ɔne sɛ ɔde Kristo befiri awufoɔ mu aba). 8 Nso ɛɛne ne ɔka? Asɛm he bɛne wo, ɔya w’ano ne w’akomam (ɔne sɛ, gyidie asɛm a yɛpae mu ka); 9 efiri sɛ, sɛ wode w’ano pae mu ka sɛ Yesu ne Awurade na wode w’akoma gye di sɛ Nyankopɔn anyane no afiri awufoɔ mu a, wobenya nkwa. 10Na akoma ne bɛde gye di ma bebu bɛ bem, na ano ne bɛde pae mu ka nya nkwa. 11 Twerɛsɛm he dɛ, “Nea ɔgye no di biaa nsa onsi fam.” 12 Na yei ho he, nsosonoeɛ biaa nni Yudani anaa Helani ntam; Awurade korɔ heaa ne di bɛ nyinaa bɛ so, ne yam yɛ ma bɛ a bɛbɔ ne dini nyinaa, 13 efiri sɛ, “Nea ɔbɛbɔ Awurade dini biaa benya nkwa.”
14 Nso ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na bi atumi abɔ nea ɔnnye no nni dini? Na ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na bi agye nea ɔntee ne ho asɛm adi? Na sɛ bi anka Awurade ho asɛm ankyerɛ bɛ a bɛbɛyɛdɛ ate? 15 Na sɛ bɛansoma asɛmpakafoɔ a, ɔbɛyɛdɛ na bi akɔka asɛm he? Nti bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Hwɛ bɛ a bɛde asɛmpa he ba he bɛ nane ase ɔfɛ fa!” 16 Nso nyɛ bɛ nyinaa ne bɛayɔ setie ama asɛmpa he; efiri sɛ Yesaia dɛ, “Awurade, hwane ne agye yɛ asɛm adi?” 17 Nti gyidie firi nea yɛate mu ne ba, na nea yɛate nso firi Kristo asɛm he mu.
18 Nso meebisa sɛ, bɛntee koraa? Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ bɛate; efiri sɛ
“Bɛapae mu aka no asaase nyinaa so,
na bɛ nsɛm nso aduru wiase afanaa nyinaa.”
19 Meebisa bio sɛ, nti Israel ante aseɛ? Nea odi kane, Mose dɛ,
“Mɛma ho atwe bɛ a bɛnyɛ mane he ho ninkunu;
mɛma ho bo afu atia amane nkwaseafoɔ.”
20 Hedeɛ, Yesaia nyaa akokoɔduro paa sɛ ɔkaa sɛ,
“Bɛ a bɛanhwehwɛ me he, bɛahu me;
Maayi me ho adi akyerɛ bɛ a bɛanhwehwɛ me.”
21 Nso Israel ho deɛ ɔkaa sɛ, “Da nyinaa metrɛɛ me nsam maa mane asoɔdenfoɔ ne nyiyianofoɔ yi.”

english_text:
God Has Not Rejected His People
1 Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin. 2God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel? 3 He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
4 But the Lord told Elijah, “I still have 7,000 followers who have not worshiped Baal.” 5It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers. 6This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
7This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn, 8 just as the Scriptures say,
“God made them so stupid
that their eyes are blind,
and their ears
are still deaf.”
9 Then David said,
“Turn their meals
into bait for a trap,
so that they will stumble
and be given
what they deserve.
10Blindfold their eyes!
Don't let them see.
Bend their backs
beneath a burden
that will never be lifted.”
Gentiles Will Be Saved
11Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous. 12But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
13I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work. 14I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved. 15When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life. 16If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
17You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree. 18But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
19Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place. 20That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid. 21If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
22Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
23If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back. 24After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree.
The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
25My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in. 26 In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say,
“From Zion someone will come
to rescue us.
Then Jacob's descendants
will stop being evil.
27 This is what the Lord
has promised to do
when he forgives their sins.”
28The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors. 29God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
30At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy. 31And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy. 32All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
33 Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
34 “Has anyone known
the thoughts of the Lord
or given him advice?
35 Has anyone loaned
something to the Lord
that must be repaid?”
36 Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
translation_text:
Israel poɔ he nyɛ n’awieeɛ
1 Afei meebisa sɛ, nti Nyankopɔn apo ne nkurofoɔ? Daabi da! M’ankasa meyɛ Israelni a meyɛ Abraham aseni a mefiri Benyamin abusuakuo mu. 2 Nyankopɔn mpoo ne nkurofoɔ a oyii bɛ sieeɛ he. Wonnim deɛ twerɛsɛm he ka fa Elia ho, sɛnea ɔbɔɔ Isreal soboɔ kyerɛɛ Nyankopɔn? 3“Awurade bɛaku w’adiyifoɔ, bɛabubu w’afɔrebokyia; me nkoaa ne maaka, na bɛɛhwehwɛ me aku me.” 4 Na mmɛ mmuaeɛ ne Nyankopɔn de maa no? “Makora nnipa mpem nson ama me ho a bɛ nkotodwe nkotoo Baal da.” 5 Nti saa ne berɛ yi mu nso nnipa nkaeɛ bi ya hɔ a bɛnam adom so ayi bɛ. 6Na sɛ ɔyɛ adom a, ɔnna so nyinaa nnwuma so, anyɛ saa a, hedeɛ na adom he nyɛ adom ankasa.
7 Afei mmɛ bio? Israel nsa anka nea ɔɔhwehwɛ he. Bɛ a bɛayi bɛ he deɛ bɛ nsa kaeɛ, nso bɛ a bɛaka he deɛ beprim bɛ akoma, 8 sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ,
“Nyankopɔn ama bɛ honhom a ɔtɔ nko,
ani a onhu adeɛ
ne aso a ɔnte asɛm,
de besi nnɛ.”
9 Na Dawid dɛ,
“Ma bɛ adidipono nyɛ fidisum ne fidie,
sintiduatoɔ ne akatua mma bɛ;
10ma bɛ ani nyɛ kusuu sɛnea benhu adeɛ,
na ma bɛ akyi nkuntunu daa.”
Amanamane nkwagyeɛ
11 Nti meebisa sɛ, bɛasonti sɛ bɛbɛhwe ase? Daabi da! Nso ɔnam bɛ sintidua he so ama Amanamane mufoɔ anya nkwagyeɛ, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a Israel bɛtwe ninkunu. 12Na afei sɛ bɛ sintiduatoɔ he kyerɛ wiase ahonya a, na sɛ bɛ nkoguo de ahonya maa Amanamane mufoɔ a, hedeɛ na gye a bɛbɛgye bɛ atom he bɛboro so!
13Afei meekasa akyerɛ ho Amanamane mufoɔ. Esiane sɛ meyɛ somafoɔ ma Amanamane mufoɔ nti, mehyɛ me som animuonyam 14 sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a m’ankasa me nkurofoɔ bɛtwe ninkunu, na bɛagye binom. 15Na sɛ bɛpo a bɛpoo bɛ he bɛyɛ wiase mpataa a, hedeɛ bɛ gyeɛ nso bɛyɛ nkwa a ofiri wuom! 16Afei sɛ dua aba a odi kane a bɛde bɔɔ afɔdeɛ he yɛ kronkron a, aba mu he nyinaa nso yɛ kronkron; na sɛ ne nhini he yɛ kronkron a, ne mmane he nso nyinaa bɛyɛ kronkron.
17 Nso sɛ mmane he bi bu firii ho a, na wo, wuram ngodua, bɛde wo hyɛɛ ananem sɛ wobenya ahonya a ɔya ngodua nhini he mu bi a, 18 nhoahoa wo ho mmane he ho. Sɛ wohoahoa wo ho a, kae sɛ ɔnyɛ wo ne wokita nhini he mu, na mmom nhini he ne kita wo mu. 19Saa nti wobɛka sɛ, “Mmane bi bubuiɛ sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛde me besi ananem.” 20Ɛɛ, yei yɛ nɔkorɛ. Gye a bɛannye anni nti ne betwaa bɛtweneeɛ, nso gyidie nkoaa so ne wobegyina. Nti nyɛ ahantan, na mmom fa suro gyina pintinn. 21Na sɛ Nyankopɔn ankora mmane he ankasa a, ebia wo nso ɔnkora wo. 22 Hyɛ Nyankopɔn ayamyɛ ne ne dene yɛ nso: ɔdene ma bɛ a bɛhwee ase, nso Nyankopɔn ayamyɛ ya hɔ ma wo, gye sɛ wo toa so n’ayamyɛ he mu; anyɛ saa a wo nso bebetwa wo atwene. 23 Na mpo Israel nso, sɛ bɛantoa so ampirim bɛ akoma annyeannie ho a, bɛde bɛ bɛhyɛ mu bio, efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn wɔ tumi sɛ ɔde bɛ hyɛ mu bio. 24Sɛ wo mpo, betwaa wo firii wuram ngodua he ho, na bɛakwati wɔawosuo de wo atɛ ngodua pa he mu a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne ontumi mfa ne ngodua pa he ankasa mmane he ɔmmɛtɛ mu bio?
Bɛbɛgye Israel nyinaa nkwa
25 Nti, anuanom, mepɛ sɛ hote saa ahintasɛm yi ase, na hoammu ho ho anyansafoɔ ho ankasa ho ani so: akomapirim bi aba Israelfoɔ bi so, kosi sɛ Amanamane mufoɔ bebree bɛba mu. 26Ne saa nti bɛbɛgye Israelfoɔ nyinaa nkwa; sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ,
“Sion mu ne Gyefoɔ befiri aba;
obetu amumuyɔ afiri Yakob.”
27“Na yei ne m’apam a me ne bɛ bɛpam,
berɛ a meyi bɛ bɔne afiri hɔ.”
28Ɔfa asɛmpa he ho deɛ, ho nti bɛyɛ Nyankopɔn atamfoɔ; nso ɔfa yie he ho deɛ, bɛyɛ adɔfoɔ, bɛ nananom nti; 29efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn ntwe n’akyɛdeɛ ne ne frɛ nsane da. 30Sɛnea mmerɛ bi hoyɔɔ asoɔdene tiaa Nyankopɔn nso bɛ asoɔdene nti hoanya mmɔborɔhunu he, 31saa nso nti bɛayɛ asoɔdene a, ahummɔborɔ a hoanya nti, bɛ nso saa berɛ he bebenya mmɔborɔhunu. 32Nyankopɔn ama bɛ asoɔdene aka bɛ ato afiase sɛ obehu bɛ mmɔbɔ.
33O! Nyankopɔn ahonya ne ne nyansa ɔne ne nimdeɛ a mu dɔ! Yentumi nhu n’atemmuo ne n’akwane mu nwie da!
34“Na hwane ne ahu Awurade adwene?
Anaa hwane ne ayɛ ne fotufoɔ?”
35“Anaa hwane ne ama no akyɛdeɛ,
a ɔbɛgye akyɛdeɛ asi ananem?”
36Biaa nni hɔ efiri sɛ, nnoɔma nyinaa firi ne mu, na ɔnam ne so, na ɔyɛ ne dea. Ɔno ne animuonyam nka no daa daa. Amen.

english_text:
Christ Brings New Life
1Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God. 2Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
3I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you. 4 A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use. 5That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
6 God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have. 7If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach. 8If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully.
Rules for Christian Living
9Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good. 10Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself. 11Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord. 12Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying. 13Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
14 Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them. 15 When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad. 16 Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people. 17Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others, 18and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
19 Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says,
“I am the one to take revenge
and pay them back.”
20 The Scriptures also say,
“If your enemies are hungry,
give them something to eat.
And if they are thirsty,
give them something
to drink.
This will be the same
as piling burning coals
on their heads.”
21Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
translation_text:
Kristo mu nkwa foforɔ he
1 Nti anuanom, megyina Nyankopɔn mmɔborɔhunu so ɔɔsrɛ ho sɛ, homfa ho nipadua nsi hɔ sɛ afɔdeɛ a ɔte ase, a ɔyɛ kronkron a ɔsɔ Nyankopɔn ani. Yei ne nyamesom pa. 2 Na homfa wiase nhyehyɛeɛ yi so, na mmom honsakra ho adwene na honyɛ foforɔ, na hoasɛ ahwɛ ahu nea Nyankopɔn pɛ a ɔyɛ na ɔsɔ ani na odi mu.
3 Na menam adom a bɛde ama me he so, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ho mu biaa mmu ne ho ntra sɛnea ɔsɛ sɛ obu ne ho, na mmom, biaa nya anidahɔ adwene, sɛnea Nyankopɔn asusu gyidie ama ho he. 4 Na sɛnea honamdua baako wɔ akwaa bebree he, na biaa wɔ dwuma sonoko a odi he, 5 saa nso ne yɛ a yɛdɔɔso yi, yɛyɛ nipadua baako Kristo mu, na yɛyeyɛ yɛ ho akwaa mmaako mmaako. 6 Yɛwɔ adom akyɛdeɛ a nsonsonoeɛ deda mu, sɛnea adom a bɛde adom yɛ he teɛ. Sɛ ɔyɛ nkɔmhyɛ a, ma no mfata sɛnea bɛasusu ho gyidie; 7 som adwuma a, ma no nsom; kyerɛkyerɛni a, ma no nkyerɛkyerɛ; 8 fotufoɔ a, ma ontu fo; ɔma a, ma no mma no adɔeɛ mu; dikanfoɔ a, ma ɔmmɔ ne ho mmɔdene akannie mu; ahummɔborɔ a, ma ɔnyɔ no animteɛ mu.
Nɔkorɛ kristoni ahyɛnsodeɛ
9 Hommma nyaatwom mfra dɔ mu; hontane nea ɔyɛ bɔne, honkura nea ɔyɛ papa mu; 10 homfa nteaseɛ nnodɔ ho ho yie; homfa anidie mmɔho mma ho ho. 11 Honnyɛ mmɔdenmmɔ ho nyaa, honyɛ hye honhom mu, honsom Awurade! 12 Homma ho ani nnye anidasoɔ mu, honto ho boase amaneɛ mu, hontu ho ho nsi hɔ ma mpaebɔ. 13 Hommoa ahoteefoɔ ma bɛ nsa nka bɛ ahiadeɛ; homma ho ani nye ahɔhoyɛ ho.
14 Honhyira bɛ a bɛtaa ho; honhyira, na honnnome. 15 Ho ne bɛ a bɛ ani agye ani nnye mmom, ho ne bɛ a besu nso nsu mmom. 16 Ho ne nnipa nyinaa ntena asomdwie mu; honnnyɛ ahantan, na mmom ho ne ahobrɛaseɛfoɔ nante; honnyɛ ahomasoɔ. 17 Hommmfa bɔne ntua bɔne so ka, na mmom honwene nea odi mu nnipa nyinaa ani so. 18 Sɛ obetumi a, ho fam deɛ, ho ne nnipa nyinaa ntena asomdwiem. 19 Adɔfo, honnntotɔ ho ho ho ho werɛ, na mmom honnyae ma Nyankopɔn abufuo; efiri sɛ bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Awerɛtɔ yɛ me dea, meaa ne metua ka, Awurade ne ɔɔka.” 20 Ne saa nti, sɛ kɔm de wo tamfoɔ a, ma no bibi nni; sɛ sukɔm de no a, ma no nsuo nom; na sɛ woyɔ saa a, wobɛhyɛ no aniwuo. 21Mma bɔne nnni wo so, na mmom fa papa di bɔne so nim!

english_text:
Obey Rulers
1 Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power. 2People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished. 3Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it. 4After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you.
If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is. 5But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
6 You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters. 7Pay all that you owe, whether it is taxes and fees or respect and honor.
Love
8Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands. 9 In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.” 10No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands.
The Day When Christ Returns
11You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord. 12Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light. 13So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous. 14Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
translation_text:
Hommrɛ ho ho ase ma tumidie
1 Homma nnipa nyinaa mmrɛbrɛ bɛ ho ase nhyɛ mane mpanyimfoɔ tumi he ase; efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn ne ma nnipa tumi ma ɔde di dwuma, na Nyankopɔn ne de saa tumidie he sii hɔ. 2Saa nti biaa a ɔte atua tumidie biaa so he te nea Nyankopɔn de no asi hɔ he so atua, na bɛ a bɛyɔ saa he bɛkɔ afobuo mu. 3 Efiri sɛ bɛ a bedi mpanyinnie he ho nyɛ hu mma bɛ a bɛbɔ bra pa, na mmom bɛho ohu ma bɛ a bɛyɔ bɔne. Sɛ wompɛ sɛ wosuro tumfoɔ he a, hedeɛ yɔ nea ɔyɛ papa, na wobenya n’anim adom; 4 efiri sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn somfoɔ ma wo yiedie. Nso sɛ woyɔ bɔne a, ɔsɛ sɛ wosuro no, efiri sɛ tumidie he nni hɔ kwa, na mmom atumfoɔ he yɛ Nyankopɔn asomfoɔ a bɛtɔ were de abufuo ba deɛ ɔyɛ bɔne he so. 5Ne saa nti ɔsɛ sɛ nipa brɛ ne ho ase, nyɛ abufuo nkoaa nti na mmom suro a osuro atumfoɔ he ɔnoaa n’ahonim nti. 6Yei nti ne ɔsɛ sɛ wo tua toɔ nso, efiri sɛ atumfoɔ yi yɛ Nyankopɔn asomfoɔ, na mane he som ti ne bɛya hɔ. 7 Homfa nea ɔsɛ sɛ hode ma biaa ma no, hontua toɔ ma bɛ a ɔsɛ sɛ hotua toɔ ma bɛ, homfa akatua ma bɛ a akatua sɛ bɛ, hommu nea ɔfata buo, homfa anidie ma nea anidie sɛ no.
Honnodɔ ho ho ho ho
8 Honnne biaa ka, gye sɛ dɔ a hode bɛdodɔ ho ho; na nea ɔdɔ foforɔ he awie mmara he so di, 9 efiri sɛ mmaransɛm a ɔka sɛ, “Mmɔ adwamane; Nni awu; Mmɔ korɔno; Mma w’ani mmere” ne mmaransɛm foforɔ biaa, bɛde ne nyinaa abom asɛm baako yi mu, “Dɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.” 10 Deɛ ɔdɔ ne yɔnko nyɔ no bɔne; nti dɔ ne ma mmara he nyinaa ba mu.
Adesrɛ a ohia
11 Yeinom nyinaa akyi he, honim sɛnea berɛ he teɛ, sɛ saa berɛ yi ne ɔsɛ sɛ hosɔre firi ho nna mu; efiri sɛ nkwagyeɛ bɛne yɛ kyɛne berɛ a yɛbɛyɛɛ agyidifoɔ; 12 anadwo akɔ n’anim, adekyeɛ abɛne. Saa nti ho ma yɛnto sum nnwuma ngu na yɛmfa hann akodeɛ; 13 homma yɛmmɔ bra a ɔfata hann mma, ɔnyɛ agobɔne ne nsaboro mu, nyɛ adwamammɔ ne akohwie mu, nyɛ ntɔkwakoo ne aniberebereɛ mu. 14 Na mmom, homfa Awurade Yesu Kristo nhyɛ (sɛ ataadeɛ), na homma kwane mma honam, akɔnnɔ nni ho so!.

english_text:
Don't Criticize Others
1 Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours. 2Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables. 3But you should not criticize others for eating or for not eating. After all, God welcomes everyone. 4What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
5Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind. 6Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
7Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves. 8Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord. 9This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living. 10 Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us. 11 In the Scriptures God says,
“I swear by my very life
that everyone will kneel down
and praise my name!”
12And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do.
Don't Cause Problems for Others
13We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith. 14The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
15If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for. 16Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ. 17God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit. 18If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people. 19We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
20Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat. 21It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord. 22What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts. 23But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
translation_text:
Hommmu biaa atɛne
1 Honnye nea ne gyidie ɔmmerɛ he na hommmu atɛne ya adwene nnaayɛ ho. 2 Binom gye di sɛ bɛwɔ ho kwane sɛ bedi nnoɔma nyinaa, na nea ɔmmerɛ he deɛ odi nhahama nkoaa. 3 Ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛ a bedi nnoɔma nyinaa he bebu bɛ a benni nnoɔma bi he animtia, na bɛ a benni nnoɔma bi he nso, ɔnsɛ sɛ bebu bɛ a bedi he atɛne; efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn agye saa nipa he. 4 Wo ne hwane sɛ wobebu bi akoa atɛne? Ɔyɛ bɛ wura he ankasa ne bɛkyerɛ sɛ nea bɛayɔ he yɛ anaa ɔnyɛ; efiri sɛ nɔkorem he Awurade betumi ama bɛagyina
5 Binom bu atɛne sɛ da bi di mu kyɛne bi, binom nso bu nna nyinaa pɛ. Homma biaa nte ne ho ase sɛnea n’adwene kyerɛ no. 6Bɛ a bebu da bi he, bɛde hyɛ Awurade animuonyam. Na bɛ a bedi he nso, bedi hyɛ Awurade animuonyam, esiane sɛ bɛda Nyankopɔn ase; bɛ a benni he nso, di a benni he bɛde hyɛ Awurade animuonyam, na bɛda Nyankopɔn ase.
7Na yɛnnte ase mma yɛ ho, saa so ne yenwu mma yɛ ho. 8 Sɛ yɛte ase a, yɛte ase ma Awurade, na sɛ yewu nso a, yewu ma Awurade; nti, sɛ yɛte ase oo, anaa sɛ yewu oo, yɛyɛ Awurade dea. 9Na yei nti Kristo wuiɛ na ɔte ase bio, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ɔbɛyɛ awufoɔ ne ateasefoɔ nyinaa bɛ Awurade.
10 Mmɛ nti ne wobu wo ankasa wo nua atɛne? Anaa wo, ɛɛne nti ne wobu wo nua animtia? 11 Na bɛatwerɛ sɛ,
“Awurade dɛ, sɛ mete ase yi, kotodwe nyinaa bɛkoto me,
na kɛtrɛma nyinaa beyi Nyankopɔn ayɛ.”
12 Ne saa nti, yemu biaa bebu n’ankasa akonta akyerɛ Nyankopɔn.
Honto biaa sintidua
13Afei hommma yemmu biaa atɛne, nso homma yɛnyɔ yɛadwene sɛ yɛmmfa akwansideɛ nto biaa kwane mu, saa ara nso ne yɛnnto biaa sintidua. 14 Menim na megye di Awurade Yesu mu sɛ bibi biaa nni hɔ a honnte ankasa; nso honnte mma nea ɔdwene sɛ honnte. 15 Sɛ aduane a wodi sɛe wo nua ahonim a, hedeɛ na ɔnyɛ dɔ mu ne woonante he. Mma aduane a wodi he nto bi a Kristo wu maa no he sintidua. 16 Nti hommma bɛmmu ho papayɔ sɛ ɔyɛ bɔne; 17 efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn ahennie he mfa adidie ne nom ho, na mmom ne nyinasoɔ ne tenenee ne asomdwie ne Honhom Kronkron mu anigyeɛ. 18Na nea ɔsom Kristo saa kwane so he ɔno ne sɔ Nyankopɔn ani na nnipa gye no tom. 19 Nti homma yenni asomdwie ɔne nnoɔma a ɔma nkɔsoɔ akyi. 20 Aduane nti, honnsɛe Nyankopɔn adwuma. Adeɛ nyinaa ho te deɛ, nso ɔnnyɛ sɛ aduane a wodi nti woma bi hwe ase; 21 ɔyɛ sɛ wonnwe nam anaa wonnom nsa anaa sɛ wonnyɔ bibi a ɔbɛto wo nua sintidua. 22Gyidie a wowɔ he, ma ɔnyɛ sɛnea w’ahonim teɛ Nyankopɔn anim. Nhyira ne nea a ɔmma nea wasi ho gyinaeɛ he mmu ne fɔ. 23 Nso bɛ a bɛ adwene nsi bɛ pi he sɛ bedi a bedi fɔ, efiri sɛ bɛannyɔ no gyidie mu, na bibiaa a omfiri gyidie mu biaa yɛ bɔne.

english_text:
Please Others and Not Yourself
1If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves. 2We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them. 3 Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.” 4 And the Scriptures were written to teach and encourage us by giving us hope. 5God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ. 6Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
7Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you. 8I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came, 9 so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say,
“I will tell the nations
about you,
and I will sing praises
to your name.”
10 The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
11 Again the Scriptures say,
“Praise the Lord,
all you Gentiles.
All you nations, come
and worship him.”
12 Isaiah says,
“Someone from David's family
will come to power.
He will rule the nations,
and they will put their hope
in him.”
13I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope.
Paul's Work as a Missionary
14My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other. 15But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace! 16He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
17Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God. 18In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him. 19Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit.
