Efiri sɛ maahyɛ bɔ sɛ mede ho bɛma agye beema baako awadeɛ, na mede ho ama Kristo sɛ babunu bi a fi nka no da. 3 Nso mesuro sɛ, ebia, sɛdeɛ wɔ he de n’aniteɛ bɛdaadaa Hawa he, saa ara nso ne bɛbɛdane ho adwene afiri Kristo mu ahofama mapa a owie pɛyɛ he. 4 Na sɛ bi bɛpae mu ka Yesu foforɔ a ɔnyɛ nea yɛkaa ne ho asɛm he ho asɛm kyerɛ ho a, anaa sɛ hogye honhom foforɔ bi a ɔnyɛ nea hogyeeɛ he a, anaa sɛ hogye asɛmpa foforɔ a ɔnyɛ nea hoagye he a, hedeɛ na hoagye no ntɛm dodo. 5 Nso mehu sɛ asomafoɔ a hobu bɛ atitire he nkyɛne me bibiaa mu. 6 Na sɛ m’ano nteeɛ kasa mu mpo a, ɔnyɛ nimdeɛ mu; nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, yɛama bibiaa ada adi ama ho, nnipa nyinaa anim.
7 Anaa mayɔ bɔne sɛ mekaa Nyankopɔn asɛmpa he kyerɛɛ ho kwa na mebrɛɛ me ho ase sɛ ɔbɛyɔ a bɛbɛma ho so? 8 Mefom asafo binom adeɛ faeɛ sɛ akatua sɛ mede bɛsom ho. 9 Na meya ho nkyɛne he a bibi hia me he, manyɛ adesoa amma biaa, efiri sɛ, anuanom a bɛya Makedonia de m’ahiadeɛ nyinaa maa me. Nɔkorem, manyɛ ho so adesoa bibiaa mu na mɛhwɛ sɛ menyɛ saa nso. 10 Sɛ Kristo nɔkorɛ da so ya me mu he, m’ahohoahoa yi bɛkɔ so ada adi ya Akaia fam hɔ. 11Ɛɛne ntia meyɔ saa? Mennɔ ho nti anaa? Nyankopɔn koraa nim sɛ medɔ ho!
12Na deɛ meyɔ he mɛkɔ so ayɔ, na bɛ a bɛɛpɛ bibi agyina so ahoahoa bɛ ho sɛ bɛne yɛ yɛ pɛ he annya bibiaa. 13 Na saa nnipa yinom yɛ asomafoɔ atorɔfoɔ, adwumayɔfoɔ nnaadaafoɔ, bɛyɛ bɛ ho sɛ Kristo asomafoɔ. 14Na ɔnyɛ nwanwa! Satan mpo tumi yɛ ne ho sɛ hann bɔfoɔ. 15Nti ɔnyɛ nwanwa sɛ n’asomafoɔ nso bɛyɛ bɛ ho sɛ nɔkorɛ asomafoɔ. Bɛ nnwuma ne bɛkyerɛ bɛ awieeɛ.
Paulo Amanehunu sɛ Somafoɔ
16 Meka bio sɛ, biaa mmmu me sɛ meyɛ kwasea, na sɛ ɔnte saa a, honnye me sɛ kwasea, na me nso mahoahoa me ho kakra. 17Deɛ meeka he, menka no Awurade tumi mu, na mmom meka no sɛ bi a ɔyɛ adwenmuhareni ɔnam ahotɔsoɔ ntrasoɔ a wanya no ahohoahoa mu nti. 18Esiane sɛ nnipa bebree hoahoa bɛ ho honam fam nti, me nso mɛhoahoa me ho bi 19Na hode anigyeɛ nya tema ma nkwaseafoɔ, efiri sɛ hoyɛ mmadwemma. 20Na sɛ bi di ho nya, sɛ bi didi ho ho, sɛ bi fa ho nommum, sɛ bi ma ne ho so, sɛ bi bɔ ho ani so a, hobehu ama no.
Paulo asiane ne ne brɛ
21Meka no aniwuo mu sɛ, yɛ deɛ yɛayɛ mmerɛ sɛ yebetumi ayɔ saa; nso deɛ bi yɛ nnam ya mu he, (meka no sɛ deɛ aba biaa nni mu,) me nso meyɛ nnam ho mu bi. 22 Bɛyɛ Hebrifoɔ? Me nso meyɛ bi. Bɛyɛ Israelfoɔ? Me nso meyɛ bi. Bɛyɛ Abraham asefoɔ? Me nso meyɛ bi. 23Bɛyɛ Kristo asomfoɔ? Meyi ano te sɛ bɔdamni, meyɛ Kristo somafoɔ kyɛne bɛ: Brɛ adwuma a ɔboro so mu, afiaseda a ɔboro so mu, hweɛ a ɔtra so mu, wuo amaneɛ a manya no nhunuiɛ mu. 24 Yudafoɔ nsam, bɛabɔ me mmaa aduasankron, saa mprenum. 25 Bɛaka me mpire mprɛnsa, bɛasi me aboɔ prɛko, maatɔ nsuo mu mprɛnsa, maadi po bunu mu adekyeeɛ ne adesaeɛ. 26Akwantuo pii mu, asubɔntene so asiane mu, adwotwafoɔ asiane mu, amannifoɔ mu asiane mu, amanamane mu asiane mu, kuro mu asiane mu, serɛ so asiane mu, po so asiane mu, anuanom atorɔfoɔ mu asiane mu; 27 adwuma ne brɛ mu, apesire mu, kɔm ne sukɔm mu, mmuada pii mu, awɔ ne adagya mu. 28 Deɛ ɔkeka ho he nyinaa nna nkyɛne, deɛ ɔhyɛ me so ne asafo he nyinaa ho dadwene. 29 Hwane ne ɔɔmmerɛ, na menyɛ mmerɛ bi? Hwane ne bɛato no sintidua, na me yam nhyehye me?
30 Sɛ ɔsɛ sɛ mehoahoa me ho a, mɛhoahoa me ho me mmerɛyɛ ho. 31 Awurade Yesu Nyankopɔn ne n’Agya a nhyira nka no daa he nim sɛ menni torɔ. 32Damasko hɔ ne Hene Areta amrado wɛnee Damaskofoɔ kuro he sɛ ɔɔpɛ me akye me; 33 na bɛde me too kentene mu de me wuraa mpomaa mu faa fasuo so gyaagyaa me maa mefirii ne nsa dwaneeɛ.

english_text:
Visions from the Lord
1I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me. 2I know about one of Christ's followers who was taken up into the third heaven 14 years ago. I don't know if the man was still in his body when it happened, but God certainly knows.
3As I said, only God really knows if this man was in his body at the time. 4But he was taken up into paradise, where he heard things too wonderful to tell. 5I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
6Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say. 7Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
8Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away. 9But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am. 10Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong.
Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
11I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles. 12When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle. 13You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
14I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children. 15So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much? 16You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you. 17Were you cheated by any of those I sent to you? 18I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
19Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
20I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob. 21I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
translation_text:
Anisoadehunu ne mmerɛyɛ
1Ɔsɛ sɛ mehoahoa me ho, nso mfasoɔ biaa nni so, nti mɛka Awurade anisoadehunu ne adiyie ho asɛm. 2Menim nipa bi a ɔya Kristo mu a mfie dunan a atwam yi behwimm no kɔɔ soro a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa he mu, sɛ yei sii honam mu oo, sɛ ansi honam mu oo, mennim; Nyankopɔn nkoaa ne nim. 3Na menim sɛ saa nipa he—sɛ ɔyɛ honam mu oo, sɛ ɔnyɛ honam mu oo, mennim; na Nyankopɔn nkoaa ne nim— 4behwimm no kɔɔ Paradise na ɔtee nsɛm a ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛbɔ so, na nipa nni ho kwane sɛ ɔka mpo. 5 Saa nipa yi ho ne mehoahoa me ho, na me a me fam deɛ menhoahoa me ho, gye sɛ me mmerɛyɛ ho. 6 Na sɛ mepɛ sɛ mehoahoa me ho mpo a, nka ɔnyɛ adeɛ a nteaseɛ nni mu, efiri sɛ mɛka nɔkorɛ, nso menkɔ so nhoahoa me ho, na ho mu bi ammu me ankyɛne sɛdeɛ waate anaa waahu afa me ho, 7 adiyie a ɔboro so he nti. Nso sɛ ɔbɛyɔ a memma mabati so mma no mmoro so nti bɛama me Satan bɔfoɔ bi a ɔte sɛ kasɛe a ɔhyɛ me honam mu sɛ ɔmma me tim ɔnyɛ me dɛ mmoro. 8Mesrɛɛ Awurade mprɛnsa fa yei ho, sɛ ɔma no mfiri me so, 9nso ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “M’adom dɔɔso ma wo, na metumi wie pɛyɛ ya mmerɛyɛ mu.” Nti mede anigyeɛ bebree bɛhoahoa me ho mmom, na Kristo ahoɔdene abɛtena me so. 10 Nti m’ani gye mmerɛyɛ mu, atennie mu, ahokyerɛ mu, taa mu, ɔne amaneɛ mu Kristo nti; efiri sɛ meyɛ mmerɛ honam mu a meyɛ dene honhom mu.
Somafoɔ bi adansedie nwoma
11 Maayɛ sɛ kwasea! Ho ne hohyɛɛ me ma meyɔɔ saa. Nɔkorɛ di ɔsɛ sɛ nka hoyi me ayɛ, efiri sɛ asomafoɔ a hobu bɛ atitire he nkyɛne me bibiaa mu, nso menyɛ hwee mpo. 12 Somafoɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne mapa he deɛ, bɛayɔ no ho mu boasetɔ nyinaa mu, nsɛnkyerɛnne ne anwanwadeɛ ne tumideɛ mu. 13 Sɛ ɔnyɛ adesoa a mayɛ ama ho a, mmɛ bio nti ne honto asafo a aka he? Mesrɛ ho, homfa mfomsoɔ yi nkyɛ me.
14 Hwɛ, masiesie me ho sɛ meeba honkyɛne ne mprɛnsa so. Na menyɛ ho so adesoa, efiri sɛ menhwehwɛ ho hɔ bibiaa ka ho kra ho; na ɔnsɛ sɛ mma ne pɛ adeɛ ma awofoɔ na mmom awofoɔ ne pɛ ma mma. 15 Na me deɛ mede anigyeɛ bɛhwere m’adeɛ, na mama bɛahwere me ama ho kra. Na sɛ medɔ ho maa no boroo so a, ɔsɛ sɛ ho dɔ he sua sene me deɛ? 16Ɔyam sɛ manyɛ ho so adesoa. Nso sɛ meyɛ nitefoɔ nti, menam kwane foforɔ so anya ho ho mfasoɔ. 17Menam bɛ a mesomaa bɛ ho nkyɛne he mu bi so asisi ho anaa? 18 Mesrɛɛ Tito, na memaa ɔne onua baako baa ho nkyɛne. Tito ba besisii ho anaa? Ɔnyɛ Honhom baako heaa mu ne yɛnanteeɛ? Ɔnyɛ anammɔno baako mu ne yetiaeɛ?
19Saa berɛ yi nyinaa hosusu sɛ yeyi yɛ ho ano kyerɛ ho anaa? Nyankopɔn anim ne yɛkasa Kristo mu. Nea yɛyɛ biaa, adɔfoɔ, yɛyɛ ma ho nkɔsoɔ. 20 Na mesuro sɛ, sɛ meba a, memmɛto ho sɛnea mepɛ; na menso honhu me sɛnea hopɛ; mesuro sɛ ebia na ntɔkwa, ahoɔyeyaa, abufuo, asinkane, nsekuro, wesewese, ahantan ne sakasakayɛ ya ho mu. 21 Mesuro sɛ sɛ meba bio a, me Nyankopɔn bɛbrɛ me ase ya ho anim, na magyam ho mu dodoɔ a hoayɔ bɔne na hoansakra ho adwene, ne adwamammɔ ne akohwisɛm nea bɛadi he ho.

english_text:
Final Warnings and Greetings
1 I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.” 2During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning. 3This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful! 4Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
5Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed. 6I hope you will discover we have not failed. 7We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
8All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it. 9Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better. 10I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
11Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other.
Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you. 12Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
13I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will bless you and be kind to you! May God bless you with his love, and may the Holy Spirit join all your hearts together.
translation_text:
Sɔhwɛ ho asotwe
1 Mprɛnsa so di ne meeba honkyɛne. “Adansefoɔ mmienu anaa mmeɛnsa ano asɛm so ne kwaadu biaa begyina.” 2Mebɔɔ bɛ a bɛyɔɔ bɔne kane he ne bɛ a bɛaka he nyinaa kɔkɔ, saa nso ne seesei a menni ho hɔ he, meebɔ ho kɔkɔ, sɛnea meyɔɔ no berɛ a mebaa ho nkyɛne ne mprenu so he, sɛ meba bio a, menhu bɛ mmɔbɔ, 3efiri sɛ hoohwehwɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne sɛ Kristo ne fa me so kasa. Ɔnyɛ mmerɛ ho fam, na mmom ɔwɔ tumi ho mu. 4 Bɛbɔɔ no asennua mu sɛ nipa a ɔmerɛ, nso ɔnam Nyankopɔn tumi so te ase. Na yɛ nso yɛyɛ mmerɛ ne mu deɛ, nso yɛne no nam Nyankopɔn tumi so bɛtena ase ho fam.
5 Honsɔ ho ho nhwɛ sɛ hote gyidie he mu anaa. Hommisa ho ho nhwɛ! Anaa honnim sɛ Yesu Kristo te ho mu?—Gye sɛ ebia homfata deɛ. 6Na m’ani da so sɛ hobehu sɛ yɛ deɛ yɛnyɛ nnipa a bɛmfata. 7Nso yɛbɔ Nyankopɔn mpaeɛ, na hoanyɔ bɔne biaa, ɔnyɛ sɛ ɔbɛkyerɛ sɛ yɛmfata, na mmom, sɛnea ɔbɛyɔ na hoanya adepa, na yɛ deɛ yɛayɛ sɛ nea yɛmfata. 8Na yentumi nyɔ hwee ntia nɔkorɛ he, mmom nea yetumi yɔ he, yɛyɔ ma nɔkorɛ. 9Yɛ ani gye berɛ a yɛyɛ mmerɛ na ho ho ɔdene.
Yɛbɔ mpaeɛ sɛ hobɛyɛ pɛ. 10Yei nti ne menni ho nkyɛne he, meetwerɛ yei abra ho, na sɛ meba a, me ne ho anni no dendene, sɛnea tumi a Awurade de ama me sɛ memfa nhyɛ dene na memmfa nsɛe he teɛ. 11 Afei, nea otwa toɔ, me nuanom, makra ho. Honyɛ pɛ, honkyekyere ho ho ho ho werɛ, honnya adwene korɔ, hontena asomdwie mu, na dɔ ne asomdwie Nyankopɔn bedi ho akyi. 12 Honkyeakyea ho ho dɔ kwane so. 13Ahoteefoɔ he nyinaa kyeakyea ho. 14 Awurade Yesu Kristo adom, Nyankopɔn dɔ ne Honhom Kronkron ayɔnkofa nka ho nyinaa.

english_text:
1-2From the apostle Paul and from all the Lord's followers with me.
I was chosen to be an apostle by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised him from death. No mere human chose or appointed me to this work.
To the churches in Galatia:
3I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! 4Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world. 5God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen.
The Only True Message
6I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message, 7when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ. 8I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven. 9I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
10I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ.
How Paul Became an Apostle
11My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach. 12It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
13 You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it. 14 I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us. 15 But even before I was born, God had chosen me by his gift of undeserved grace and had decided 16to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone. 17I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
18 Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for 15 days. 19The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother. 20And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
21Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia. 22But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person. 23They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy. 24And because of me, they praised God.
translation_text:
Nkyea
1 Me Paulo a meyɛ somafoɔ a ɔnyɛ nipa ne yii me, na afei nso bɛamfa amfa nipa bi so ne yii me, na mmom Yesu Kristo ne Agya Nyankopɔn a ɔnyanee Kristo firii awufoɔ mu he ne yii me, 2ne anuanom a bɛka me ho nyinaa, twerɛ krataa yi de kɔma asafo ahodoɔ a bɛya Galatia.
3 Adom ne asomdwie a ofiri yɛ Agya Nyankopɔn ne Awurade Yesu Kristo nka ho. 4 Yesu Kristo a yɛ bɔne nti ɔde ne ho maeɛ sɛ ɔbɛgye yɛ afiri wiase yi bɔne mu, sɛnea Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ yɛ Agya pɛ he teɛ, 5ɔno ne animuonyam nka no daa daa. Amen.
Asɛmpa foforɔ biaa nni hɔ bio
6Ɔyɛ me ahodwiri sɛ hootwe ho ho ntɛm afiri nea ɔnam Kristo adom so frɛɛ ho he ho na hoodane ho ho akɔfa nea hodwene sɛ ɔyɛ asɛmpa foforɔ, 7 nso asɛmpa foforɔ biaa nni hɔ, na mmom binom ya hɔ a bɛɛsɛe ho adwene, na bɛpɛ sɛ bɛdane Kristo asɛmpa he. 8 Nso sɛ yɛ mpo anaa abɔfoɔ bi firi soro bɛka asɛmpa bi a ɔne nea yɛka kyerɛɛ ho he bɔ abira a, mmusuo nka oniyaa! 9Sɛnea yɛadi kane aka he, saa ne meeka no bio sɛ, sɛ bi bɛka asɛm bi a ɔne nea hogyeeɛ he bɔ abira a, onii he nnue!
10 Na afei, nnipa ne mepɛ sɛ bɛgye me tom anaa Nyankopɔn? Anaa mepɛ sɛ mesɔ nnipa ani? Sɛ meda so hwehwɛ sɛ mɛsɔ nnipa ani a, hedeɛ, na menyɛ Kristo akoa.
Paulo asomafoyɛ si pi
11Anuanom, mepɛ sɛ hohu sɛ, asɛmpa a meka kyerɛɛ ho he mfiri nnipa hɔ ne baeɛ; 12efiri sɛ, mannya no nnipa nkyɛne, saa ara nso ne ɔnyɛ nea nipa bi ne kyerɛkyerɛɛ me, na mmom ɔnam Yesu Kristo adiyie mu ne me nsa kaeɛ.
13 Menim paa sɛ hoate abrabɔ a medii kane bɔɔ sɛ Yudani he ho asɛm. Metaa Nyankopɔn asafo he maa no traa so, na mebɔɔ mmɔdene sɛ mɛsɛe no koraa. 14Mekɔɔ nkane Yuda som mu kyɛnee m’atipɛnfoɔ bebree, efiri sɛ meyɛɛ nam nananom atetesɛm mu. 15 Nso ɔsɔɔ Nyankopɔn a oyii me firii me maame yam na ɔnam n’adom so frɛɛ me he ani, 16 sɛ obeyi ne Ba he adi akyerɛ me, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a mɛpae mu aka ne ho asɛm Amanamane mufoɔ mu he, me ne nipa biaa antu ho agyina. 17Saa nso ne mankɔ asomafoɔ a na bɛadi kane ayi bɛ dada he nkyɛne Yerusalem, na mmom mekɔɔ Arabia ntɛm pa so, na akyire he, mesane baa Damasko.
18 Afei mfie mmeɛnsa akyi he, mekɔɔ Yerusalem sɛ meekɔsra Kefa na metenaa ne nkyɛne dadu nnanum; 19 nso manhu asomafoɔ biaa gye sɛ Awurade nua Yakobo nkoaa. 20Nea meetwerɛ abra ho yi, Nyankopɔn ne me danseni sɛ, menni torɔ! 21 Na afei mekɔɔ Siria ne Kilikia mpɔtamu. 22Na nnipa bebree a na bɛya Kristo mu Yuda asafo mu he, na benhuu m’anim da; 23na te nkoaa ne na bɛate sɛ, “Nea na kane ɔtaataa yɛ he, saa berɛ yi deɛ, ɔɔka gyidie a na nka ɔsɛe no he ho asɛm.” 24Na me nti bɛhyɛɛ Nyankopɔn animuonyam.

english_text:
1 Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus. 2But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
3Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised. 4We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves. 5But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
6 Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message. 7They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews. 8God, who had sent Peter on a mission to the Jews, was now using me to preach to the Gentiles.
9James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews. 10They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do.
Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
11When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong. 12He used to eat with Gentile followers of the Lord, until James sent some Jewish followers. Peter was afraid of the Jews and soon stopped eating with Gentiles. 13He and the others hid their true feelings so well that even Barnabas was fooled. 14But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said:
Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
15We are Jews by birth and are not sinners like Gentiles. 16 But we know that God accepts only those who have faith in Jesus Christ. No one can please God by simply obeying the Law. So we put our faith in Christ Jesus, and God accepted us because of our faith.
17When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't! 18But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first. 19It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God.
I have been nailed to the cross with Christ. 20I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me. 21I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
translation_text:
Paulo ne asomafoɔ a bɛaka he
1 Na mfie dunan akyi he, mekɔɔ Yerusalem bio a na Barnaba ne Tito ka me ho. 2 Ɔnam adiyie bi so nti ne mekɔeɛ. Na mede asɛmpa a meka no Amanamane mufoɔ mu he too bɛ anim (mmom ɔyɛ mpanyimfoɔ a bɛagye bɛ atom he ne me ne bɛ yɔɔ kokoam nhyiamu), sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a mirika a meetu, anaa nea metuiɛ he, ɔnyɛ kwa. 3 Nso bɛanhyɛ Tito mpo a na ɔka me ho a ɔyɛ Helani he sɛ ontwa twetia. 4 Nso ɔnam sɛ na anuanom atorɔfoɔ bi awiawia bɛ ho aba yɛ mu sɛ bɛbɛtetɛ yɛ ahwɛ ahofadie a yɛwɔ no Kristo Yesu mu, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛde yɛ bɛkɔ Mmara nkoasom mu nti 5 yɛ deɛ, yɛampene so amma bɛ koraa, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a asɛmpa he bɛkɔ so atena ho mu. 6Na bɛ a nnipa bu bɛ sɛ akannifoɔ he (sɛnea bɛteɛ biaa nyɛ hwee mma me; Nyankopɔn nyɛ nyiyimu), mefam deɛ saa akannifoɔ he amfa bibi foforɔ biaa amma; 7 na mmom, berɛ a behuu sɛ bɛde bɛ a bentwaa bɛ twetia mu asɛmpa-ka ahyɛ me nsa, sɛnea Petro nso bɛde twetiatwafoɔ mu asɛmpa ka ahyɛ ne nsa he 8(na nea ɔde Petro yɔɔ adwuma sɛ somafoɔ maa twetiatwafoɔ he, ɔno ankasa nso ne yɔɔ me mu adwuma sɛ ɔɔsoma me Amanamane mufoɔ mu), 9 na berɛ a Yakobo ne Kefa ne Yohane a bebu bɛ sɛ asafo he akannifoɔ he huu adom a manya he, bɛde bɛ nsa nifa too me ne Barnaba nsam kyerɛɛ ayɔnkofa a ɔda yɛ ntam, na bɛpenee so sɛ yɛnkɔ Amanamane mufoɔ bɛ mu na bɛ nso bɛnkɔ twetiatwafoɔ he mu. 10 Adeɛ baako pɛ ne bɛsrɛeɛ, sɛ yɛbɛkae ahiafoɔ, adeɛ a na ɔda m’akoma so sɛ mɛyɔ.
Paulo kaa Petro (Kefa) anim Antiokia
11Nso berɛ a Kefa baa Antiokia he, mekaa n’anim, efiri sɛ, na ɔno ankasa abu ne ho fɔ dada. 12 Ɔne sɛ, na ɔne Amanamane mufoɔ to nsa didi kosi berɛ a binom firi Yakobo nkyɛne baeɛ. Na afei bɛbaeɛ he, ɔtwee ne ho firii Amanamane mufoɔ he ho, efiri sɛ, na osuro bɛ a befiri twetiatwafoɔ mu baeɛ he. 13Na Yudafoɔ a bɛaka he nso faa saa nyaatwom kwane he so yɔɔ saa, ɔnam saa so maa bɛde saa nyaatwom he twee Barnaba mpo kɔɔ bɛ afa. 14Nso berɛ a mehuu sɛ bɛ adwene nsi bɛ pi sɛnea asɛmpa he nɔkorɛ teɛ he, meka kyerɛɛ Kefa bɛ nyinaa bɛ anim sɛ, “Sɛ wo a, mpo woyɛ Yudani, wobɔ wo bra sɛ Amanamane muni na ɔnyɛ sɛ Yudani a, mmɛ nti ne wopɛ sɛ wohyɛ Amanamane mufoɔ sɛ bɛmmɔ bɛ bra sɛ Yudafoɔ?”
Gyidie ne bɛde bɛgye Yudafoɔ ne Amanamane mufoɔ
15Yɛ ankasa yefiri yɛ awusuo mu yɛ Yudafoɔ, na yɛnyɛ Amanamane mufoɔ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ; 16 nso yenim sɛ ɔnyɛ mmara nnwuma so ne bɛbɛfa abu bi bem, na mmom ɔnam Yesu Kristo mu gyidie so. Na yɛabɛgye Kristo Yesu adi, sɛnea ɔbɛyɔ a bɛnam Kristo mu gyidie so bebu yɛ bem, na mmom ɔnnam mmara nnwuma a yɛbɛyɔ so, efiri sɛ, biaa nni hɔ a bɛnam mmara nnwuma he so bebu no bem. 17Nso sɛ berɛ a yɛbɔ mmɔdene sɛ bebebu yɛ bem Kristo mu he, sɛ behu nso sɛ yɛyɛ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ a, na Kristo yɛ bɔne akoa anaa? Daabi da, ɔnte saa koraa! 18Nso sɛ mesane kɔfa nnoɔma a madwiri agu he ba sɛ afei deɛ ɔyɛ a, hedeɛ na meyɛ me ho sɛ mmaratoni. 19 Na menam mmara he so wu maa mmara he, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a mɛtena ase ama Nyankopɔn. Bɛabɔ me ne Kristo asennua mu; 20 na ɔnyɛ me ne mete ase bio, na mmom ɔyɛ Kristo ne te me mu, nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ m’ase a mete no honam mu seesei yi, mete no Nyankopɔn Ba a ɔdɔ me na ɔde ne ho maa me he mu gyidie mu. 21Memma Nyankopɔn adom he nyɛ kwa; na sɛ bembuo nam mmara so ne ba a, hedeɛ na Kristo awu kwa.

english_text:
Faith Is the Only Way
1You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you? 2I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him? 3How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you? 4Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing? 5God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
6 The Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith. 7 And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham. 8 Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him. 9This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
10 Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.” 11 No one can please God by obeying the Law. The Scriptures also say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.”
12 The Law isn't based on faith. It promises life only to people who obey its commands. 13 But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse. 14And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit.
The Law and the Promise
15My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement. 16That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ. 17 What I am saying is that the Law cannot change or cancel God's promise made 430 years before the Law was given. 18 If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
19What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people. 20There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him.
Slaves and Children
21Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law. 22But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
23The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith. 24In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God. 25But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
26All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus. 27And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes. 28Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman. 29 So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
translation_text:
Mmara anaa Gyidie
1O, Galatifoɔ a hoyɛ adwenharefoɔ! Hwane ne adaadaa ho saa. Ho ampa ara a bɛde Yesu Kristo a bɛbɔɔ no asennuam agyina ho anim akyerɛ pefee paa di! 2Adeɛ baako pɛ ne mepɛ sɛ mehu firi ho hɔ, ɔne sɛ: ɔnam honam mmara nwuma so ne honyaa Honhom he anaa ɔnam asɛm a hoteeɛ na hogye diiɛ so? 3Nti honnwene koraa saa? Hode Honhom hyɛɛ aseɛ, afei deɛ hode honam ne oowie? 4Nti hohuu saa amaneɛ yi kwa anaa? Mennye nni sɛ ɔte saa. 5Hedeɛ ɔnam mmara nnwuma a hoyɔeɛ so ne Nyankopɔn maa ho Honhom he na ɔyɔɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne ho mu, anaa ɔnam asɛm a hoteeɛ na hogye diiɛ so?
6 Sɛnea Abraham “gyee Nyankopɔn diiɛ, na obuu ne gyidie he sɛ tenenee maa no he,” 7saa ara nso ne ɔsɛ sɛ hohu sɛ bɛ a bɛgye di he bɛ mmom ne bɛyɛ Abraham asefoɔ. 8 Na ɔnam sɛ twerɛsɛm he hu sieeɛ sɛ ɔnam gyidie so ne Nyankopɔn bebu Amanamane mufoɔ bem nti, ɔkaa asɛmpa he ho asɛm kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ, “Wo mu ne bebehyira Amanamane mufoɔ nyinaa,” ansa na asɛmpa he ɔɔba. 9 Ne saa nti, ohyira bɛ a bɛgye di nyinaa, sɛnea ohyiraa Abraham he.
10 Biaa a ɔde ne ho to mmara nnwuma he so he hyɛ nnome ase; efiri sɛ bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Nnomee ne nipa biaa a onni nea bɛatwerɛ no mmara nwoma mu he nyinaa so na ɔmfa nni dwuma.” 11 Afei ɔda adi pefee sɛ biaa nni hɔ a ɔnam mmara he so bedi bem Nyankopɔn anim; efiri sɛ, “Nea ɔtene he nam gyidie so ne benya nkwa.” 12Nso mmara he mfiri gyidie mu; na mmom, “Biaa a ɔyɔ mmara nnwuma he nam mmara he so ne bɔ ne bra.” 13 Kristo bɛyɛɛ abusudeɛ maa yɛ, na ɔnam saa so gyee yɛ firii mmara nnomee he ase, efiri sɛ bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Bɛadome biaa a ɔsɛne dua so”, 14sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a Abraham nhyira bɛba Amanamane mufoɔ so Kristo Yesu mu, nti yɛnam gyidie so benya Honhom a bɛde ahyɛ yɛ bɔ he.
Bɔ a bɛhyɛɛ Abraham he
15Anuanom, mfantoho a ɔfa daa asetena ho ne mepɛ sɛ mede ma ho sɛ: Nipa nsamannseɛ a bɛde nsa ahyɛ aseɛ mpo, biaa ntumi nsesa no, na biaa ntumi mfa bi nka ho nso. 16 Na bɛde bɔhyɛ he maa Abraham ne n’aseni; twerɛsɛm he nka sɛ, “Ɔne n’asefoɔ,” te sɛ nea bɛyɛ bebree; na mmom ɔka sɛ, “Ɔne w’aseni,” nti, n’aseni he yɛ nipa baako, na ɔno ne Kristo. 17 M’asɛm a meeka ne sɛ: Mmara a ɔbaa mfie ahannan-aduasa, bɔhyɛ he akyi he, ntumi nsɛe apam a na Nyankopɔn ahyehyɛ ato hɔ dadaada a odi mu he, mma bɔhyɛ he nyɛ kwa. 18Na sɛ agyapadeɛ firi mmara he mu a, na omfiri bɔhyɛ mu bio; nso Nyankopɔn nam bɔhyɛ so ne de agyapadeɛ he maa Abraham.
Mmara he botaeɛ
19 Hedeɛ na mmara he botaeɛ ne sɛ? Mmaratoɔ nti ne bɛde mmara he kaa apam he ho, kosi mmerɛ a Abraham aseni a na bɛahyɛ ne ho bɔ he bɛba; na abɔfoɔ nam ntamgyinafoɔ bi so hyɛɛ mu kena. 20Afei ɔnam sɛ Nyankopɔn yɛ baako nti, na onhia ntamgyinafoɔ biaa, efiri sɛ ntamgyinafoɔ gyina mu ma afa mmienu.
21 Nti mmara he ko tia Nyankopɔn bɔhyɛ he anaa? Daabi da! Na sɛ bɛde mmara bi ama a otumi ma nkwa a, hedeɛ nka tenenee befiri mmara mu ne bɛba. 22 Nso twerɛsɛm he da no adi sɛ nnoɔma nyinaa hyɛ bɔne tumi ase, na ama bɔ a bɛnam Yesu Kristo mu gyidie so ahyɛ he aba bɛ a bɛgye di he so.
23 Afei ansa na gyidie ɔɔbɛba he, mmara he de yɛ too afiase wɛnee yɛ kosii sɛ beyii gyidie adi. 24Nti mmara he bɛyɛɛ kwankyerɛfoɔ kosii sɛ Kristo baeɛ, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛnam gyidie so bebu yɛ bem. 25 Na afei a Kristo mu gyidie mmara aba yi, yɛnhyɛ kwankyerɛfoɔ he ase bio, 26 efiri sɛ, yɛnam gyidie so yɛ Nyankopɔn mma Kristo Yesu mu. 27 Na ho mu dodoɔ a bɛbɔɔ ho asu kɔɔ Kristo mu he, hoahyɛ Kristo (te sɛ ataadeɛ). 28 Nsonsonoeɛ nni Yudani anaa Helani, akoa anaa dehyeɛ, baa anaa beema ntam bio, efiri sɛ, ho nyinaa yɛ baako Kristo mu. 29Sɛ hoya Kristo afa a, hedeɛ hoyɛ Abraham asefoɔ, honam bɔhyɛ so yɛ adedifoɔ.

english_text:
1Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs. 2This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set. 3This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
4But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law, 5 so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children. 6 Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father. 7You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised.
Paul's Concern for the Galatians
8Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real. 9But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers? 10You even celebrate certain days, months, seasons, and years. 11I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
12My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me 13when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick. 14My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself. 15Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me. 16Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
17Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention. 18It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you. 19My children, I am in terrible pain until Christ may be seen living in you. 20I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled.
Hagar and Sarah
21Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says? 22 In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free. 23The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
24All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave. 25Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
26 But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave. 27 The Scriptures say about her,
“You have never had children,
but now you can be glad.
You have never given birth,
but now you can shout.
Once you had no children,
but now you will have
more children than a woman
who has been married
for a long time.”
28My friends, you were born because of this promise, just as Isaac was. 29 But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today. 30 The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.” 31My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
translation_text:
Yɛnam Kristo mu gyidie so yɛ Nyankopɔn mma
1M’asɛm ne sɛ: mmerɛ tentene a adedifoɔ he yɛ abɔfra he, ewom sɛ agyapadeɛ he nyinaa yɛ ne dea, nso nsonsonoeɛ biaa nna ɔne akoa ntam; 2nso ɔbɛkɔ so ahyɛ wɛmfoɔ ne hwɛsofoɔ ase kosi da a ne papa he ahyɛ ato hɔ sɛ abɔfra he ɔntena wɛmfoɔ ne hwɛsofoɔ he nane ase bio. 3 Saa ara ne yɛ nso yɛteɛ; berɛ a na yɛyɛ mmɔfra he, ahonhom a bɛyɛ wiase mfitiasedeɛ he faa yɛ nkoa. 4 Nso berɛ a bɛahyɛ ato hɔ he soeɛ he, Nyankopɔn somaa ne Ba a baa woo no, na bɛwoo no mmara he ase, 5 sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a ɔbɛgye bɛ a na bɛya mmara ase, na ama wafa yɛ ayɛ Nyankopɔn mma. 6 Na esiane sɛ hoyɛ mma nti, Nyankopɔn asoma ne Ba Honhom aba ho akomam a ɔteaam sɛ, “Abba, a ɔkyerɛ sɛ Agya!” 7 Nti honyɛ nkoa bio na mmom hoyɛ mma, na sɛ hoyɛ mma a, honam Nyankopɔn so yɛ adedifoɔ.
Paulo twii Galatifoɔ he anim
8Kane he a na honnim Nyankopɔn he, hosomm ahonhom a bɛnyɛ Nyame kann he sɛ nkoa. 9Na afei a hoahu Nyankopɔn yi anaa Nyankopɔn ahu ho yi, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne hoasane akɔ mfitiasedeɛ a benni tumi biaa, na benni hwee nso de ma he nkyɛne bio? 10 Hodi nna ne bosome, mmerɛ ne mfie ho nhyehyɛeɛ so dendenndene. 11 Mesuro ma ho sɛ ebia adwuma a mayɔ ama ho he ayɛ kwa.
12 Anuanom, honyɛ sɛ me, efiri sɛ me nso maayɛ sɛ ho. Honnyɔɔ me bɔne biaa. 13 Honim sɛ da a medii kane kaa asɛmpa he kyerɛɛ ho he, na meyare; 14ewom sɛ me tebea he yɛ nsɔhwɛ ma ho deɛ, nso hoammu me animtia, na hoampo me, na mmom hogyee me sɛ Nyankopɔn bɔfoɔ, sɛ nea hogyee Kristo Yesu ankasa he. 15Ho anigyeɛ a honyaeɛ he faa he? Na medi adanseɛ sɛ, sɛ na hobetumi mpo a, nka hotutuu ho ani maa me. 16 Ɔnam nɔkorɛ a meka kyerɛɛ ho nti mabɛyɛ ho tamfoɔ? 17 Ɔnyɛ adwenepa ne anuanom atorɔfoɔ he de yɔɔ adwuma-dene ya ho mu; na mmom bɛpɛ sɛ bɛtete me ne ho ntam, na hoapere ahwehwɛ bɛ mmom. 18Sɛ ɔyɛ bibi pa ntia, ɔyɛ sɛ hobɔ ho ho mmɔdene berɛ nyinaa, na mmom ɔnyɛ sɛ ebia meya ho nkyɛne nkoaa ne hobɔ ho ho mmɔdene. 19 Me mma nkumaa a meko ho awoɔ bio sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a Kristo su bewie pɛyɛ ho mu, 20mepɛ sɛ nka saa mmerɛ yi meya ho nkyɛne na metumi fa kwane foforɔ so ne ho kasa, efiri sɛ, ho asɛm he ha me paa.
Hagar ne Sara mfantoho
21 Honyi me ano, ho a hopɛ sɛ hode ho ho hyɛ mmara ase he, honte nea mmara he ka? 22 Na bɛatwerɛ sɛ Abraham woo mmeema mmienu, baako yɛ afenaa ba na baako he nso yɛ dehyeɛ ba. 23 Nea na ɔyɛ afenaa ba he, ɔnam honam kwane so ne bɛwoo no; na nea ɔka ho he deɛ, dehyeɛ ba he, ɔnam bɔhyɛ so ne bɛwoo no. 24 Afei yei yɛ mfantoho: saa mmaa mmienu yi gyina hɔ ma apam mmienu. Nɔkwasɛm, baa he mu baako a ɔno ne Hagar a ofiri Sinai Bepɔ so, woo mma guu nkoasom mu. 25Afei Hagar gyina hɔ ma Sinai Bepɔ a ɔya Arabia; na ɔno ne asaase yi so Yerusalem nsɛso, na ɔne ne mma ya nkoasom mu. 26Nso Yerusalem a ɔya soro he deɛ, ɔde ne ho, na ɔno ne yɛ na, 27 efiri sɛ, bɛatwerɛ sɛ,
“Di ahurusie, wo bonyini a wonni ba, wo a wanwo mma,
to nnwom na teateam, wo a woanko awoɔ
na onnibie mma bɛdɔɔso
akyɛne nea waware mma.”
28 Afei anuanom, hoyɛ bɔhyɛ he mma, te sɛ Isak. 29 Na sɛnea saa berɛ he abɔfra a bɛnam honam kwane so woo no he sɔre tiaa abɔfra a bɛnam Honhom so woo no he, saa ne ɔteɛ nnɛ nso. 30 Nso mmɛ ne twerɛsɛm he ka? “Pamo afenaa he ne ne ba he; na afenaa ba he ne dehyeɛ ba he nkyɛ agyapadeɛ.” 31 Ne saa nti, anuanom, yɛnyɛ afenaa mma, na mmom yɛyɛ dehyeɛ mma.

english_text:
Christ Gives Freedom
1Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
2I, Paul, promise you that Christ won't do you any good if you get circumcised. 3If you do, you must obey the whole Law. 4And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace. 5But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ. 6If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
7You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth. 8And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you. 9 A little yeast can change a whole batch of dough, 10but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
11My friends, if I still preach that people need to be circumcised, why am I in so much trouble? The message about the cross would no longer be a problem, if I told people to be circumcised. 12I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
13My friends, you were chosen to be free. So don't use your freedom as an excuse to do anything you want. Use it as an opportunity to serve each other with love. 14 All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself. 15But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves.
God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
16If you are guided by the Spirit, you won't obey your selfish desires. 17 The Spirit and your desires are enemies of each other. They are always fighting each other and keeping you from doing what you feel you should. 18But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
19People's desires make them give in to immoral ways, filthy thoughts, and shameful deeds. 20They worship idols, practice witchcraft, hate others, and are hard to get along with. People become jealous, angry, and selfish. They not only argue and cause trouble, but they are 21envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
22God's Spirit makes us loving, happy, peaceful, patient, kind, good, faithful, 23gentle, and self-controlled. There is no law against behaving in any of these ways. 24And because we belong to Christ Jesus, we have killed our selfish feelings and desires. 25God's Spirit has given us life, and so we should follow the Spirit. 26But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
translation_text:
Mmara si Kristo mu nkwagyeɛ kwane
1 Adehyedie nti ne Kristo ama yɛade yɛ ho. Nti, honnyina pintinn, hommfa ho ho nhyɛ nkoasom konnua ase bio.
Sɛnea akristofoɔ ahofadie teɛ
2Hontie! Me, Paulo, ne meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, sɛ homa betwa ho twetia a, Kristo ho ɔmma mfasoɔ mma ho. 3Medi adanseɛ bio sɛ, ɔsɛ sɛ nea ɔma betwa no twetia de hwehwɛ Nyankopɔn anim bembuo he di mmara a aka he nyinaa nso so. 4Wo a wopɛ sɛ bɛde mmara bu wo bem he woate wo ho afiri Kristo ho; woahwe ase afiri adom ho. 5Na yɛ deɛ, yɛnam Honhom he so ɔɔtwɛne tenenee anidasoɔ a ogyina gyidie so he dendenndene. 6 Na Kristo Yesu mu he, gyidie a ɔnam dɔ so yɔ adwuma nkoaa ne hia, na ɔnyɛ twetia bɛatwa bi anaa bentwaa no he ne hia.
Kasaa tiaa a odi akyire
7Na hootu mmirika he yie; na afei deɛ hwane ne sii ho kwane sɛ honntie nɔkorɛ he? 8 Saa adwene-danee he mfiri nea ɔfrɛɛ ho he. 9 Mmɔreka ketewa bi ne sɛe mmɔre mũ he nyinaa. 10 Mewɔ ho mu awerɛhyemu paa Awurade mu sɛ honnwene adwene foforɔ biaa. Nso nea ɔɔma ho adwene ayɔ ho ntanta he benya n’asotwee. 11 Na me deɛ, anuanom, sɛ meda so ka twetiatwa ho asɛm a nka mmɛ nti ne bɛda so taa me? Sɛ nka meda so ka twetiatwa ho asɛm a, hedeɛ na nka asennua he nto sintidua bio. 12 Ɔkaa me nkoaa a, nka bɛ a bɛmaa ho hweteeɛ he dii bɛ ho awu!
Dɔ si ahofadie ho kwane
13 Na adehyedie mu ne bɛafrɛ ho aba, me nuanom; homma ahofadie he nyɛ akwannya mma honnyɔ bɔne, na mmom ho mfa dɔ so nyɛ nkoa ma ho ho, 14 efiri sɛ, mmara he nyinaa wie pɛyɛ asɛm baako mu sɛ, “Dɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.” 15Na mmom, sɛ ho keka na homemene ho ho a, honhwɛ yie na hoansɛe ho ho.
Honam nnwuma
16 Hontena Honhom mu, nea meeka ne sɛ, honnnyɔ nea honam pɛ. 17 Na nea honam pɛ he ko tia Honhom he, na nea Honhom he nso pɛ he ko tia honam he; efiri sɛ saa nnoɔma he ko tia ne yɔnko, sɛ obesi ho kwane na hoanyɔ nea hopɛ. 18Na mmom, sɛ Honhom he di ho anim a, honhyɛ mmara ase. 19 Afei honam nnwuma he da adi pefee, yeinom ne: adwamammɔ, fi, akohwie, 20abosonsom, adutoɔ, tane, akayɔ, ahoɔyeyaa, abufuo, atutupɛ, kunsumkunsum, mpaepaemu, 21 anibereɛ, nsaboro, agobɔne ne nnoɔma a ɔtete saa. Meebɔ ho kɔkɔ, sɛnea madi kane abɔ ho kɔkɔ he; sɛ bɛ a bɛyɔ saa nnoɔma yi, bennya Nyankopɔn ahennie he.
Honhom aba
22 Nso Honhom aba he deɛ, ɔno ne dɔ, anigyeɛ, asomdwie, abodwokyɛre, ayamyɛ, papayɔ, gyidie, 23 dwoɔ, ne anidahɔ. Mmara biaa nni hɔ a otia yeinom. 24 Na bɛ a bɛyɛ Kristo Yesu deɛ he, bɛabɔ honam ne n’akɔnnɔ asennua mu. 25 Sɛ yɛte ase Honhom mu a, homma Honhom he nkyerɛ yɛ kwane. 26 Hommma yɛmpɛ animuonyam hunu, nhyehyɛ yɛ ho yɛ ho abufuo, na hommma yɛ ani mmereberɛ yɛ ho.

english_text:
Help Each Other
1My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself. 2You obey the law of Christ when you offer each other a helping hand.
3If you think you are better than others, when you really aren't, you are wrong. 4Do your own work well, and then you will have something to be proud of. But don't compare yourself with others. 5We each must carry our own load.
6Share every good thing you have with anyone who teaches you what God has said.
7You cannot fool God, so don't make a fool of yourself! You will harvest what you plant. 8If you follow your selfish desires, you will harvest destruction, but if you follow the Spirit, you will harvest eternal life. 9Don't get tired of helping others. You will be rewarded when the time is right, if you don't give up. 10We should help people whenever we can, especially if they are followers of the Lord.
Final Warnings
11You can see what big letters I make when I write with my own hand.
12Those people who are telling you to get circumcised are only trying to show how important they are. And they don't want to get into trouble for preaching about the cross of Christ. 13They are circumcised, but they don't obey the Law of Moses. All they want is to brag about having you circumcised. 14But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of his cross, the world is dead as far as I am concerned, and I am dead as far as the world is concerned.
15It doesn't matter if you are circumcised or not. All that matters is that you are a new person.
16If you follow this rule, you will belong to God's true people. God will treat you with undeserved kindness and will bless you with peace.
17 On my own body are scars that prove I belong to Christ Jesus. So I don't want anyone to bother me anymore.
18My friends, I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you! Amen.
translation_text:
Hommoaboa ho ho ho ho
1 Anuanom, sɛ hohu sɛ bi afom a, ho a hoyɛ honhom mufoɔ he mfa akoma a ɔdwo mfa saa nipa he nsi yie. Honhwɛ ho ho yie sɛ ho nso bɛnnsɔ ho nhwɛ. 2Hommoaboa ho ho ho ho mma biaa ntumi nsoa ne nnesoa, na honam saa kwane he so awie Kristo mmara he so di. 3Na sɛ bɛ a benni mu bu bɛ ho sɛ bedi mu a, bɛdaada bɛ ho. 4 Ɔsɛ sɛ biaa sɔ n’ankasa n’adwuma hwɛ. Na adwuma a biaa ayɔ he ne ɔde bɛhoahoa ne ho, na ɔnyɛ deɛ bi foforɔ ayɔ, 5 efiri sɛ ɔsɛ sɛ biaa soa n’ankasa ne nnesoa.
6Ɔsɛ sɛ bɛ a bɛakyerɛkyerɛ bɛ asɛm he, bɛ ne bɛ akyerɛkyerɛfoɔ he kyɛ nnepa nyinaa.
7Honnnaadaa ho ho, Nyankopɔn bensi no atwetwe, efiri sɛ, nea hobedua heaa ne hobɛtwa. 8 Sɛ hodua ma honam a, hobefiri honam mu atwa porɔeɛ; nso sɛ hodua no Honhom mu a, honam Honhom he so betwa daa nkwa. 9 Nti hommma yɛmmerɛ papayɔ mu, na sɛ yɛampa aba a, yɛbɛtwa so aba ne berɛ mu. 10 Ne saa nti, akwannya biaa a yebenya he, homma yɛnyɔ nnipa nyinaa papa, ne titire bɛ a bɛka agyidifoɔ abusua ho he.
Afutuo a otwatoɔ ne nhyira
11Hoahu ntwerɛeɛ akɛseɛ a meaa mede me nsa atwerɛ yi! 12 Ɔyɛ bɛ a bɛpɛ sɛ bɛ anim bɛba nyam honam mu he ne bɛhyɛ ho sɛ hontwa twetia, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛnntaa bɛ Kristo asennua he ho. 13Twetiatwafoɔ he mpo nni mmara he so, nso bɛpɛ sɛ hotwa twetia na bɛagyina so ahoahoa bɛ ho. 14 Menhoahoa me ho bibiaa ho ka yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo asennua a ɔnam so abɔ wiase asennua mu ama me, na me nso bɛabɔ me asennua mu ama wiase. 15 Ɔnyɛ twetia a bɛatwa bi, anaa twetia a bentwa bi ne hia, na mmom sɛ bi bɛyɛ abɔdeɛ foforɔ ne hia! 16 Na bɛ a bɛbɛfa saa nhyehyɛeɛ yi so he, asomdwie, ne mmɔborɔhunu, mmra bɛ ne Nyankopɔn mane Israel so.
17 Ofiri nnɛ ɔɔkɔ yi, mma biaa nhyɛ me brɛ; efiri sɛ mewɔ Yesu ahyɛnsodeɛ me nipadua mu.
18Anuanom, yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo adom nka ho honhom. Amen.

english_text:
1 From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus.
To God's people who live in Ephesus and are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
2I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
Christ Brings Spiritual Blessings
3Praise the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ for the spiritual blessings that Christ has brought us from heaven! 4Before the world was created, God had Christ choose us to live with him and to be his holy and innocent and loving people. 5God was kind and decided that Christ would choose us to be God's own adopted children. 6God treated us with undeserved grace because of the Son he dearly loves, and so we should praise God.
7-8 Christ sacrificed his life's blood to set us free, which means our sins are now forgiven. Christ did this because of God's gift of undeserved grace to us. God has great wisdom and understanding, 9and by what Christ has done, God has shown us his own mysterious ways. 10Then when the time is right, God will do all he has planned, and Christ will bring together everything in heaven and on earth.
11God always does what he plans, and this is why he appointed Christ to choose us. 12He did this so we Jews would bring honor to him and be the first ones to have hope because of him. 13Christ also brought you the truth, which is the good news about how you can be saved. You put your faith in Christ and were given the promised Holy Spirit to show you belong to God. 14The Spirit also makes us sure we will be given what God has stored up for his people. Then we will be set free, and God will be honored and praised.
Paul's Prayer
15I have heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people. 16So I never stop being grateful for you, as I mention you in my prayers. 17I ask the glorious Father and God of our Lord Jesus Christ to give you his Spirit. The Spirit will make you wise and let you understand what it means to know God. 18My prayer is that light will flood your hearts and you will understand the hope given to you when God chose you. Then you will discover the glorious blessings that will be yours together with all God's people.
19I want you to know about the great and mighty power that God has for us followers. It is the same wonderful power he used 20 when he raised Christ from death and let him sit at his right side in heaven. 21There Christ rules over all forces, authorities, powers, and rulers. He rules over all beings in this world and will rule in the future world as well. 22 God has put all things under the power of Christ, and for the good of the church he has made him the head of everything. 23The church is the body of Christ and is filled with Christ who completely fills everything.
translation_text:
Nkyea
1 Paulo a ɔnam Nyankopɔn pɛ so yɛ Kristo Yesu somafoɔ; twerɛ krataa yi de kɔma ahoteefoɔ a bɛya Efeso na bɛyɛ Kristo Yesu mu agyidifoɔ nyinaa: 2Adom ne asomdwie a ofiri yɛ Agya Nyankopɔn ne Awurade Yesu Kristo nka ho.
Honhom mu nhyira a ɔya Kristo mu
3 Nhyira nka Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo Agya, nea ɔde honhom mu nhyira a ɔya soro nyinaa ahyira yɛ Kristo mu, 4 sɛnea oyii yɛ Kristo mu ansa na bɛtoo wiase fapem sɛ yɛbɛyɛ kronkron, na yɛ ho ɔmma asɛm n’anim. 5 Nyankopɔn nam Yesu Kristo so yii yɛ sii hɔ sɛ ne mma, sɛnea ɔpɛ na ɔsɔ n’ani, 6 na ayɛ ayeyie ama animuonyam adom a ɔde nam ne Dɔba he so akyɛ yɛ kwa. 7 Ne mu ne yɛnam ne mogyaa so anya gyeɛ, a ɔne yɛ mfomsoɔ ho fakyɛ, sɛnea n’adom mmorosoɔ teɛ 8a wahwie agu yɛ so. 9 Na waama yɛahu nyansa nyinaa ne nhunumu ahintasɛm a ɔpɛ he, sɛnea osiesiee no Kristo mu he. 10 Yei ne nhyehyɛeɛ a ɔda hɔ sɛ berɛ pa mu he, ɔbɛka nnoɔma a ɔya soro ne nea ɔya asaase so nyinaa abom ne mu.
11 Kristo heaa mu ne wayɔ yɛ adedifoɔ nso, sɛnea nnoɔma nyinaa ne ne pɛ agyinatuo teɛ, bɔɔ ne tim pɔ hyehyɛ too hɔ he, 12sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a yɛ a yedii kane nyaa Kristo mu anidasoɔ he, yɛbɛtena ase ama n’ayeyie animuonyam. 13Na ho nso, berɛ a hotee nɔkorɛ asɛm he, ho nkwagyeɛ ho asɛmpa he, honyaa ne mu anidasoɔ. Ne muaa nso ne berɛ a hogyediiɛ he, bɛde Honhom Kronkron a bɛhyɛɛ ne ho bɔ he hyɛɛ ho nso; 14 yei ne yɛ agyapadeɛ he nyinasoɔ kosi sɛ yɛ nsa bɛka ama yɛayi Nyankopɔn ayɛ animuonyam mu.
Paulo mpaebɔ
15 Maate ho gyidie a howɔ ne Awurade Yesu mu ne ho dɔ a hodɔ ahoteefoɔ he nyinaa, ne saa nti, 16mennyae ho ho aseda a ɔsɛ sɛ meda no me mpaebɔ mu. 17Mebɔ mpaeɛ sɛ yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo Nyankopɔn, animuonyam Agya he, bɛma ho nyansa ne adiyie honhom berɛ a hoahu no yi, 18na ama hoanya nteaseɛ, na hoahu anidasoɔ a ɔfrɛɛ ho baa mu he, nnoɔma a ɔyɛ ma n’animuonyam agyapadeɛ ahonyadeɛ ahoteefoɔ he mu, 19ne ne tumi a mu nni hwehwɛbea ma yɛ a yɛgyedi, sɛnea ne tumi a ɔsõ a ɔyɔ adwuma he teɛ. 20 Ɔyɛ saa tumi yi ne Nyankopɔn de dii dwuma berɛ a ɔnyanee Kristo firii awufoɔ mu na ɔde no tenaa ne nsa nifa so ya soro hɔ, 21 boroo mpanyinnie ne tumidie ne ahoɔdene ne nommumfa nyinaa, ne dini biaa a bɛbɔ, nyɛ wiase yi mu nkoaa na mmom wiase a ɔbɛba he mu nso. 22 Na ɔde nnoɔma nyinaa ahyɛ ne nane ase na waayɔ no nnoɔma nyinaa ti ama asafo a 23 ɔyɛ Kristo nipadua, a nea ɔhyɛ adeɛ nyinaa ma he hyɛ no ma.

english_text:
From Death to Life
1 In the past you were dead because you sinned and fought against God. 2You followed the ways of this world and obeyed the devil. He rules the world, and his spirit has power over everyone who doesn't obey God. 3Once we were also ruled by the selfish desires of our bodies and minds. We had made God angry, and we were going to be punished like everyone else.
4-5But God was merciful! We were dead because of our sins, but God loved us so much he made us alive with Christ, and God's gift of undeserved grace is what saves you. 6God raised us from death to life with Christ Jesus, and he has given us a place beside Christ in heaven. 7God did this so in the future world he could show how truly good and kind he is to us because of what Christ Jesus has done. 8You were saved by faith in God, who treats us much better than we deserve. This is God's gift to you, and not anything you have done on your own. 9It isn't something you have earned, so there is nothing you can brag about. 10God planned for us to do good things and to live as he has always wanted us to live. This is why he sent Christ to make us what we are.
United by Christ
11Don't forget that you are Gentiles. In fact, you used to be called “uncircumcised” by those who take pride in being circumcised. 12At that time you did not know about Christ. You were foreigners to the people of Israel, and you had no part in the promises God had made to them. You were living in this world without hope and without God, 13and you were far from God. But Christ offered his life's blood as a sacrifice and brought you near God.
14Christ has made peace between Jews and Gentiles, and he has united us by breaking down the wall of hatred that separated us. Christ gave his own body 15 to destroy the Law of Moses with all its rules and commands. He even brought Jews and Gentiles together as though we were only one person, when he united us in peace. 16 On the cross Christ did away with our hatred for each other. He also made peace between us and God by uniting Jews and Gentiles in one body. 17 Christ came and preached peace to you Gentiles, who were far from God, and peace to us Jews, who were near God. 18And because of Christ, all of us can come to the Father by the same Spirit.
19You Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens with everyone else who belongs to the family of God. 20You are like a building with the apostles and prophets as the foundation and with Christ as the most important stone. 21Christ is the one who holds the building together and makes it grow into a holy temple for the Lord. 22And you are part of that building Christ has built as a place for God's own Spirit to live.
translation_text:
Hoafiri wuom kɔ nkwa mu
1 Na Nyankopɔn maa ho a na hoawu mfomsoɔ ne bɔne a 2 na hote mu he mu, na hodii wiase yi su akyi, tumidie ne mpanyinnie a ɔya wiem, honhom a nnɛ ɔyɔ adwuma asoɔdenfoɔ mu, he nkwa. 3 Saa ara ne mmerɛ bi a atwam he yɛ nyinaa nso yɛbɔɔ yɛ bra sɛnea yɛ honam akɔnnɔ teɛ, yedii honam ne n’akɔnnɔ akyi, na yɛbɛyɛɛ abufuo mma, yɛ awosuo mu, sɛnea nnipa a aka nyinaa teɛ. 4Nso Nyankopɔn a, ne mmɔborɔhunu dɔɔso he, firi ne dɔ kɛseɛ a ɔde dɔɔ yɛ he mu, 5 ama yɛ a na yɛawu mfomsoɔ mu he ne Kristo nkwa. Ɔnam adom so ne Nyankopɔn agye ho nkwa, 6 na wanyane yɛ ne Kristo na ɔde yɛ atena soro hɔ Kristo Yesu mu, 7 sɛnea ɔbɛyɔ a mmerɛ a ɔɔba he mu ɔnam n’ayamyɛ so bɛda n’adom mmorosoɔ a mu nni hwehwɛbea he adi akyerɛ yɛ Kristo Yesu mu. 8 Na adom ne ama bɛnam gyidie so agye ho nkwa, na omfiri ho ankasa; ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn akyɛdeɛ a 9 omfiri nnwuma mu, sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a bi ntumi mfa nhoahoa ne ho. 10 Nti seesei yɛabɛyɛ abɔdeɛ a Nyankopɔn bɔɔ yɛ Kristo mu maa nnwuma pa a wasiesie ato hɔ dadaada sɛ yɛmma yɛ abrabɔ nyɛ saa he.
Kristo mu baakoyɛ
11 Ne saa nti, ho a na ho awosuo mu he, na hoyɛ amanamane mufoɔ he, honkae sɛ, na “bɛ a bɛatwa bɛ twetia,” honam fam twetiatwa a bɛde nsa atwa, he frɛ ho sɛ “ho a bentwaa ho twetia,” 12 honkae sɛ saa mmerɛ he na honnim Kristo, na hoyɛ amanfrafoɔ na honnyɛ Israel mane mma, na hoyɛ ahɔhoɔ apam he bɔhyɛ he ho, na honni anidasoɔ biaa, afei nso na honnim Nyankopɔn ho wiase abrabɔ mu. 13Nso afei deɛ Kristo Yesu mu, ho a nka na hoya akyirikyiri he, bɛnam Kristo mogyaa so atwe ho abɛne Nyankopɔn. 14 Na ɔyɛ yɛ asomdwie; ɔnam ne honam so aka nnipakuo mmienu he abom ayɛ yɛ baako na wabubu nitane a ɔte sɛ afasuo he afiri hɔ. 15 Kristo Yesu guu mmara he ne n’ahyɛdeɛ he ne ne nsusuansoɔ nyinaa, sɛ ɔnam ne nipadua so bɛbɔ nipadua foforɔ baako asi mmienu he anane mu, na ɔde asomdwie aba, 16na ɔnam asennua he so aka mmienu he abom ayɔ no nipadua baako ama Nyankopɔn, na waayi nitane a na ɔya hɔ he afiri hɔ. 17 Nti ɔbaa sɛ ɔɔbɛka asomdwie ho asɛm akyerɛ ho a na hoya akyirikyiri ne ho a hobɛne Nyankopɔn; 18 na ɔnam ne so ne yɛ nyinaa nam Honhom baako a ɔde yɛ kɔ Agya he hɔ. 19Nti afei honyɛ ahɔhoɔ ne amanfrafoɔ bio, na mmom hoyɛ yɔnko-mane-mma ne Nyankopɔn fiefoɔ, ho ne ahoteefoɔ nyinaa, 20 bɛde ho ato fapem a asomafoɔ ne adiyifoɔ he toeɛ he so a Kristo Yesu he ankasa ne tweasotiboɔ he. 21Ne mu ne nea bɛasi he nyinaa akeka atoa mu na ɔɔpagya akɔyɛ Nyankopɔn asɔredane kronkron; 22 ne mu ne ho nso bɛde ho abom asi hɔ sɛ Nyankopɔn tenabea, honhom mu.

english_text:
Paul's Mission to the Gentiles
1Christ Jesus made me his prisoner, so I could help you Gentiles. 2You have surely heard about God's gift of undeserved grace in choosing me to help you. 3In fact, this letter tells you a little about how God has shown me his mysterious ways. 4 As you read the letter, you will also find out how well I really do understand the mystery about Christ. 5No one knew about this mystery until God's Spirit told it to his holy apostles and prophets. 6And the mystery is this: Because of Christ Jesus, the good news has given the Gentiles a share in the promises God gave to the Jews. God has also let the Gentiles be part of the same body.
7God treated me with kindness. His power worked in me, and it became my job to spread the good news. 8I am the least important of all God's people. But God was kind and chose me to tell the Gentiles that because of Christ there are blessings that cannot be measured. 9 God, who created everything, wanted me to help everyone understand the mysterious plan that had always been hidden in his mind. 10Then God would use the church to show the powers and authorities in the spiritual world that he has many different kinds of wisdom.
11God did this according to his eternal plan. And he was able to do what he had planned because of all Christ Jesus our Lord had done. 12Christ now gives us courage and confidence, so we can come to God by faith. 13This is why you should not be discouraged when I suffer for you. After all, it will bring honor to you.
Christ's Love for Us
14I kneel in prayer to the Father. 15All beings in heaven and on earth receive their life from him. 16God is wonderful and glorious. I pray that his Spirit will make you become strong followers 17and that Christ will live in your hearts because of your faith. Stand firm and be deeply rooted in his love. 18 I pray that you and all God's people will understand what is called wide or long or high or deep. 19I want you to know all about Christ's love, although it is too wonderful to be measured. Then your lives will be filled with all that God is.
20-21I pray that Christ Jesus and the church will forever bring praise to God. His power at work in us can do far more than we dare ask or imagine. Amen.
translation_text:
Paulo adwuma ya Amanamane mufoɔ mu
1 Yei nti ne me Paulo a meyɛ Kristo Yesu deduani ma ho amanamane mufoɔ, 2sɛnea hoate no nɔkorem dada, Nyankopɔn adom a ho nti ɔde maa me, som adwuma he 3 ɔne sɛnea ɔnam adiyie so yii ahintasɛm adi kyerɛɛ me, sɛnea m’adi kane atwerɛ no nsɛm tiaa bi mu he; 4ɔno ne sɛ hokenkane a ɔbɛboa ho ama hoahu nteaseɛ a mewɔ no Kristo ho ahintasɛm mu. 5 Mmerɛ bi a atwa mu he woanyi saa ahintasɛm yi adi ankyerɛ nipa biaa, sɛnea nnɛ ɔnam Honhom he so abɛda no adi akyerɛ n’asomafoɔ ne adiyifoɔ kronkron he: 6 ɔne sɛ, Amanamane mufoɔ he abɛyɛ adedifoɔ te sɛ yɛ, nipadua korɔ heaa ho akwaa, na bɛnam asɛmpa he so anya bɔhyɛ he mu kyɛfa Kristo Yesu mu.
7Mabɛyɛ saa asɛmpa yi ho somfoɔ sɛnea Nyankopɔn adom akyɛdeɛ teɛ, nea ɔde maa me sɛnea ne tumi yɔ adwuma he. 8 Ewom sɛ me ne ahoteefoɔ he nyinaa mu kumaa koraa, nso bɛde adom yi maa me sɛ memfa so nna asɛm a ɔyɛ Kristo ahonya mu a onni hwehwɛbea he adi nkyerɛ amanamane mufoɔ. 9 Na afei nso, waama nnipa nyinaa ahu nhyehyɛeɛ a bɛahyehyɛ a ɔfa ahintasɛm a bɛde asie tete mmerɛ nyinaa Nyankopɔn a ɔbɔɔ adeɛ nyinaa he mu; 10 sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a ɔnam asafo he so bɛda Nyankopɔn nyansa ahodoɔ bebree he adi akyerɛ mpanyinnie ne tumidie a bɛya soro, 11 sɛnea Nyankopɔn timpɔ a wadi ho dwuma yɛ Awurade Kristo Yesu mu he teɛ. 12 Ne mu na yɛnam gyidie so anya nnamyɛ ne akwanya, ahotɔsoɔ mu. 13 Yei nti mesrɛ ho sɛ hompa aba m’amanehunu a mehu ma ho he ho; ɔyɛ animuonyam ma ho.
Mpaeɛ a ɔbɔ maa akenkanfoɔ
14Ne saa nti ne mebu nkotodwe Agya he anim, 15nea befiri ne mu frɛ abusua biaa a ɔya soro ne asaase so he. 16 Mebɔ mpaeɛ sɛ, sɛnea n’ahonya animuonyam teɛ sɛ ɔbɛma ho mu nipadua a ahinta he ase atim tumi a ɔnam ne Honhom so he mu, 17ɔne sɛ Kristo nam gyidie so bɛtena ho akoma mu, sɛdeɛ hoagye nhini na ho ase atim dɔ mu. 18Mebɔ mpaeɛ sɛ hobenya tumi a ɔbɛma ho nteaseɛ, ho ne ahoteefoɔ he nyinaa, nea ɔtrɛ na ɔbae na ɔkrɔnn na mu dɔ, 19na hoahu Kristo dɔ a ɔtra nimdeɛ nyinaa so, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a hobɛyɛ ma Nyankopɔn su nyinaa mu.
20Afei nea ɔwɔ tumi a ɔyɔ adwuma yɛ mu a ɔboro so kyɛne so sɛnea yebisa anaa yesusu he, 21ɔno ne animuonyam nka no asafo he mu ne Kristo Yesu mu firi mmerɛsantene so kosi awoɔ ntoatoasoɔ nyinaa mu, daa daa. Amen.

english_text:
Unity with Christ
1As a prisoner of the Lord, I beg you to live in a way that is worthy of the people God has chosen to be his own. 2 Always be humble and gentle. Patiently put up with each other and love each other. 3Try your best to let God's Spirit keep your hearts united. Do this by living at peace. 4All of you are part of the same body. There is only one Spirit of God, just as you were given one hope when you were chosen to be God's people. 5We have only one Lord, one faith, and one baptism. 6There is one God who is the Father of all people. Not only is God above all others, but he works by using all of us, and he lives in all of us.
7Christ has generously divided out his gifts to us. 8 As the Scriptures say,
“When he went up
to the highest place,
he led away many prisoners
and gave gifts to people.”
9When it says, “he went up,” it means that Christ had been deep in the earth. 10This also means that the one who went deep into the earth is the same one who went into the highest heaven, so he would fill the whole universe.
11Christ chose some of us to be apostles, prophets, missionaries, pastors, and teachers, 12so his people would learn to serve and his body would grow strong. 13This will continue until we are united by our faith and by our understanding of the Son of God. Then we will be mature, just as Christ is, and we will be completely like him.
14We must stop acting like children. We must not let deceitful people trick us by their false teachings, which are like winds that toss us around from place to place. 15Love should always make us tell the truth. Then we will grow in every way and be more like Christ, the head 16 of the body. Christ holds it together and makes all of its parts work perfectly, as it grows and becomes strong because of love.
The Old Life and the New Life
17As a follower of the Lord, I order you to stop living like stupid, godless people. 18Their minds are in the dark, and they are stubborn and ignorant and have missed out on the life that comes from God. They no longer have any feelings about what is right, 19and they are so greedy they do all kinds of indecent things.
20-21But this isn't what you were taught about Jesus Christ. He is the truth, and you heard about him and learned about him. 22 You were told that your foolish desires will destroy you and that you must give up your old way of life with all its bad habits. 23Let the Spirit change your way of thinking 24 and make you into a new person. You were created to be like God, and so you must please him and be truly holy.
Rules for the New Life
25 We are part of the same body. Stop lying and start telling each other the truth. 26 Don't get so angry that you sin. Don't go to bed angry 27and don't give the devil a chance.
28If you are a thief, quit stealing. Be honest and work hard, so you will have something to give to people in need.
29Stop all your dirty talk. Say the right thing at the right time and help others by what you say.
30Don't make God's Spirit sad. The Spirit makes you sure that someday you will be free from your sins.
31Stop being bitter and angry and mad at others. Don't yell at one another or curse each other or ever be rude. 32 Instead, be kind and merciful, and forgive others, just as God forgave you because of Christ.
translation_text:
Baakoyɛ a ɔya Kristo nipadua mu
1 Nti afei me, Awurade mu deduani, mesrɛ ho sɛ hommɔ ho bra sɛnea ɔfata frɛ a bɛafrɛ ho he, 2 ahobrɛaseɛ, dwoɔ, aboterɛ ne abodwokyɛre nyinaa mu, na honnodɔ ho ho ho ho. 3Hommɔ mmɔdene sɛ hobekura Honhom baakoyɛ mu, asomdwie mu. 4 Nipadua baako ne Honhom baako ne ya hɔ, sɛnea bɛafrɛ ho aba anidasoɔ baako mu he, 5 Awurade baako, gyidie baako, asubɔ baako, 6 Nyankopɔn baako a ɔyɛ yɛ nyinaa yɛ Agya, a odi yɛ nyinaa so, na ɔfa yɛ nyinaa mu, na ɔte yɛ nyinaa mu.
7 Nso ɔmaa yɛ mu biaa adom sɛnea Kristo akyɛdeɛ a woasusu teɛ. 8 Nti ɔkaa sɛ:
“Berɛ a ɔforo kɔɔ soro he ɔde bɛ a na bɛafa bɛ nommum a ɔmaa bɛdee bɛ ho he kɔeɛ;
na ɔde akyɛdeɛ maa nnipa.”
9 (Sɛ ɔka sɛ, “Ɔforo kɔɔ soro” a, na ɔkyerɛ sɛ odii kane siane kɔɔ asaase ase ansa. 10Nea ɔsianeeɛ heaa ne foro kɔɔ sorosoro, sɛnea ɔbɛyɔ a ɔbɛma nnoɔma nyinaa awie pɛyɛ.) 11 Akyɛdeɛ a ɔde maa yɛ he bi ne sɛ waama binom ayɛ asomafoɔ, binom nkɔmhyɛfoɔ, binom asɛmpakafoɔ, binom ahwɛfoɔ ne akyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, 12 sɛ obesiesie ahoteefoɔ ama som nnwuma, na ama Kristo nipadua he anyini, 13kosi sɛ yɛ nyinaa beduru Nyankopɔn Ba he ho gyidie baakoyɛ ne nimdeɛ mu, na yɛbɛwie nyini honhom mu, adi mu sɛnea Kristo a ɔyɛ susudua a ɔyɛ pɛ he teɛ; 14 sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a yɛnkɔ so nyɛ te sɛ mmɔfra a bedi akɔneaba, na nkyerɛkyerɛ biaa, te sɛ mframa a ɔnam nnipa mmradɛtoɔ so, ne torɔ ne nnaadaa akwane so, woso bɛ. 15 Na mmom, homma yenni nɔkorɛ, dɔ mu, na yɛnyini akwane nyinaa mu, Kristo a ɔno ne asafo he ti he mu, 16 ne mu ne nipadua he nyinaa akeka atoatoa mu pɛpɛɛpɛ na ɔyɔ adwuma bom ma ɔde nea ohia ma sɛnea akwaa biaa dwumadie teɛ, na ama nipadua he anyini ayɛ dene dɔ mu.
Abrabɔ dadaa ne foforɔ
17 Na afei nea meeka na madi ho adanseɛ Awurade mu ne sɛ ɔnsɛ sɛ hobɔ ho bra bio sɛ amanamane mufoɔ a bɛaka a bɛnante bɛ adwene hunu mu he. 18 Bɛ adwene aduru sum, bɛmmɛne Nyankopɔn nkwa he ho, efiri sɛ, benni ho nimdeɛ ɔnam bɛ akoma pirim nti; 19osiane sɛ bɛ ani nwu adeɛ bio nti, bɛde bɛ ho ama ahohwie, na bɛyɔ afideɛ nyinaa anibereɛ mu. 20Na ho deɛ nyɛ saa ne hosua firii Kristo hɔ! 21Nɔkorem, hoate ne ho asɛm na bɛakyerɛkyerɛ ho ne mu, sɛnea Yesu mu nɔkorɛ teɛ he. 22 Ɔne sɛ, ho kane abrabɔ fam deɛ, honto nipa dada a ɔɔsɛe nnaadaa akɔnnɔ mu he ntwene, 23 na honyɔ foforɔ ho adwene honhom mu, 24 na homfa nipasu foforɔ nhyɛ, tenenee ne kronkronyɛ mapa mu sɛ nnipa a Nyankopɔn abɔ bɛ sɛ ne nsɛso.
Nkwa Foforɔ he ho nhyehyɛeɛ
25 Ne saa nti, honnyi atorɔ mfiri ho akwane mu, homma yɛ nyinaa ni nɔkorɛ mma yɛ yɔnkonom, efiri sɛ yɛyɛ yɛ ho yɛ ho akwaa. 26 Sɛ ho bo fu a, hommma ɔmfa ho nkɔ bɔne mu; hommma awia nkɔtɔ ho abufuo so, 27na hoamma bronsam kwane. 28 Korɔmfoɔ nyae korɔno bɔ; na mmom ɔmmɔ mmɔdene mfa ne nsa nyɔ adwuma pa, na woanya bibi de ama ahiafoɔ. 29 Hommma nsɛmmɔne biaa mfiri ho anum mma, na mmom asɛm a ohia a obetumi ama bi anya nkɔsoɔ nkoaa ne honka, na ama bɛ a bɛte ho nsɛm he anya adom. 30 Na honnho Nyankopɔn Honhom Kronkron he werɛ, nea bɛde no ahyɛ ho nso ama gyeɛ da he. 31 Honto yeyaadie ne kokobɔne ne abufuo ne apereapereɛ ne nnaadaa, ɔne adebɔne biaa ngu, 32 na honyɔ ayamyɛ mma ho ho ho ho, honya ahummɔborɔ, homfa afoforɔ bɔne nkyɛ bɛ, sɛnea Nyankopɔn nam Kristo so de ho bɔne akyɛ ho he.

english_text:
1Do as God does. After all, you are his dear children. 2 Let love be your guide. Christ loved us and offered his life for us as a sacrifice that pleases God.
3You are God's people, so don't let it be said that any of you are immoral or indecent or greedy. 4Don't use dirty or foolish or filthy words. Instead, say how thankful you are. 5Being greedy, indecent, or immoral is just another way of worshiping idols. You can be sure people who behave in this way will never be part of the kingdom that belongs to Christ and to God.
Living as People of Light
6Don't let anyone trick you with foolish talk. God punishes everyone who disobeys him and says foolish things. 7So don't have anything to do with anyone like that.
8You used to be like people living in the dark, but now you are people of the light because you belong to the Lord. So act like people of the light 9and make your light shine. Be good and honest and truthful, 10as you try to please the Lord. 11Don't take part in doing those worthless things that are done in the dark. Instead, show how wrong they are. 12It is disgusting even to talk about what is done in the dark. 13But the light will show what these things are really like. 14Light shows up everything, just as the Scriptures say,
“Wake up from your sleep
and rise from death.
Then Christ will shine on you.”
15Act like people with good sense and not like fools. 16 These are evil times, so make every minute count. 17Don't be stupid. Instead, find out what the Lord wants you to do. 18Don't destroy yourself by getting drunk, but let the Spirit fill your life. 19 When you meet together, sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, as you praise the Lord with all your heart. 20Always use the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to thank God the Father for everything.
Wives and Husbands
21Honor Christ and put others first. 22 A wife should put her husband first, as she does the Lord. 23A husband is the head of his wife, as Christ is the head and the Savior of the church, which is his own body. 24Wives should always put their husbands first, as the church puts Christ first.
25 A husband should love his wife as much as Christ loved the church and gave his life for it. 26He made the church holy by the power of his word, and he made it pure by washing it with water. 27Christ did this, so he would have a glorious and holy church, without faults or spots or wrinkles or any other flaws.
28In the same way, a husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself. A husband who loves his wife shows he loves himself. 29None of us hate our own bodies. We provide for them and take good care of them, just as Christ does for the church, 30because we are each part of his body. 31 As the Scriptures say, “A man leaves his father and mother to get married, and he becomes like one person with his wife.” 32This is a great mystery, but I understand it to mean Christ and his church. 33So each husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself, and each wife should respect her husband.
translation_text:
Honnɔ ho ho ho ho sɛ ho soro Agya dɔ ho he
1 Nti honsua Nyankopɔn, sɛ mma adɔfo, 2 na hontena dɔ mu, sɛnea Kristo dɔɔ yɛ na ɔde ne ho maeɛ sɛ ayɛyɛdeɛ ne afɔrebɔdeɛ a oohwam ma Nyankopɔn, ɔnam yɛ nti.
Honngu abosonsom
3 Nso Adwammammɔ, fi, anaa anibereɛ deɛ, ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛka bata ho dini ho koraa, saa ne fata ahoteefoɔ. 4 Na hommma aniwudeɛ, nsɛnhunu ɔne nsɛnkwaa a kasafi yam mfiri ho anum na mmom, homma nea ofiri ho anum nyɛ aseda. 5 Honhu sɛ, adwamammɔfoɔ anaa nea ɔyɔ nnoɔma a ho nte biaa, anaa aniberefoɔ (meekyerɛ bosomsonni) biaa, nya kyɛfa biaa Kristo ne Nyankopɔn ahennie mu.
6Hommma nnipa biaa mfa nsɛnhunu nnaadaa ho, efiri sɛ saa nnoɔma yi ne de Nyankopɔn abufuo ba asoɔdenfoɔ so. 7Nti hommmfa ho ho mmɔ bɛ. 8 Na kane he na hoyɛ sum, nso afei deɛ hoyɛ hann Awurade mu, nti hommɔ ho bra sɛ hann mma— 9na hann aba da adi papa ne tenenee ne nea ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ nyinaa mu. 10 Hommɔ mmɔdene nhwehwɛ nea ɔsɔ Awurade ani. 11Hommmfa ho ho mmfra sum nnwuma a ɔnso aba biaa mu, na mmom hompa yeinom ho ntoma. 12 Na nnoɔma a behinta yɔ he yɛ aniwuo sɛ bɛbɛbɔ dini mpo; 13 nso hann he pa bibiaa ho ntoma ma no da adi, na nea ɔda ne ho adi biaa yɛ hann. 14 Nti ne Twerɛ he ka sɛ,
“Wo a wada, nyane!
Sɔre firi awufoɔ mu,
na Kristo bɛhyerɛn ama wo.”
15 Honhwɛ sɛnea hobɛbɔ ho bra, honnnyɔ sɛ bɛ a bennim nyansa, na mmom honnyɔ sɛ anyansafoɔ, 16honhwɛ ho mmerɛ so yie, efiri sɛ saa mmerɛ yi yɛ nna bɔne. 17 Nti honnyɛ ho ho sɛ bɛ a bennim nyansa, na mmom honte Awurade pɛ ase. 18 Hommoro nsa, efiri sɛ ɔde basabasa ba; na mmom honhyɛ ma Honhom mu, 19 na homfa sankuo so nnwom ne ayeyie nnwom ne honhom mu nnwom nkasa nkyerɛkyerɛ ho ho ho ho, honto nnwom na hommɔ sankuo ho akoma mu mma Awurade, 20na homfa aseda mma Agya Nyankopɔn daa adeɛ nyinaa mu, yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo dini mu.
Kristofoɔ afisɛm
21 Hommrɛbrɛ ho ho ase mma ho ho, ɔnam anidie a howɔ ma Kristo nti.
22 Ayerenom, hommerɛ ho ho ase mma ho kununom sɛnea hoyɔ ma Awurade. 23 Na kunu yɛ yere ti sɛnea Kristo nso yɛ asafo he ti he, asafo a ɔyɛ ne nipadua na ɔno ankasa nso yɛ asafo he Agyenkwa. 24Sɛnea asafo he brɛ ne ho ase hyɛ Kristo ase he, saa nso ne ɔsɛ sɛ yerɛnom yɔ, adeɛ nyinaa mu, ma bɛ kununom.
25 Kununom, honnɔ ho yerɛnom, sɛnea Kristo nso dɔɔ asafo he na ɔde ne ho too hɔ maa asafo he, 26 sɛ ɔde nsuo adwareɛ bɛte ne ho asɛm he mu, ayɛ no kronkron, 27 na ɔde no asi hɔ ama ne ho animuonyam mu a dɛmdie, nkekaeɛ, anaa bibiaa a ɔte saa nni ne ho, na mmom ayɛ kronkron a onni dɛm biaa. 28Saa kwane he soaa ne ɔsɛ sɛ kununom dɔ bɛ yerɛnom sɛ bɛaa bɛ ho. Beema a ɔdɔ ne yerɛ he dɔ ne ho. 29Na biaa nni hɔ a ɔtane ɔno ankasa ne nipadua, na mmom biaa yɛne ne nipadua na ɔhwɛ so yie, sɛnea Kristo yɔ maa asafo he, 30 efiri sɛ yɛyɛ ne nipadua akwaa. 31 “Saa nti ne beema begya ne papa ne ne maame hɔ na ɔde ne ho akɔbata ne yerɛ, na bɛ mmienu abɛyɛ honam baako.” 32Yei yɛ ahintasɛm kɛseɛ a mede ɔɔtoto Kristo ne asafo he ho. 33Nti ho mu biaa nnɔ ne yerɛ sɛ ɔno ankasa ne ho, na yerɛ nya buo kɛseɛ ma ne kunu.

english_text:
Children and Parents
1 Children, you belong to the Lord, and you do the right thing when you obey your parents. The first commandment with a promise says, 2 “Obey your father and your mother, 3and you will have a long and happy life.”
4 Parents, don't be hard on your children. Raise them properly. Teach them and instruct them about the Lord.
Slaves and Masters
5 Slaves, you must obey your earthly masters. Show them great respect and be as loyal to them as you are to Christ. 6Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. You are slaves of Christ, so with your whole heart you must do what God wants you to do. 7Gladly serve your masters, as though they were the Lord himself, and not simply people. 8You know that you will be rewarded for any good things you do, whether you are slaves or free.
9 Slave owners, you must treat your slaves with this same respect. Don't threaten them. They have the same Master in heaven that you do, and he doesn't have favorites.
The Fight against Evil
10Finally, let the mighty strength of the Lord make you strong. 11 Put on all the armor that God gives, so you can defend yourself against the devil's tricks. 12We are not fighting against humans. We are fighting against forces and authorities and against rulers of darkness and powers in the spiritual world. 13So put on all the armor that God gives. Then when that evil day comes, you will be able to defend yourself. And when the battle is over, you will still be standing firm.
14 Be ready! Let the truth be like a belt around your waist, and let God's justice protect you like armor. 15 Your desire to tell the good news about peace should be like shoes on your feet. 16Let your faith be like a shield, and you will be able to stop all the flaming arrows of the evil one. 17 Let God's saving power be like a helmet, and for a sword use God's message that comes from the Spirit.
18Never stop praying, especially for others. Always pray by the power of the Spirit. Stay alert and keep praying for God's people. 19Pray that I will be given the message to speak and that I may fearlessly explain the mystery about the good news. 20I was sent to do this work, and this is the reason I am in jail. So pray that I will be brave and will speak as I should.
Final Greetings
21-22 I want you to know how I am getting along and what I am doing. This is why I am sending Tychicus to you. He is a dear friend, as well as a faithful servant of the Lord. He will tell you how I am doing, and he will cheer you up.
23I pray that God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will give peace, love, and faith to every follower! 24May God be kind to everyone who keeps on loving our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_text:
Mma ne awofoɔ
1 Mma, honyɔ setie mma ho awofoɔ, Awurade mu na yei ne tene. 2 “Homfa anidie mma ho agya ne ho na” (yei ne mmaransɛm a odi kane a bɔhyɛ bata ho) 3 “na asi ho yie na hoanyini akyɛre asaase yi so.”
4 Na, agyanom, honnhyɛ ho mma abufuo, na mmom hontete bɛ Awurade kasakyerɛ ne nyansakyerɛ mu.
Nkoa ne awuranom
5 Nkoa, homfa suro ne ahopopoɔ nyɔ setie mma ho wuranom a bɛya asaase yi so, homfiri akoma pa mu, sɛnea hoyɔ setie ma Kristo he; 6nyɛ ani so som mu, te sɛ bɛ a bɛpɛ sɛ bɛsɔ nnipa ani, na mmom sɛ Kristo nkoa a hoayi ho yam ɔɔyɔ nea Nyankopɔn pɛ. 7Homfa pɛ pa nsom, sɛ hooyɔ ama Awurade, na mmom ɔnyɛ sɛ hooyɔ ama nnipa. 8 Na honim sɛ papa biaa a yɛyɔ he, nkoa oo, adehyeɛ oo, yebenya ho akatua afiri Awurade hɔ.
9 Saa ne awuranom nso honyɔ mma ho nkoa. Honnyae bɛ ahunahuna, na honhu sɛ ho nyinaa howɔ Wura baako ya soro; na nyiyimu biaa nni ne mu.
Nyankopɔn akodeɛ nyinaa
10 Ne korakora ne sɛ, honyɛ dene Awurade ne n’ahoɔdene tumi mu. 11 Honhyɛ Nyankopɔn akodeɛ nyinaa, na ama hoatumi agyina Bronsam nnaadaa ano; 12 efiri sɛ ɔnyɛ nnipa ne yɛ ne bɛ oodi asie, na mmom yɛ ne mpanyinnie, tumidie, wiase yi sum mu atumfoɔ, ahohommɔne a bɛya soro meamea ne oodi asie. 13Nti homfa Nyankopɔn akodeɛ nyinaa, na hoatumi agyina mmerɛ bɔne he mu, na hoko wie a, hoagyina pintinn. 14 Ne saa nti honnyina hɔ, na homfa nɔkorɛ mmɔ ho asene sɛ abɔsoɔ, na honhyɛ tenenee sɛ nkataboɔ. 15Na homfa asomdwie he ho asɛmpa nhyehyɛ ho nane sɛ mpaboa. 16 Na mmerɛ nyinaa ne adeɛ nyinaa mu he, homfa gyidie kyɛm a hobetumi de adum bɔnefoɔ he pea a ɔdɛre he biaa. 17 Homfa nkwagyeɛ kyɛ, na homfa Honhom mu sekane a ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn asɛm nkita.
18 Homfa mpaebɔ ne nkotosrɛ nyinaa mmɔ mpaeɛ mmerɛ nyinaa Honhom mu. Afei nso, homma ho ani nna hɔ na honkɔ so nsrɛ mma ahoteefoɔ nyinaa. 19 Hommɔ mpaeɛ mma me nso, na sɛ mebie m’ano a, manya akokoɔduro a mede bɛpae mu aka asɛmpa he ho ahintasɛm, 20 na ɔno ho somafoɔ ne meyɛ ya nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn mu yi.
