Na begyinagyina ahwehwɛ po he ho a bekitakita Nyankopɔn nsankuo. 3 Na bɛɛto Nyankopɔn akoa Mose nnwom ne Adwamaa he nnwom sɛ,
Awurade Nyankopɔn, adeɛ nyinaa so tumfoɔ,
wo nnwuma yɛ akɛseɛ na ɔyɛ nwanwa.
O! meresantene hene, w’akwane tene na ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ.
4 Awurade, hwane na oonsuro wo
na ɔɔnhyɛ wo dini animuonyam?
Efiri sɛ wo nkoaa ne woyɛ kronkron,
na amane nyinaa bɛba
abɛkoto w’anim,
efiri sɛ w’atɛne tenenee ada adi.
5 Na yeinom akyi he, mehuu sɛ asɔredane a ɔyɛ adanseɛ ntomadane a ɔya soro he abie. 6 Abɔfoɔ nson a bɛmaa bɛ tumi sɛ bɛmfa haw nson mmra he pie firii asɔredane he mu a na bɛhyehyɛ ntaadeɛ fitaa a sikakɔkɔɔ aboomu bɔ bɛ mu. 7 Afei ateasefoɔ nnan he mu baako maa abɔfoɔ nson he sikakɔkɔɔ nkuruwaa nson a, Nyankopɔn a ɔte ase daa daa he abufuo ahyɛ no ma. 8 Wusie a ofiri Nyankopɔn animuonyam ne ne tumi mu he hyɛɛ asɔredane he ma. Na biaa antumi ankɔ asɔredane he mu kosii sɛ haw nson a abɔfoɔ nson he de baeɛ he to twaeɛ.

english_text:
The Bowls of God's Anger
1From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
2 The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
3The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
4 The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood. 5Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say,
“You have always been,
and you always will be
the holy God.
You had the right
to judge in this way.
6They poured out the blood
of your people
and your prophets.
So you gave them blood
to drink as they deserve!”
7After this, I heard
the altar shout,
“Yes, Lord God All-Powerful,
your judgments are honest
and fair.”
8The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire. 9Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
10 The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain. 11And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
12 The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east. 13An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet. 14These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
15 Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
16 Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
17As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!” 18 There were flashes of lightning, roars of thunder, and the worst earthquake in all history. 19 The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger. 20 Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared. 21 Hailstones, weighing about 50 kilograms each, fell from the sky on people. Finally, the people cursed God, because the hail was so terrible.
translation_text:
Nyankopɔn abufuo nkuruwa nson he
1 Afei metee nne kɛseɛ bi a ofiri asɔredane he mu a ɔka kyerɛɛ abɔfoɔ nson he sɛ, “Kɔ na kohwie Nyankopɔn abufuo nson a ɔya nkuruwaa nson mu he gu asaase so.” 2 Bɔfoɔ a odi kane he kohwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu asaase so. Akuro a ohu na ɔyeya tutuu bɛ a bɛde aboa he ahyɛ bɛ ho agyinaeɛ he ne bɛ a bɛsomm ne sɛso honyini he nso.
3 Bɔfoɔ a ɔto so mmienu he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu po mu. Afei nsuo he danee te sɛ awufoɔ mogyaa, na abɔdeɛ biaa a nkwa yam ya po he mu he wuiɛ.
4 Bɔfoɔ a ɔto so mmeɛnsa he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu nsubɔntene mu maa nsuo he nyinaa danee mogyaa. 5Metee sɛ bɔfoɔ a ɔhwɛ nsuo so he ɔɔka sɛ,
“O Kronkronni a wotene, na woya hɔ, na woya ha firi tetentredee,
w’atɛne a wobuiɛ he yɛ atɛntenenee.
6Na bɛahwie Nyankopɔn ahoteefoɔ ne adiyifoɔ mogyaa agu, nti woama bɛ mogyaa sɛ bɛ nom. Nea ɔfata bɛaa ne beenya yi.”
7 Na metee sɛ nne bi firi afɔrebokyia he mu a ɔdɛ:
Ɛɛ! Awurade Nyankopɔn, adeɛ nyinaa so tumfoɔ,
w’atemmuo yɛ nɔkorɛ ne tenenee.
8Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nnan he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu awia so na bɛmaa awia tumi sɛ ɔmfa ne hyeɛ yaayaa he nhye nnipa. 9 Awia he hyeɛ yaayaa he hyee nnipa ma bɛkaa Nyankopɔn a ɔwɔ saa haw yi nyinaa so tumi he dini he ho abususɛm. Nso bɛannu bɛ ho anhyɛ no animuonyam. 10 Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so num he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu aboa he ahennwa he so. Sum duruu aboa he ahemmane mu, na osiane yeya nti, nnipa wee bɛ kɛtrɛma, 11 na esiane saa yeyaa he ne akuro a otutuu bɛ he nti, bɛdomee soro Nyankopɔn. Nso bɛansakra bɛ adwene amfiri bɛ bɔne ho. 12 Bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nsia he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu asubɔntene Eufrate mu. Nsuo he weweeɛ na ɔdanee kwane maa ahene a befiri awia apieeɛ. 13 Afei mehuu ahohommɔne mmeɛnsa bi a na bɛte sɛ atwerɛ a na befifiri tweaseɛ he ne aboa he ne atorɔ diyifoɔ he anum. 14 Bɛyɛ ahohommɔne a bɛyɔ anwanwadeɛ. Saa ahohom mmeɛnsa yi kɔɔ ahene a bɛya asaase so nyinaa nkyɛne kɔka bɛ nyinaa boa ano ma Tumfoɔ Nyankopɔn da kɛseɛ he mu ko he. 15 (“Hontie! Hwɛ, meeba te sɛ korɔmfoɔ. Nhyira ne nea ɔwɛne na ɔhwɛ ne ntaadeɛ so sɛnea ɔɔnnante adagya, ngu ne ho anim ase badwam!”) 16 Na bɛboaboaa ahene he ano kɔɔ baabi a Hebri kasa mu bɛfrɛ hɔ Armagedon.
17Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nson he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu mframa mu. Na nne dendene bi firi ahennwa a ɔya asɔredane he mu kaa sɛ, “Bɛawie.” 18 Na anyinam tete maa apranaa paepaeɛ, na asaasewosoɔ kɛseɛ bi a ohu paa a ofiri berɛ a nnipa baa asaase so bi nsii saa da, baeɛ. Yei ne asaasewosoɔ nyinaa mu nea ano ɔdene paa. 19 Kuro kɛseɛ he mu paee mmeɛnsa, na amane nyinaa nkuro akɛseɛ sɛesɛee. Nyankopɔn kaee Babel kɛseɛ he maa ɔnom nsa a ano ɔdene he, firii ne kuruwa ano. 20 Nsupɔ ne mmepɔ nyinaa yeraeɛ. 21 Na asukɔtwea akɛseɛ a ɔte sɛ nkariboɔ ha firi soro guguu nnipa so, na asukɔtwea he haw nti, nnipa kaa Nyankopɔn ho abususɛm efiri sɛ na haw he so kɛse paa.

english_text:
The Prostitute and the Beast
1 One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans. 2 Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
3 With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns. 4 The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done. 5On her forehead a mysterious name was written:
I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
6I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me, 7and the angel said:
Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns. 8 The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
9Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings. 10Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
11You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
12 The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time. 13They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast. 14 These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
15The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages. 16The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire. 17God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
18The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_text:
Dwamamfoɔ kɛseɛ he ne aboa he
1 Afei abɔfoɔ nson he mu baako a okita nkuruwaa nson he ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Bra na menkyerɛ wo dwamanfoɔ kɛseɛ a ɔte nsuo pii so he atemmuo. 2 Ɔno ne wiase ahemfo ne no abɔ adwamane, na n’adwamammɔ nsa he aboroboro bɛ a bɛtete asaase so he.” 3 Honhom he faa me maa bɔfoɔ he de me kɔɔ serɛ so. Hɔ ne mehuu baa bi sɛ ɔte aboa kɔkɔɔ bi a abususɛm dini ahyɛ no mã so. Na aboa he wɔ ti nson ne mmɛne du. 4 Na baa he hyɛ ataadeɛ a n’ahosuo yɛ afasebiri ne kɔkɔɔ, afei nso ɔde sikakɔkɔɔ aboɔ ne nhwene pa ahyehyɛ ne ho. Na okita sika kuruwa a afideɛ ne adwamammɔ ho nnoɔma ayɛ no ma tɔ. 5 Na bɛatwerɛ dini bi ya ne moma so a ne nkyerɛaseɛ yɛ ahintasɛm: “Babel kɛseɛ he, asaase so adwamanfoɔ ne akyiwadeɛ na.” 6 Na mehuu sɛ ahoteefoɔ ne Yesu akyidifoɔ a bekuu bɛ he mogyaa aboro baa he.
Mehu no he, ɔyɛɛ me nwanwa paa. 7Bɔfoɔ he bisaa me sɛ, “Mmɛ nti na ɔyɛ wo nwanwa? Mɛkyerɛ wo baa he ne aboa a ɔsõ no a ɔwɔ tire nson ne mmɛne du he ho ahintasɛm he. 8 Aboa a wohuu no he, na ɔte ase nso ɔnte ase bio. Oobefiri amena donkodonko he mu aba ama bɛasɛe no. Nnipa a bɛte asaase so a bɛantwerɛ bɛ dini angu ateasefoɔ nwoma he mu ansa na bɛɛbɔ wiase he, sɛ wohu aboa he a, ɔbɛyɛ bɛ nwanwa, efiri sɛ mmerɛ bi na ɔte asɛ, nso seesei deɛ, ɔnte ase na ɔbɛsane aba.
9 “Yei ho ne nimdeɛ ne nteaseɛ ho hia. Ti nson he gyina hɔ ma mmepɔ nson a baa he tena so he. Bɛyɛ ahemfo nson nso, 10bɛ mu nnum awu, na bɛ mu baako da so di hene, na baako a ɔka bɛ ho he mmaeɛ. Na sɛ ɔba a, ɔsɛ sɛ ɔtwɛne kakra bi. 11 Aboa he a nka na ɔte ase he na afei deɛ ɔnte ase he yɛ hene a ɔto so nwɔtwe a ɔka nson a bedi kane he ho a ɔɔkɔ ne sɛeɛ mu. 12 “Na mmɛne du a wohuiɛ he yɛ ahemfo du a bennyaa ahennie, nso bɛne aboa he benya tumi sɛ ahemfo dɔnhwere baako. 13Yeinom wɔ adwene korɔ, na bɛde bɛ tumi ne bɛ ahoɔdene bɛma aboa he. 14 Bɛbɛko atia Adwamaa he ne n’akyidifoɔ, agyidifoɔ a bɛafrɛ bɛ he ne no bedi bɛ so, efiri sɛ, ɔyɛ Awurade mu Awurade ne Ahemfo mu Hene.”
15 Bɔfoɔ he nso ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Nsuo a wohuiɛ a dwamanfoɔ he te so he yɛ nkuro ne nkurofoɔ akuoakuo ne amane ne kasa ahodoɔ. 16 Mmɛne du ne aboa he a wohuiɛ he bɛtane dwamanfoɔ he. Bɛbɛfa bibiaa a ɔya ne ho ama wada adagya. Bɛbɛwe ne nam na bɛde gya ahye no, 17 efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn de ahyɛ bɛ akoma mu sɛ bɛbɛyɔ adwene korɔ de bɛ ahennie ama aboa he na wadi hene akosi sɛ Nyankopɔn asɛm he bɛba mu. 18 Afei baa a wohuu no he yɛ kuro kɛseɛ a odi asaase so ahemfo so hene he.”

english_text:
The Fall of Babylon
1I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory. 2 The angel shouted,
“Fallen! Powerful Babylon
has fallen
and is now the home
of demons.
It is the den
of every filthy spirit
and of all unclean birds,
and every dirty
and hated animal.
3 Babylon's evil and immoral wine
has made all nations drunk.
Every king on earth
has slept with her,
and every merchant on earth
is rich because of
her evil desires.”
4 Then I heard another voice
from heaven shout,
“My people, you must escape
from Babylon.
Don't take part in her sins
and share her punishment.
5 Her sins are piled
as high as heaven.
God has remembered the evil
she has done.
6 Treat her as she
has treated others.
Make her pay double
for what she has done.
Make her drink twice as much
of what she mixed
for others.
7 That woman honored herself
with a life of luxury.
Reward her now
with suffering and pain.
“Deep in her heart
Babylon said,
‘I am the queen!
Never will I be a widow
or know what it means
to be sad.’
8And so, in a single day
she will suffer the pain
of sorrow, hunger, and death.
Fire will destroy
her dead body,
because her judge
is the powerful Lord God.”
9 Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire. 10Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say,
“Pity that great
and powerful city!
Pity Babylon!
In a single hour
her judgment has come.”
11 Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods. 12 There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble. 13No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
14Babylon, the things
your heart desired
have all escaped
from you.
Every luxury
and all your glory
will be lost forever.
You will never
get them back.
15 The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning. 16Then they shouted,
“Pity the great city
of Babylon!
She dressed in fine linen
and wore purple
and scarlet cloth.
She had jewelry
made of gold
and precious stones
and pearls.
17 Yet in a single hour
her riches disappeared.”
Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea. 18 When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
19 They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said,
“Pity the great city
of Babylon!
Everyone who sailed the seas
became rich
from her treasures.
But in a single hour
the city was destroyed.
20 The heavens should be happy
with God's people
and apostles and prophets.
God has punished her
for them.”
21 A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said,
“This is how the great city
of Babylon
will be thrown down,
never to rise again.
22 The music of harps and singers
and of flutes and trumpets
will no longer be heard.
No workers will ever
set up shop in that city,
and the sound
of grinding grain
will be silenced forever.
23Lamps will no longer shine
anywhere in Babylon,
and couples will never again
say wedding vows there.
Her merchants ruled
the earth,
and by her witchcraft
she fooled all nations.
24 On the streets of Babylon
is found the blood
of God's people
and of his prophets,
and everyone else.”
translation_text:
Babel ahweaseɛ
1 Yei akyi he, mehuu bɔfoɔ foforɔ sɛ ofiri soro ɔɔba fam. Na ɔwɔ tumi kɛseɛ na n’animuonyam hyerɛn asaase so nyinaa. 2 Ɔteaam dendenndene sɛ,
“Wahwe ase! Babel kɛseɛ ahwe ase,
na adane ahonhommɔne tenaberɛ
ne ahonhom fi nyinaa nso atenaeɛ.
Nnomaa fi a bekyiri bɛ nyinaa te ne mu.
3 Efiri sɛ, amanamane nyinaa anom
n’adwamammɔ nsa dendene he bi,
na asaase so ahemfo ne no abɔ adwamane,
na asaase so adwadifoɔ anya bɛ ho ɔnam n’akɔnnɔbɔne ntrasoɔ nti.”
4 Afei metee nne foforɔ firii soro sɛ,
“Me mane, homfiri ne mu,
na hoanyɔ ne nneyɔeɛ bɔne he bi,
na hoannya ne haw he bi;
5 efiri sɛ, ne nneyɔeɛ bɔne atoatoa abeduru soro, na Nyankopɔn kae n’akwammɔne he.
6 Sɛnea ɔyɔɔ ho he, honyɔ no saa pɛpɛɛpɛ; nea ɔyɔɔ ho biaa he, hontua no so ka mprenu. Nsa a ɔde maa ho he, honhyɛ no bi a ano ɔdene mmɔho mmienu.
7 Homma onhu amane na ne werɛ nho ɔnam animuonyam ne n’ahonya a ɔpɛ maa ne ho he nti, efiri sɛ, daa ɔka sɛ, ‘Mete ha sɛ hemaa. Menyɛ kunafoɔ, me werɛ ɔnho da!’
8 Esiane yei nti, n’amanehunu bɛba ne so da bi,
yadeɛ, awerɛhoɔ ne kɔm,
na bɛde gya ahye no;
efiri sɛ Awurade Nyankopɔn a obu no atɛne he yɛ hoɔdenfoɔ.”
9 Sɛ asaase so ahemfo a bɛne no bɔɔ adwamane, dii dɛ he hu ne hyeɛ wusie a, bebesu, atwa agyaadwoɔ. 10 N’amanehunu he hu a ɔyɛ nti, bebegyina akyirikyiri asu sɛ,
“Due! Due! O! kuro kɛseɛ,
O Babel, kuro a ɔwɔ tumi.
Hwɛ! dɔnhwere baako pɛ mu, na w’atemmuo aba.”
11 Adwadifoɔ a bɛya asaase so nso su no efiri sɛ, biaa ntɔ bɛ nnoɔma bio. 12 Biaa ntɔ sikakɔkɔɔ ne dwetɛ, ne aboɔdeneboɔ ne nhwene pa, ne nwera pa ne ntoma afasebiri ne serekye ne ntoma kɔkɔɔ, ne nnua a ohwam nyinaa ne asommɛne adwinnie nyinaa, ne adwinnie a bɛde nnua a ɔwɔ dini ayɔ, ne yaawa ne dadeɛ ne abohemmaa, 13ne pɛprɛ ne mmorɔngo, ne nnuhwam ne kurobo, ne hwamfufuo ne nsa ne ngo, ne siam muhumuhu ne ayuo, ne nyɛmmoa bi te sɛ nnwane ne apɔnkɔ ne nteaseɛnam ne nnipadua ne nnipa akra bio.
14Na aduaba a wo kra kɔno dɔ he
afiri wo nkyɛne,
na nnepa ne animuonyanneɛ nyinaa
afiri wo ho ayera, na wonhu bio koraa.”
15Nnoɔma yi ho adwadifoɔ a kuro he nti bɛanya adeɛ he, begyinagyina akyirikyiri n’ayayadeɛ he suro nti, na bɛasu
16 na bɛagyam sɛ, “Due! Due! O! kuro kɛseɛ
a ofira nwera pa ne ntoma afasebiri ne ntoma kɔkɔɔ
na ɔde sika ne aboɔdenboɔ ne nhwene pa abobɔ mu,
17 efiri sɛ, dɔnhwere pɛ mu, na saa ahonya dodoɔ yi asa.”
Nsuomhyɛnkafoɔ, ne hyɛne he mu nnipa, ne hyɛne he mu adwadifoɔ, ne bɛ a bɛka ho a benya bɛ ano aduane firi hyɛne adwumayɔ mu he nyinaa gyinaa akyirikyiri 18 na behuu ne hyeɛ wusie he, bɛteaam sɛ:
Abɛ kuro ne te sɛ kuro kɛseɛ yi?”
19 Na bɛde mfuturo guu bɛ atifi, na bɛde sũ ne gyam teaa mu sɛ:
Due! Due! Kuro kɛseɛ yi
a n’aboɔdenneɛ nti bɛ a bɛya hyɛne mu ya po so nyinaa
anyinya bɛ ho ya mu,
hwɛ! Dɔnhwere baako pɛ mu na bɛayɛ no pasaa. 20 Soro ne ho ahoteefoɔ ne asomafoɔ ne adiyifoɔ,
ɔno nti homma ho ani nnye ya ne ho,
efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn abu no atɛne ama ho.
21 Afei bɔfoɔ hoɔdenfoɔ bi faa boɔ a ne kɛseɛ bɛyɛ sɛ ayuyammoɔ to twenee po mu kaa sɛ, “Saa ahoɔdene yiaa ne bɛde bɛto Babilon kɛse he atwene fam a bennhu no bio. 22 Bɛnnte sankubɔfoɔ ne nwomtofoɔ, atɛntɛbɛnbɔfoɔ ne totorobɛntohyɛnfoɔ nka bio mfiri ho mu. Bennhu dwumfoɔ biaa ya wo mu bio. Na bɛnte ayuyammoɔ biaa nka ho mu bio.
23 Kanea hann onhyerɛn ya ho mu bio.
Na bɛnnte ayefokunu ne ayeforɔ nne ya ho mu bio. W’adwadifoɔ yɛ wiase mu nnipa titire. Na bɛde wo ntafowayie adaadaa amane nyinaa.
24 Na ɔno mu ne behuu adiyifoɔ ne ahoteefoɔ
ne bɛ a bɛaku bɛ asaase so he nyinaa mogyaa.”

english_text:
1After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting,
“Praise the Lord!
To our God belongs
the glorious power to save,
2 because his judgments
are honest and fair.
That filthy prostitute
ruined the earth
with shameful deeds.
But God has judged her
and made her pay
the price for murdering
his servants.”
3 Then the crowd shouted,
“Praise the Lord!
Smoke will never stop rising
from her burning body.”
4After this, the 24 elders and the four living creatures all knelt before the throne of God and worshiped him. They said, “Amen! Praise the Lord!”
The Marriage Supper of the Lamb
5 From the throne a voice said,
“If you worship
and fear our God,
give praise to him,
no matter who you are.”
6 Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying,
“Praise the Lord!
Our Lord God All-Powerful
now rules as king.
7So we will be glad and happy
and give him praise.
The wedding day of the Lamb
is here,
and his bride is ready.
8She will be given
a wedding dress
made of pure
and shining linen.
This linen stands for
the good things
God's people have done.”
9 Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
10I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.”
The Rider on the White Horse
11 I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war. 12 He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
13 The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.” 14He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen. 15 From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made. 16On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
17 I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast! 18You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
19I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army. 20 But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur. 21But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
translation_text:
Sorofoɔ dii ahurisie
1 Yei akyi he, metee bibi a ɔte sɛ nnipakuo bebree nne firii soro sɛ: “Haleluya!
Nkwagyeɛ ne animuonyam ne tumi yɛ yɛ Nyankopɔn dea
2 efiri sɛ, n’atemmuo yɛ nɔkorɛ na ɔtene
sɛ obuu dwamanfoɔ kɛseɛ
a ɔde n’adwamammɔ asɛe asaase he atɛne,
na ɔtɔɔ ne nkoa mogyaa so werɛ ne nsa mu.”
3 Na afei bɛteaam bio sɛ,
“Haleluya!
Na wusie a ofiri ne mu he kɔ soro daa daa.”
4 Mpanyimfoɔ aduonu nnan ne ateasefoɔ nnan he butubutu Nyankopɔn a ɔte n’ahennwa so he anim somm no. Na bɛteaam sɛ,
“Amen, Haleluya!”
5Afei nne bi firi ahennwa he so baa sɛ,
“Honkamfo yɛ Nyankopɔn,
ho a hoyɛ ne nkoa,
ne ho a hosuro no nyinaa, akɛseɛ ne nkumaa!”
6 Afei metee nne bi te sɛ nnipadɔm nne, ne nsuo nworosoɔ ne apranaa a ɔɔpaepae a ɔɔteam sɛ, “Haleluya! Yɛ Tumfoɔ Awurade Nyankopɔn di hene.
7 Homma yɛ ho nsɛpɛ yɛ na yɛ ani nnye
na yɛnhyɛ no animuonyam, efiri sɛ,
dwammaa he ayefohyia aduru,
na ne yerɛ asiesie ne ho.
8 Bɛde nwera pa a
ani hyerɛn na ani te maa no sɛ ɔnhyɛ.”
Nwera pa he ne ahoteefoɔ tenenee adeyɔ.
9 Afei bɔfoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Twerɛ sɛ, Nhyira nka bɛ a bɛato nsa afrɛ bɛ dwammaa he ayefohyia apontoɔ ase.” Na ɔka kaa ho sɛ, “Yei yɛ Nyankopɔn asɛm turodoo.” 10 Afei mebutuu ne nane ase sɔree no. Nso ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Nyɔ saa, efiri sɛ, meyɛ akoa te sɛ wo ne wo nuanom a bedi Yesu ho adanseɛ heaa, Nyankopɔn mmom ne koto sɔre no! Na Yesu adanseɛ ne yɛ adiyie honhom he.”
Gyefoɔ he ho anisoadehunu kɛseɛ
11 Afei mehuu sɛ soro abie a pɔnkɔ fitaa ya hɔ. Na nea ɔte ne so he dini de “Ɔseadeɛyɔ ne Nɔkwafoɔ,” na tenenee ne ogyina so bu atɛne ko ne ko nso. 12 N’ani te sɛ gya a ɔɔdɛre, na na ɔbɔ ahemmotire bebree. Na bɛatwerɛ ne dini bi a biaa nnim gye sɛ ɔno nkoaa. 13 Na bɛde n’ataadeɛ a ɔhyɛ he anu mogyaa mu. Dini a bɛde frɛ no ne “Nyankopɔn Asɛm.” 14 Soro asraafoɔ a bɛhyehyɛ nwera fitaa a ani te a bɛtete pɔnkɔ fitaa so dii n’akyi. 15 Afei nkrantɛ nnamnnam a ɔde bedi amane so firi n’anum baeɛ. Ɔde dadeɛ poma ne bedi bɛ so. Obetiatia Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ adeɛ nyinaa so tumfoɔ he abufuo a ɔdene he nsa-kyi-amena he so. 16 Na bɛatwerɛ dini “Ahene mu Hene ne awuranom mu Awurade” agu n’ataadeɛ mu ne ne nane ho.
Bedii aboa he ne n’asraafoɔ he so nkunim
17 Afei mehuu bɔfoɔ bi a ogyina awia mu. Ɔteaam guu nnomaa a bɛnam wiem nyinaa so sɛ, “Hommra mmehyiam Nyankopɔn apontoɔ kɛseɛ he ase. 18Hommedi ahemfo nam ne asafohene nam ne atumfoɔ nam ne apɔnkɔ ne bɛ a bɛtete bɛ so nam ne adehyeɛ ne nkoa ne akɛseɛ ne nkumaa nyinaa nam.” 19 Afei mehuu aboa he ne asaase so ahemfo ne bɛ asraafoɔ a bɛaboa bɛ ano ɔɔkɔko atia nea ɔte pɔnkɔ he so he ne ne dɔm. 20 Bɛkyee aboa he ne ne diyifoɔ torɔfoɔ a ɔyɔɔ anwanwadeɛ n’anim he de no too afiase. Saa anwanwadeɛ he so ne ɔnam daadaa bɛ a bɛde aboa he ahyɛ bɛ agyinaeɛ ne bɛ a bɛsom aboa he honyini he. Bɛtoo aboa he ne diyifoɔ torɔfoɔ he anikann guu gya tadeɛ a sɔfe ɔɔdɛre mu he mu. 21Bɛde nkrantɛ a ofiri pɔnkɔkafoɔ he anum kukuu bɛ asraafoɔ, na nnomaa he dii bɛ nam meeɛ.

english_text:
The Thousand Years
1I saw an angel come down from heaven, carrying the key to the deep pit and a big chain. 2 He chained the dragon for 1,000 years. It is that old snake, who is also known as the devil and Satan. 3Then the angel threw the dragon into the pit. He locked and sealed it, so 1,000 years would go by before the dragon could fool the nations again. But after that, it would have to be set free for a little while.
4 I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for 1,000 years.
5-6These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for 1,000 years.
No other dead people were raised to life until 1,000 years later.
Satan Is Defeated
7At the end of the 1,000 years, Satan will be set free. 8 He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach, 9and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army. 10Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever.
The Judgment at the Great White Throne
11 I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go. 12 I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
13The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done. 14Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death. 15Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_text:
Mfie apem he
1 Afei mehuu bɔfoɔ bi sɛ ofiri soro ɔɔba fam a na okita amena donkodonko he ano safoa ne nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn kɛseɛ bi. 2 Ɔkyee wɔ kɛseɛ dadaa a ɔno ne Bronsam ne Satan he kyekyeree no maa odii mfie apem. 3 Bɔfoɔ he too no twenee amena donkodonko he mu too mu safoa bram akyire na wantumi annaadaa amane bio kosi sɛ mfie apem he betwam. Nso mfie apem he akyi he bebegyaa no kakra. 4 Afei mehuu nhennwa bi ne bɛ a bɛtete so a na bɛama bɛ tumi sɛ bemmu atɛne. Na mesane huu bɛ a Yesu adanseɛ ne Nyankopɔn asɛm nti betwitwaa bɛ ti he kra, ne bɛ a bɛankoto aboa he anaa ne honyini na bɛannye agyinaeɛ he ammɔ bɛ moma ne bɛ nsa so he. Na benyaa nkwa bio, na bɛne Kristo dii hene mfie apem. 5 (Na awufoɔ nkaeɛ he deɛ, bɛannya nkwa bio kosii sɛ mfie apem he twaa mu.) Yei ne wusɔreɛ a odi kane he. 6 Nhyira ne bɛ a bɛate bɛ ho ama Awurade a benya kyɛfa sɔreeɛ a odi kane he mu. Saa nkurofoɔ he deɛ, wuprenu he nni bɛ so tumi, na mmom bɛbɛyɛ Nyankopɔn ne Kristo asɔfoɔ, na bɛne no bedi ahene mfie apem. 7Mfie apem he akyi he, bebesane Satan afiri afiase hɔ; 8 na ɔbɛkɔ akɔdaadaa amane a bɛya wiase nyinaa, Gog ne Magog, akɔboaboa nnipa a bɛ dodoɔ te sɛ mpoano anwea de bɛ akɔ sa. 9 Betuu sa faa asaase so nyina kotwaa kuro a Nyankopɔn dɔ no he, na n’ahoteefoɔ tete mu he ho hyiaeɛ. Nso gya firi soro bɛhyee bɛ. 10 Na bɛtoo Bronsam a ɔdaadaa bɛ he too gya ne sɔfe tadeɛ a bɛde aboa he ne diyifoɔ torɔfoɔ he ato mu he mu. Na bɛbɛyɛ bɛ ayayadeɛ awia ne anadwo daa daa.
Bebuu awufoɔ atɛne
11 Afei mehuu ahennwa kɛseɛ fitaa bi ne nea ɔte ahennwa he so a asaase ne soro dwane firii n’anim, na bɛanya baabiaa amma bɛ. 12 Na mehuu awufoɔ akɛseɛ ne nketewa sɛ begyinagyina ahennwa he anim. Bebiebiee nwoma bebree mu, na bebiee nwoma foforɔ bi a ɔyɛ nkwa nwoma he mu. Bebuu awufoɔ he atɛne, sɛnea bɛ nnwuma teɛ, ne sɛnea bɛatwerɛ ya nwoma ahoroɔ he mu he. 13 Afei po nso yii n’awufoɔ maeɛ. Wuo ne Asamando nso yii bɛ awufoɔ maeɛ. Na bebuu bɛ nyinaa atɛne sɛnea bɛ nnwuma teɛ. 14 Na bɛtoo Wuo ne Asamando guu gya tadeɛ he mu. Saa gya tadeɛ yi ne wuprenu he. 15 Na biaa a bɛantwerɛ ne dini nkwa nwoma he mu he, bɛtoo no twenee gya tadeɛ he mu.

english_text:
The New Heaven and the New Earth
1 I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea. 2 Then I saw New Jerusalem, the holy city, coming down from God in heaven. It was like a bride dressed in her wedding gown and ready to meet her husband.
3 I heard a loud voice shout from the throne:
God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people. 4 He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
5Then the one sitting on the throne said:
I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted. 6 Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty. 7 All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
8But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death.
The New Jerusalem
9I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.” 10 Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
11The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone. 12 The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel. 13Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west. 14The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
15 The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls. 16The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about 2,400 kilometers high and 2,400 kilometers wide. 17Then the angel measured the wall, and by our measurements it was about 60 meters high.
18 The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal. 19Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald, 20the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst. 21Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
22I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple. 23 And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
24 Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there. 25 Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes. 26The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city. 27 But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_text:
Soro foforɔ ne asaase foforɔ
1 Afei mehuu soro foforɔ ne asaase foforɔ, na kane soro ne kane asaase he nni hɔ bio, na po nso nni hɔ bio. 2 Na mehuu Kuro Kronkron a ɔyɛ Yerusalem foforɔ sɛ osiane afiri soro afiri Nyankopɔn nkyɛne ɔɔba fam, a bɛasiesie ne ho sɛ ayeforɔ a bɛahyehyɛ no ama ne kunu. 3 Na metee nne dendene bi firi ahennwa he so sɛ:
“Hwɛ! Nyankopɔn ntomadane ya nnipa mu.
Ɔne bɛ bɛtena
na bɛayɛ ne mane.
Na Nyankopɔn he ankasa ne bɛ bɛtena
4 Na ɔbɛpepa bɛ aniwa mu nisuo nyinaa.
Na wuo nni hɔ bio;
na awerɛhoɔ ne su ne yeyaa biaa nni hɔ bio,
efiri sɛ, kane nnoɔma he atwam.”
5 Afei nea ɔte ahennwa he so he kaa sɛ, “Hwɛ! Meeyɔ nnoɔma nyinaa foforɔ!” Ɔka kyerɛɛ me nso sɛ, “Twerɛ yei, efiri sɛ, saa nsɛm yi yɛ nɔkorɛ, na wobetumi de wo ho ato so.” 6 Na ɔkɔɔ so kaa sɛ, “Bɛawie! Mene Ahyɛaseɛ ne Awieeɛ, kyerɛ sɛ, mfitiaseɛ ne awieeɛ he. Biaa a sukɔm de no he, mɛma no nsuo a ofiri nkwa asuti mu anom kwa. 7 Biaa a obedi nkunim he benya yei afiri me nkyɛne. Mɛyɛ ne Nyankopɔn na ɔno nso ayɛ me ba. 8 Nso ahufoɔ ne bɛ a bɛnnye nni ne akyiwadeyɔfoɔ ne awudifoɔ ne adwamanfoɔ ne adutofoɔ ne abosonsomfoɔ ne atorɔfoɔ nyinaa benya bɛ kyɛfa ya tadeɛ a gya ne sɔfe ɔɔdɛre mu he. Yei ne wuprenu he.”
Yerusalem foforɔ he
9 Abɔfoɔ nson a bekitakita nkuruwaa nson a haw nson a otwa toɔ he ahyɛ no ma he mu baako baa me nkyɛne bɛkaa sɛ, “Bra, na mɛkyerɛ wo Ayeforɔ a ɔyɛ Dwammaa he yerɛ he.” 10 Na ɔde me nam honhom mu kɔɔ bepɔ kɛseɛ tentene bi atifi. Ɔkyerɛɛ me Yerusalem, Kuro Kronkron a ofiri soro, Nyankopɔn hɔ ɔɔba fam, 11 a ɔɔhyerɛn Nyankopɔn animuonyam mu. Na kuro he hyerɛn sɛ aboɔdenboɔ, sɛ ahwehwɛboɔ a mu te sɛ ahwehwɛ. 12 Na ne fasuo he wɔ ahoɔdene na ɔware yie, na ɔwɔ pono dummienu gu ano a, abɔfoɔ dummienu na bɛhwɛ apono he so. Apono he so ne bɛatwerɛ Israel mmusuakuo dummienu he mu nnipa dini. 13Na apono mmeɛnsa ya apieɛ; mmeɛnsa ya anafoɔ; mmeɛnsa ya atifi na mmeɛnsa nso ya atɔeɛ. 14Besii kuro he afasuo sii aboɔ dummienu a bɛatwerɛ Dwammaa he asomafoɔ dummienu he dini ya so he so. 15 Bɔfoɔ a ɔkasa kyerɛɛ me he wɔ sikakɔkɔɔ poma susudua a bɛde besusu kuro he, n’apono ne ne fasuo. 16 Na kuro he yɛ ahinanan, ne tentene ne ne tɛtrɛtɛ yɛ pɛ. Bɔfoɔ he de ne susudua he susu kuro he. Na ne tentene yɛ nkwansini apem ahanum na ne tɛtrɛtɛ, ne tentene ne sorokɔ nyinaa yɛ pɛ. 17Bɔfoɔ he susuu afasuo he nso a na ne sorokɔ yɛ basafa ha aduannan nnan sɛnea na saa berɛ he nsusuiɛ a bɔfoɔ he nam so kaeɛ he teɛ. 18Ahwehwɛboɔ ne ɔde too afasuo he. Na kuro he deɛ, sikakɔkɔɔ mapa a ɔyɛ hyerɛnn te sɛ ahwehwɛ na bɛde toeɛ. 19 Bɛde aboɔdenboɔ ahodoɔ a ɔfɛ ne hyehyɛɛ kuro he fapem. Fapem a odi kane he yɛ ahwehwɛboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so mmienu he yɛ hoaboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so mmeɛnsa he yɛ mununkumboɔ. 20Fapem a ɔto so nnan he yɛ ahabamonoboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so num he yɛ tɔnboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so nsia he yɛ bogyanamboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so nson he yɛ sikabereboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so nwɔtwe he yɛ apopobibireboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so nkron he yɛ akrateboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so du he yɛ dɛnkyɛmmoɔ. Fapem a ɔto so dubaako he yɛ wusieboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so dummienu he yɛ beredumboɔ. 21 Na bɛde nhwene pa dummienu ne yɔɔ apono dummienu he. Apono he mu biaa, bɛde ahwene pa baako ne yɔeɛ. Na kuro he abɔntene kɛseɛ yɛ sika mapa, kronkron a ɔte sɛ ahwehwɛ. 22Na manhu asɔredane ya kuro he mu baabiaa, efiri sɛ, na Awurade Nyankopɔn, ade nyinaa so tumfoɔ, ne Adwammaa he ne yɛ n’asɔredane. 23 Na kuro he nhia awia anaa bosome hann, efiri sɛ, na Nyankopɔn animuonyam hyerɛn mu, na kuro he kanea ne Dwammaa he. 24 Ne hann he bɛhyerɛn asaase so amane nyinaa so, na asaase so amane sodifoɔ de bɛ animuonyam bɛba mu. 25 Bɛntoto kuro he apono mu koraa awia, efiri sɛ adeɛ nsa hɔ da. 26Bɛde amane he animuonyam ne anidie bɛba kuro he mu. 27 Nso bibiaa a ho nte nkɔ kuro he mu. Saa ara ne bi a ɔyɛ aniwudeɛ anaa odi atorɔ nkɔ mu. Bɛ a bɛatwerɛ bɛ dini ahyɛ Dwammaa he nkwa nwoma he mu he nkoaa na bɛbɛkɔ kuro he mu.

english_text:
1 The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated. 2 Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
3 God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God 4 and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people. 5 Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever.
The Coming of Christ
6Then I was told:
These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon. 7Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
8My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
9But the angel said,
Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
10Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
11 Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
12 Then I was told:
I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done. 13 I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
14 God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city. 15But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
16 I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
17 The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!”
Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!”
If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
18 Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book:
If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book. 19If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
20The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!”
So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
21I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
translation_text:
Nkwa asubɔntene ne nkwa dua hee
1 Afei bɔfoɔ he kyerɛɛ me nkwa asubɔntene a ani te krɔgyee sɛ ahwehwɛ a ofiri Nyankopɔn ne Dwammaa he ahennwa so 2 a, ɔtene fa kuro he abɔntene mfinimfini. Na nkwa dua a ɔsõ aba ahodoɔ dummienu sisi Asubɔntene he afanu nyinaa, na ɔsoso n’aba bosome biaa. Na dua he ahahane ne bɛde sa amane yadeɛ. 3 Bibiaa a ɔhyɛ Nyankopɔn nnome ase he nkɔ kuro he mu. Bebehu Nyankopɔn ne Dwammaa he ahennwa ya kuro he mu na ne nkoa bɛsom no. 4 Bebehu n’anim na bɛbɛtwerɛ ne dini agu bɛ moma so. 5 Adeɛ ɔnnsa hɔ bio na nkanea anaa awia ho nhia, efiri sɛ, Awurade Nyankopɔn bɛyɛ bɛ hann na bebedi ahene daa daa. 6 Afei bɔfoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Saa nsɛm yi yɛ nɔkorɛ a wode wo ho to so a, ɔyɛ. Na Awurade Nyankopɔn a ɔnam adiyifoɔ so kasaeɛ he somaa ne bɔfoɔ sɛ ɔmmɛkyerɛ ne nkoa adeɛ a ɔbɛba nnansa yiaa he. 7 Hwɛ! Ɔnkyɛre koraa mɛba.”
Nhyira nka bɛ a betie adiyisɛm a ɔya saa nwoma yi mu he!
8 Na me, Yohane, mene mehuu nnoɔma yi na meteeɛ. Na meteeɛ na mehuiɛ he, mehwee ase sɛ meekoto asɔre bɔfoɔ a ɔkyerɛɛ me nnoɔma yi. 9Na ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ: Nyɔ saa, meyɛ akoa te sɛ wo ne wo nuanom adiyifoɔ, ne bɛ a bedi nwoma yi mu nsɛm mu he soaa pɛ. Nyankopɔn nkoaa ne koto no sɔre no! 10 Na ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Nsɔ nwoma yi mu adiyisɛm he ano, efiri sɛ berɛ he abɛne sɛ nea bɛtwerɛ ya mu nyinaa ba mu. 11Ee! Deɛ ɔyɔ bɔne he nkɔso nyɔ bɔne ara, na nea ɔyɔ fi he nyɔ fi ara. Na deɛ ɔtene he nkɔso ntene ara, na deɛ ɔyɛ kronkron he nkɔso nyɛ kronkron ara.” 12 “Hwɛ! Ɔnkyɛre koraa mɛba! Na mekita m’akatua sɛ mede bra biaa sɛnea n’adwuma teɛ. 13 Mene Alpha ne Omega, kannifoɔ ne kyidifoɔ, mfitiaseɛ ne awieeɛ he.” 14 Nhyira nka bɛ a bɛahoro bɛ ntaadeɛ na afiri, na bɛ a bɛwɔ ho kwane sɛ bedi nkwa dua he aba na bɛfa kuro he pono he mu. 15 Na kuro he akyi ne nkramane ne adutofoɔ ne adwamanfoɔ ne awudifoɔ ne abosonsomfoɔ ne bɛ a bɛpɛ torɔ na bedi he nyinaa ya.
16 “Me, Yesu, ne masoma me bɔfoɔ sɛ ɔmmɛbɔ ho saa nwoma yi nyinaa ho amanneɛ ya asafo mu. Mene Dawid nhini ne n’aseni, mene anɔpa nsoromma a ɔhyerɛn he.”
17 Honhom he ne ayeforɔ he dɛ, “Bra!”
Na biaa a ɔte saa asɛm yi nso ka sɛ, “Bra!”
Na deɛ sukɔm de no he mmra;
na nea ɔpɛ he nso mmɛgye nkwa nsuo he kwa.
Awieeɛ
18 Me, Yohane, mebɔ biaa a ɔte saa adiyisɛm a ɔya nwoma yi mu kɔkɔ sɛ: Sɛ bi de bi ka ho a, Nyankopɔn de asotwe bɛka haw a bɛatwerɛ no saa nwoma yi mu he ho de ama no. 19 Na sɛ bi nso yi bi firi adiyisɛm a ɔya saa nwoma yi mu he mu a, Nyankopɔn beyi ne kyɛfa a obenya afiri nkwa dua he aba he ne kuro Kronkron a bɛakyerɛkyerɛ mu nwoma yi mu he. 20Nea odi nwoma yi ho adanseɛ he ka sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ! Ɔnkyɛre koraa mɛba!” Ɔmmra mu saa. Awurade Yesu, bra!
21Yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo adom nka ho nyinaa. Amen.
